# Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

If you can dream it, you can build it
=====================================

Our developer platform puts the power in your hands to create the tools teams need to improve efficiency, expand functionality, and bring ideas to life. Take a look at our APIs, documentation, guides, and references to get started.

![Abstract illustration that represents a Figma Plugin](https://static.figma.com/uploads/2b6cb3fa0c8256e958e2954ae37f8bfb9475a4af)

### Plugin APIs

Automate tasks, add new functionality, and improve your workflow with plugins for everything from documentation to communication.

[View plugin docs](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/)

![Abstract illustration that represents a widget](https://static.figma.com/uploads/edf502651aa643f0a5631b45cee6d384271aeab0)

### Widget APIs

Encourage team collaboration and run meetings more efficiently in FigJam with interactive games, polls, trackers, and more.

[View widget docs](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/)

Bring Figma to you
------------------

Leverage the power of our REST API to bring Figma into your external tools and products. Use our Embed kit to share or embed real-time Figma designs and prototypes to whoever, whenever you need to.

[Start building](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/)

Automate account provisioning
-----------------------------

Simplify onboarding and offboarding with role-setting via our SCIM API. Any changes to members’ roles within your identity provider are automatically pushed to Figma—saving your team time and energy.

[Get started](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/360048514653-Set-up-automatic-provisioning-via-SCIM)

“Figma’s API has helped bring ideas we have dreamt about for ages to life.”
===========================================================================

Erik Klimczak, Principal Designer at Uber

Share your builds with millions
-------------------------------

### Show us what you got

Publish and share what you build with the millions of users in our Figma community.

[View Figma community](https://www.figma.com/community)

### Partner with Figma

Give your work more exposure when you feature your integration in our collection.

[View integrations](https://www.figma.com/product-integrations)

### Be a team player

Create private plugins and widgets to keep internal information and data safe.

[Start building](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/4404228629655-Create-private-organization-plugins)

Join the club
-------------

Learn from others, share your work, and extend your tool set with a diverse group of developers from around the world.

*   [Browse plugin samples on GitHub](https://github.com/figma/plugin-samples)
    
*   [Browse widget samples on GitHub](https://github.com/figma/widget-samples)
    
*   [View video tutorials on YouTube](https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLXDU_eVOJTx5YBAszyuOTyxlgIxkQVyii)
    
*   [Join the conversation on Discord](https://discord.gg/xzQhe2Vcvx)
    
*   [Share Plugin API feedback](https://forum.figma.com/c/plugin-api/20)
    
*   [Share REST API feedback](https://forum.figma.com/c/rest-api/11)
    
*   [Share Widget API feedback](https://forum.figma.com/c/plugin-widget-api/20)
    
*   [See what users are requesting](https://forum.figma.com/c/figjam/resource-ideas/59)

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

*   Added grid gap types to [VariableBindableNodeField](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableBindableNodeField/)
    
*   Added grid types to [AnnotationProperty](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/AnnotationProperty/)
    

*   Added the `visible` property to the [Noise](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#noiseeffect)
     and [Texture](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#textureeffect)
     effect types.
*   Added the `boundVariables` property to [Noise](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#noiseeffect)
     and [Texture](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#textureeffect)
     effect types to prevent validation errors on write. Note that binding variables for Noise and Texture effects is not yet supported.
*   Added a new [Glass](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#glasseffect)
     effect type in beta. Note that the effect is only supported on Frames at this time, and binding variables is not yet supported.
*   Fixed [`GridTrackSize`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/GridTrackSize/)
     to properly handle `value` as optional.

Added documentation for figma.ui.getPosition().

Added support for Grid layout properties on grid containers and children.

Grid Containers[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/#grid-containers "Direct link to Grid Containers")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`gridRowCount`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridrowcount/)
    
*   [`gridColumnCount`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridcolumncount/)
    
*   [`gridRowGap`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridrowgap/)
    
*   [`gridColumnGap`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridcolumngap/)
    
*   [`gridRowSizes`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridrowsizes/)
    
*   [`gridColumnSizes`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridcolumnsizes/)
    
*   [`appendChildAt`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-appendchildat/)
    

Grid Children[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/#grid-children "Direct link to Grid Children")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`gridRowSpan`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridrowspan/)
    
*   [`gridColumnSpan`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridcolumnspan/)
    
*   [`gridRowAnchorIndex`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridrowanchorindex/)
    
*   [`gridColumnAnchorIndex`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridcolumnanchorindex/)
    
*   [`gridChildHorizontalAlign`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridchildhorizontalalign/)
    
*   [`gridChildVerticalAlign`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-gridchildverticalalign/)
    
*   [`setGridChildPosition`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-setgridchildposition/)
    

*   Added missing stroke cap types to [StrokeCap](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StrokeCap/)
     including `"DIAMOND_FILLED"`, `"TRIANGLE_FILLED"`, and `"CIRCLE_FILLED"`
*   Added the `color` property to the [Noise](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#noiseeffect)
     effect type

**New**:

*   Type definitions now ship with docstrings, so you can view Plugin API documentation alongside your code in IDEs such as VSCode.

**Bug fixes**:

*   Fix the type definition for [InteractiveSlideElementNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InteractiveSlideElementNode/)
     to include a `clone()` method.
*   Fix the type definition for [PatternPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#patternpaint)
     to include `visible`, `opacity` and `blendMode` properties.

Bug fixes:

*   The type definition for [CodegenPreferences.unit](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-codegen/#preferences)
     now uses uppercase string values instead of lowercase string values

We're adding the following new node types and properties in beta:

*   [Text path](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextPathNode/)
     node type
*   [Transform group](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TransformGroupNode/)
     node type
*   [Texture](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#textureeffect)
     and [Noise](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#noiseeffect)
     effect types
*   [Progressive](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/#blureffectprogressive)
     blur effect type
*   [Pattern](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#patternpaint)
     paint type

The node types and properties in beta can be used with Figma Design and Figma Draw.

Some exciting news for codegen plugins! Thanks to your feedback, we've extended the [`figma.codegen.on` callback](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-codegen-on/)
 timeout from 3s to 15s.

For more details, see:

*   [Codegen plugins](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/codegen-plugins/)
    
*   [`figma.codegen.on` callback](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-codegen-on/)
    

**New**:

We've updated our Plugin API to support [**Annotation Categories**](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/AnnotationCategory/)
, enabling developers to create organized and structured annotations with their plugins. Plugin API users can now:

*   Initialize and manage Annotation Categories at the file level
*   Create Categories with custom labels and colors.
*   Read existing Annotation Categories defined for a file
*   Assign a new Category to an Annotation for better organization
*   Remove, add, and edit Categories as needed

This update builds upon our previous [annotations](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Annotation/)
 support, giving you more control over how annotations are categorized and displayed in your plugins.

For more information, see:

*   [Annotation Categories](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/AnnotationCategory/)
    
*   [Annotation Category Colors](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/AnnotationCategoryColor/)
    
*   [`figma.annotations`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-annotations/)
    

We're making some changes to plugin data storage.

New:

*   The total [client storage](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-clientStorage/)
     limit is now 5 MB, up from 1 MB previously
*   [`setSharedPluginData`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-setplugindata/)
     and [`setSharedPluginData`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-setsharedplugindata/)
     have new per-entry 100 kB limits

Bug fixes:

*   Fix the return type for [node.getRangeBoundVariable()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#getrangeboundvariable)
    .

# Compare the Figma APIs | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/compare-apis/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Compare the Figma APIs
======================

Learn how Figma's APIs can support the use cases of your work and organization! Figma provides the [Plugin API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/)
, [Widget API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/)
, and [REST API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/)
, powerful developer interfaces that let you extend how Figma can be used by you, your team, and the entire Figma community. On this page, we dive into the differences between our APIs.

API overview
------------

The **Plugin API** enables you to create interactive experiences that extend what Figma can do. For example, you can use plugins to:

*   Generate content or ideas with AI
*   Bring outside content like stock photos into Figma
*   Insert context from other tools, like your project tracker, into Figma

The **Widget API** enables you to create custom interactive on-canvas nodes with rich user interfaces that can be applied to the Figma or FigJam canvas. For example, you can use widgets to:

*   Show an alignment scale where all the viewers of a file can vote on how aligned they feel
*   Stamp the canvas with emojis or custom stickers
*   Show a snippet of information, like the details of a ticket or work item, from a project tracker

The **REST API** enables you to access your Figma files from your own script, tool, or app. You can use the REST API to:

*   Automate syncing your Figma libraries to and from your codebase
*   Write a custom automation script or app
*   Bring the best of Figma into your own apps and experiences as one of our [integration partners](https://www.figma.com/product-integrations/)
    

API comparison
--------------

This section breaks down Figma's APIs by interaction model, use case, and more. Use these comparisons to determine the right API for what you're trying to build.

### Interaction models

#### Plugin API

**In Figma and FigJam:** A user has a particular Figma design or FigJam file open; the plugin is reading or writing to that file as they work. The plugin might also be sending details to other apps, like updating a ticket in Jira.

#### Widget API

**In Figma and FigJam:** A user has a particular Figma design or FigJam file open; the widget is added to the canvas of that file. The widget persists on the canvas and is visible to anyone viewing the file. The widget can update when any of those viewers interact with it; it can also be manually refreshed with updated details from another app or service.

#### REST API

**In any automated system:** A user sets up a pre-programmed routine or script that runs at a later time: either manually, on a set schedule (like a job that runs every night), or triggered by an event (like a build that runs whenever code files are changed). **Note:** Figma does not need to be open and a user does not need to be present.

**In [integrated apps](https://www.figma.com/product-integrations/)
:** A user has any an integrated app (e.g. Slack / Storybook / Notion / Maze / etc.) open and is either (a) looking at a live embed of a Figma design or FigJam file or (b) pulling details from a Figma design or FigJam file into the app for further processing, like creating a prototype from designs or pulling design details into a live design system.

**In Figma, via a plugin or widget:** A user running a plugin or widget (using the Plugin and Widget APIs) can also call the REST API to access contents of files other than the file where the plugin or widget is running.

### Example scenarios

#### Plugin API

**Bring content into Figma:** Interactively browse content from another source (like [Unsplash](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/738454987945972471/Unsplash)
 or [LottieFiles](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/809860933081065308/LottieFiles)
) and easily add it to your Figma design

**Create shared workflows that involve Figma and other tools:** Minimize context switching by bringing key context and actions (like viewing or creating a [Jira](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1220802563996996107/Jira)
 ticket) into the Figma experience where you're already working

**Bring designs to code:** Transform Figma designs into working code in real time, like [Anima](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/857346721138427857/Anima---Figma-to-React-and-HTML)
 or [LottieFiles](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/809860933081065308/LottieFiles)

#### Widget API

**Enable real-time interactivity:** Drive alignment with voting, have fun with games, and more - right on the canvas where everyone can see

**Bring context from other tools onto the canvas:** Enable viewers of a Figma design or FigJam file to see relevant context from another app (like a row from a Coda or Notion table) directly on the canvas

**Add notes and annotations:** Add markup or context to a Figma design or FigJam file in exactly the format and UI that viewers will expect

**Start with rich, interactive templates:** Start off common projects like creating a table, building a timeline, or assembling a project board with smart templates that can react to the user's changes

#### REST API

**Build an integration:** [Integration partners](https://www.figma.com/product-integrations/)
 or Enterprise customers can leverage the REST API to build experiences enriched with Figma data and embeds in their own apps

**Sync Figma and your codebase:** Enterprises use the REST API to sync changes from their Figma design libraries to their codebase's design systems (and vice versa)

**Access details of other files from a plugin:** Plugins that want to compare the contents of the file in which they're running with other files, like linters, can access other files using the REST API

### Advantages

#### Plugin API

*   Allows real-time changes to the contents of a Figma design or FigJam file
*   Allows editing almost any aspect of a Figma design or FigJam file

#### Widget API

*   Allow you to create rich and interactive multiplayer experiences directly on the canvas

#### REST API

*   Allows other systems (e.g. apps like Notion, build systems like GitHub Actions, custom automations and scripts on a power user's local computer) to read the contents of one or many Figma design and/or FigJam files, even without having Figma open
*   Better for accessing a large quantity of nodes (design items) in a single file and for accessing nodes from many files
*   When using a user's Personal Access Token to call the API, can access all files that a user can access
*   When registered as an OAuth app, any user can give the app permission to access all of their files

### Limitations

#### Plugin API

*   Can only read and edit the current file that a user has open in Figma
*   Cannot work across multiple files unless the user manually triggers the plugin on each file or the plugin code also calls the REST API

#### Widget API

*   Can only read and edit the current file that a user has open in Figma
*   Cannot work across multiple files; each instance of a widget acts only on the file where it's inserted

#### REST API

*   Is largely read-only, with the exception of:
    *   [Comments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/#post-comments-endpoint)
         and [Comment Reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/#post-comment-reactions-endpoint)
        
    *   [Variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-endpoints/)
        
    *   [Dev Resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/)
        

Was this page helpful?

[Yes](https://form.asana.com/?k=Pv3MvbQgMnobDHw8sQwmqg&d=10497086658021)
[No](https://form.asana.com/?k=9iFTV52Hl7aeuJugKLYIzA&d=10497086658021)

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/12/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

New:

*   As per popular request, the default typings have moved have moved to NPM. See the [Typings](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/typings/)
     page for more information.
*   Added `getPublishStatusAsync` to components and styles.

Last update erroneously set the typings file to update 13, so this skips ahead to update 14.

New:

*   The [setRelaunchData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-setrelaunchdata/)
     API (plugin inspector) now supports multiple buttons.
*   The `setRelaunchData` API is now available on the [DocumentNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DocumentNode/)
    . Relaunch buttons set on this node will appear on every page when nothing is selected.

New:

*   New `selectedTextRange` API that allows you to get and set the [current text selection](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/PageNode-selectedtextrange/)
    .
*   New `insertCharacters` and `deleteCharacters` that make it easier to [edit text](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/TextNode-insertcharacters/)
     while preserving existing styles.
*   ICYM, new `setRelaunchData` API (plugin inspector) introduced at [Config](https://www.figma.com/blog/config-2020-new-feature-announcements/)
    .
*   `"STRETCH"` is now a valid value for `layoutAlign` in [children of frames](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-layoutalign/)
    .

Fixed:

*   The following properties have been removed on group nodes (they didn't do anything): `clipsContent`, `guides`, `layoutGrids`, `gridStyleId`.

New:

*   Access to the current [viewport bounds in canvas coordinates](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-viewport/)
    .
*   Read-only access to the [current file name](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DocumentNode/)
     via `figma.root.name`.
*   New helper methods `findChild` and `findChildren` to [search immediate children](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/)
    .
*   Access to the `constrainProportions` as a [node property](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/FrameNode/)
    .
*   Access to the `strokeMiterLimit` property and `outlineStroke()` method as [node properties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VectorNode/)
    .
*   Access to the `scaleFactor` as an [instance property](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/)
    .
*   Access to the `expanded` property on [nodes with children](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/FrameNode/)
    .
*   Added a definition `Rect` (x, y, width, height) to the typings.

Fixed:

*   Fixed how the `getRange`/`setRange` functions handle unicode characters. Previously, those function took a `start` and `end` parameter which were interpreted as unicode code points (UCS4). However, `node.characters` returns a JavaScript string which is encoded as UTF16. This created an inconsistency where calling `getRange`/`setRange` could end up referencing a different range than the one you would expect, if characters that encode differently were present in your strings (such as emojis). Most plugins should simply work better as a result of this change. If you had added workaround code in your plugin, you may now need to remove the workaround.

We also recently released a new version of the desktop app, which uses Electron 6 and Chrome 76. This gives you access to newer Chrome APIs that weren't previously available in the desktop app.

New:

*   Read-only access to prototyping properties is now available for all plugins

Fixed:

*   Fixed a bug where cloning a page did not clone the prototyping connections

New:

*   Auto-layout properties are now available! Please refer to the [`FrameNode` documentation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/FrameNode/)
    .

Fixed:

*   The `FrameNode` and `GroupNode` objects now have separate [prototypes](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/JavaScript/Objects/Object_prototypes)
    , which means that code such as `'strokes' in group` will correctly return false. Note that we still recommend checking the `.type` property of node objects over using the `in` operator. We expect this to be an implementation detail -- however, let us know if this is causing you any issues.

Proposed API ([how do they work?](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
):

*   Overlay properties have been added to the proposed prototyping APIs.

Figma has [released auto-layout](https://www.figma.com/release-notes/)
!

As always, we try to make sure plugins [don't break when we release new features](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/stability-updates/)
. That being said, this release affects a lot of the core functionality of Figma and you may need to update your plugin to be aware of those concepts.

### New layout constraints[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/12/#new-layout-constraints "Direct link to New layout constraints")

Auto-layout frames introduces **new types of relations between layers**. Existing APIs around size, positioning and children order have different behavior:

*   Children of auto-layout frames have a `x` and `y` position set automatically. Writing to the position of those nodes via the API will no-op. Similarly, setting `relativeTransform` may ignore the translation component (but retain the rotation component).
*   Calling `resize(w, h)` and `resizeWithoutConstraints(w, h)` will no-op in one or both dimensions if the auto-layout frame has a size set automatically by auto-layout.
*   Changing the size of a node will have the side effect of changing the size of its parent if the parent is an auto-layout frame.
*   Changing the order of children in an auto-layout frame will have the side effect of changing the position of its children.

The plugin API is intended to always return the most up to date value for all properties (i.e. has auto-layout applied). As such, be aware of these new restrictions and side effects when setting properties.

### New frame properties[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/12/#new-frame-properties "Direct link to New frame properties")

Frame-like containers (frames, components & instances) now support new properties! The properties listed below now exist on nodes of type `"FRAME"`, `"COMPONENT"` and `"INSTANCE"`. Note that they are **not** available on nodes of type `"GROUP"`.

Note that adding these new properties required re-organizing type definitions in the [typings file](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/typings/)
. Notably, we've split `FrameNode` into `FrameNode` and `GroupNode` because a lot of properties are not supported on group nodes.

*   Fill properties
    *   `fills`
    *   `fillStyleId`
*   Stroke properties
    *   `strokes`
    *   `strokeStyleId`
    *   `strokeWeight`
    *   `strokeAlign`
    *   `strokeCap`
    *   `strokeJoin`
    *   `dashPattern`
*   Corner properties
    *   `cornerRadius`
    *   `topLeftRadius`
    *   `topRightRadius`
    *   `bottomLeftRadius`
    *   `bottomRightRadius`
    *   `cornerSmoothing`

Tip: don't write code like `if ('fills' in node) { ... }` to check if a property exists on a node. This is not guaranteed to work, especially with group nodes. Compare against the node type instead. It also helps TypeScript infer properties about the node better.

    function isFrameLike(node: BaseNode): node is FrameNode | ComponentNode | InstanceNode {  return node.type === 'FRAME' || node.type === 'COMPONENT' || node.type === 'INSTANCE'}if (isFrameLike(node)) {  console.log(node.fills, node.strokes, node.cornerRadius)}

### Deprecation of `backgrounds` property[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/12/#deprecation-of-backgrounds-property "Direct link to deprecation-of-backgrounds-property")

On frame-like nodes, the `backgrounds` and `backgroundStyleId` properties are **marked as DEPRECATED**. Use the newly-introduced `fills` and `fillStyleId` properties instead. We made this change to be consistent with other types of nodes that also use the `fills` properties. We may remove the property entirely in a new version so you should not use it. In the meantime, `backgrounds` and `backgroundStyleId` will keep working as an alias for `fills` and `fillStyleId` (i.e. they read and write to the same value).

On group nodes, `backgrounds` and `backgroundStyleId` are **no longer supported**, both in the editor and the API. If you have existing frames with a background, you will notice that the backgrounds panel in the properties panel has been changed with a message prompting you to replace it with a fill. Consequently, in the API, writing to the `backgrounds` and `backgroundStyleId` properties of a group node will no-op.

On page nodes, `backgrounds` is not deprecated.

### Auto-layout properties[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/12/#auto-layout-properties "Direct link to Auto-layout properties")

Coming soon!

(Also included: update 6)

New:

*   We updated the [typings](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/typings/)
     and replaced instances of `| symbol` with `PluginAPI['mixed']` for all properties that can return the special `figma.mixed` value. This new syntax also fixes the problem where TypeScript's control flow analysis was not able to understand `if (node.prop !== figma.mixed)` and narrow down the type.
*   We added "Use Developer VM" option in the plugin developer menu that gives plugin code access to the browser's debugger during development. [Read more](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/debugging/#developer-vm)
    .
*   We fixed a bug where the `selectionchanged` event could sometimes fire multiple times when the selection changed.

Proposed API ([how do they work?](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
):

*   We decided not to go forward with the optional `position` option in `figma.showUI()`. Instead, plugins now always remember their last opened position by default.
*   Most prototyping properties are now available, read-only, under the proposed flag! [Read more about these new properties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
    . As always, let the Figma team know if these properties are working for you.

New:

*   The `figma.ui.on('message')`, `figma.on('selectionchange')` and `figma.on('currentpagechange')` APIs, which were previously [proposed APIs](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
     can now be used in published plugins.
*   A new `figma.on('close')` API for allowing you to run shutdown actions when the plugin closes.

Proposed API ([how do they work?](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
):

*   An optional `position` option in `figma.showUI()` with possible values `'default' | 'last' | 'auto'`

New:

*   The concept of a developer-only "proposed API". [Click here to learn more](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
    .
*   Selection change and page change event APIs (`figma.on('selection')`) as a proposed API.

Fixed:

*   Previously a stray vertex not belonging to any segment would throw an error when converting the network to a path.

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/11/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

New:

*   Added `title` parameter to options for [showUI](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-showui/)
    .
*   Added [use\_absolute\_bounds property](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ExportSettings/#useabsolutebounds)
     for [ExportSettings](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ExportSettings/)
    .

New:

*   Added [hyperlink](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#hyperlink)
     property for [TextNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/)
    . New methods [getRangeHyperlink](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#getrangehyperlink)
     and [setRangeHyperlink](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#setrangehyperlink)
     are also available for manipulating hyperlink targets.
*   Added [rotation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#rotation)
     property for [ImagePaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#imagepaint)
    .
*   Added `ON_KEY_DOWN` type to [Trigger](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Trigger/)
    .

New:

*   Added [documentationLinks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#documentationlinks)
     property for [ComponentNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/)
     and [ComponentSetNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/)
    .
*   Added [detachInstance](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#detachinstance)
     functionality for [InstanceNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/)
    . This function preserves the same behavior as the equivalent functionality in the Figma editor.

New:

*   Added `swapComponent` to swap an instance's main component to a different main component. This function preserves overrrides using the same heuristics as instance swap in the Figma editor UI.

New:

*   Added `commitUndo` to commit new undo states to undo history. This does not trigger an undo.
*   Added `triggerUndo` to trigger an undo action. This reverts to the last state in undo history.

New:

*   Added `moveLocalPaintStyleAfter`, `moveLocalTextStyleAfter`, `moveLocalEffectStyleAfter`, and `moveLocalGridStyleAfter` to reorder local styles within folders.
*   Added `moveLocalPaintFolderAfter`, `moveLocalTextFolderAfter`, `moveLocalEffectFolderAfter`, and `moveLocalGridFolderAfter` to reorder style folders that share the same parent folder.

New:

*   Support for the new autolayout APIs! They are available on [frame nodes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/FrameNode/)
    .
*   The `primaryAxisSizingMode`, `primaryAxisAlignItems`, `counterAxisAlignItems` properties have been added on frame-like nodes.
*   The `horizontalPadding` and `verticalPadding` properties are now deprecated. Use `paddingLeft`, `paddingRight`, `paddingTop` and `paddingBottom` instead.
*   The `layoutGrow` property has been added on all nodes (except documents & page nodes).
*   The `layoutAlign` has changed. It now supports the values `"STRETCH" | "INHERIT"`.

Fixed:

*   The `getPluginData` and `getSharedPluginData` APIs now properly inherit from components when accessed from an instance.
*   The `setRelaunchData` APIs is now able to override relaunch data on instances.

New:

*   Support for Variants! This update comes with a new node type: learn more about [component sets](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/)
    .
*   New functions [`combineAsVariants`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#combineasvariants)
     and [`importComponentSetByKeyAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#importcomponentsetbykeyasync)
     to support variants.

Fixed:

*   Fixed a bug where `instance.children` would not return any children when the instance was invisible
*   Fixed a bug where setting a local style to private (by prepending `_` to the style name) did not stop it from being published
*   Fixed a bug where accessing `textNode.textAutoResize` would crash
*   Fixed a bug where it was possible to give a node invalid coordinates by setting `relativeTransform` to some value where the two axes had zero dot product
*   Fixed a bug where components imported from the team library became writeable
*   Fixed a bug where `figma.getLocal*Styles()` would return an array containing `null` or invalid style nodes

New:

*   `enablePrivatePluginApi` option in [manifest.json](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#enableprivatepluginapi)
     to enable Private Plugin specific APIs
*   For private plugins with the above option set, [`figma.fileKey`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#filekey)
     will be set to the key of the current file.
*   The [`ui`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#ui)
     property in the plugin manifest can now either contain a filename in the form of a string. Or a list of filenames in the form of a map.

    // either"ui": "ui.html"// or"ui": {  "loginPage": "login.html",  "main": "ui.html",}

*   If you use the former, the file is available through the [`__html__`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/global-objects/#html)
     property. If you use the latter, then the files are available through a new [`__uiFiles__`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/global-objects/#uifiles)
     property (eg.`__uiFiles__.loginPage`, `__uiFiles__.main` etc)

Fixed:

*   [`InstanceNode.mainComponent`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#maincomponent)
     now returns null instead of raising an exception if the underlying component doesn't actually exist.
*   `setPluginData` and `setSharedPluginData` now removes the the relevant entries when the value is set to the empty string `""`, which is the default value returned by `getPluginData` and `getSharedPluginData`.

New:

*   The newly released easing curves are [now available in the API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Transition/)
    .
*   The existing `reactions` property returns multiple reactions to support this newly launched feature.
*   The `spread` property in [ShadowEffect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/)
     to match the newly released spread feature.
*   A `rescale` function to match to toolbar's [Scale Tool](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-rescale/)
    .
*   The `masterComponent` property has been renamed to `mainComponent`

Fixed:

*   The QuickJS runtime used to [sandbox plugin code](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/how-plugins-run/)
     has been upgraded to the latest version. Exceptions thrown will now contain a property stack trace (e.g. in the `(new Error()).stack`). Note that you could previously get stack traces using the [Developer VM](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/debugging/#developer-vm)
    , which also supports the `debugger` keyword.
*   The `setSharedPluginData` namespace argument now supports the characters `_`, `.` and uppercase characters.
*   Fixed an issue where `exportAsImage` API would sometimes would fail to render nodes correctly due to instances not being updated.
*   Fixed an issue where plugins could sometimes set a non-invertible affine transform to `relativeTransform`.
*   Fixed an issue where cloning a component via `.clone()` would not trigger library updates for publishing.
*   Fixed an issue where very long plugin names would stretch the plugin window.

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/10/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

More features added to plugin parameters (which itself is still in beta)!

*   It is now allowed to make all parameters optional
*   The [setSuggestions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-parameters/#setsuggestions)
     function now supports icons. Check out the updated [Svg Inserter](https://github.com/figma/plugin-samples/tree/master/svg-inserter)
     for an example
*   You can set a custom error message using [setError](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-parameters/#seterror)
    . This can also be used for validating user typed values and enforcing pre-conditions like selection or document contents. [Resizer](https://github.com/figma/plugin-samples/tree/master/resizer)
     contains an example for how to do this
*   You can also set custom loading messages [setLoadingMessage](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-parameters/#setloadingmessage)
     when accessing network requests
*   Lots of rendering improvements to the parameter UI

New:

*   Support for Multiple Flows! Added [flowStartingPoints](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#flowstartingpoints)
     property for [PageNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/)
    .

New:

*   Added [variantProperties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#variantproperties)
     property for [ComponentNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/)
     and [InstanceNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/)
     to get variant properties and values for a node.
*   Added [variantGroupProperties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#variantgroupproperties)
     property for [ComponentSetNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/)
     to get all variant properties and possible values that exist on a component set.
*   Added [setProperties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#setproperties)
     function for [InstanceNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/)
     to set the variant properties and values for an instance of a variant node.

*   Bugfix: callbacks take an `event` param again

/working-in-figjam/--- title: Version 1, Update 31[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/10/#working-in-figjam---title-version-1-update-31 "Direct link to /working-in-figjam/---
title: Version 1, Update 31")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Introducing [FigJam Plugins](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-figjam/)
 for public beta! We're introducing a number of APIs and events to enable plugin development in FigJam.

New:

*   `editorType` is now required in [manifests](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#editortype)
    . Specifying an editorType will control the APIs your plugin has access to in each editor, and whether your plugin shows up in Figma Design or FigJam (or both).
*   `figma.editorType`: returns either `figma` or `figjam` and enables you to have conditional logic in your plugin depending on the editor it’s run in.

FigJam Plugins only:

*   Timer support with [figma.timer](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-timer/)
     object
*   [StickyNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StickyNode/)
     type. Create a StickyNode with [createSticky](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createsticky/)
    
*   [ConnectorNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorNode/)
     type. Create a ConnectorNode with [createConnector](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createconnector/)
    
*   [ShapeWithTextNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ShapeWithTextNode/)
     type. Create a ShapeWithTextNode with [createShapeWithText](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createshapewithtext/)
    
*   [StampNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StampNode/)
     type.
*   UI addition: In FigJam relaunch buttons are shown in the in-line property menu of a node.
*   UI addition: We added all of the same plugin menus that you have in Figma (e.g. right-click menu, top-left menu, quick actions)

New:

*   Added [plugin parameters](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/plugin-parameters/)
     in public beta. This feature offers developers a way to receive user input through our in-editor search UI, quick actions, instead of building a custom UI.

Small tweak to the typings as part of [plugin parameters](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2021/08/17/version-1-update-29/)
. Made type for values of [`ParameterValues`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-parameters/#parametervalues)
 `any`.

New:

*   `reactions` node properties for prototyping now support writes (e.g. [`FrameNode` reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/FrameNode/#reactions)
     )
*   Updated [`Reaction`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Reaction/)
     type to allow null `Action` or `Trigger`. This change more accurately reflects what is possible through the UI and allows you to create reactions with partially null properties.

New:

*   Added `showShadowBehindNode` parameter to options for [Effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/)
    .

New:

*   Added `getRangeListOptions`, `setRangeListOptions`, `getRangeIndentation`, `setRangeIndentation` to TextNode to allow for creation of ordered/unordered lists and to adjust indentation levels

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/13/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This update only contains bug fixes.

*   Pressing ESC no longer closes the plugin automatically when a UI is shown. The plugin is now responsible for handling the ESC event now. This allows plugins to use ESC for editing operations inside the plugin UI.
*   Fix a bug where invalid styles or components could be sometimes be created accidentally by plugins.

This update only contains bug fixes.

*   Previously, assigning a `textStyleId` to a text node required you to load the existing font on the text node, even though it is being replaced. This is now no longer necessary.
*   `text.setRangeFontName` now requires you to load all existing fonts used in the text nodes, as they might still be present after the text node gets modified.
*   Fix a bug where `text.setRangeXYZ` functions would clear the `textStyleId` of characters outside the range that was being modified.

This is the first update to the plugins API since launch! This update does not contain breaking changes, so all plugins receive these updates automatically. Please refer to [Stability and Updates](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/stability-updates/)
 for more on our update and versioning policies.

*   Added `figma.union`, `figma.subtract`, `figma.intersect`, `figma.exclude` to replace `figma.createBooleanOperation()`.
*   Added `figma.notify` as an easy way to show messages to the user
*   Added `figma.getLocalPaintStyles`, `figma.getLocalGridStyles`, `figma.getLocalTextStyles`, `figma.getLocalEffectStyles` to enumerate local styles.
*   Fix a bug where the `"MAX"` layout grid type was not accepted when setting the value.

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/3/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

In February 2024, Figma [implemented dynamic page loading](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2024/02/21/version-1-update-87/#apis-for-dynamic-page-loading)
 for Figma design files. Now, dynamic page loading has been extended to FigJam files. If your plugin handles FigJam files, you should follow the steps in [Migrating Plugins to Dynamically Load Pages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/migrating-to-dynamic-loading/)
.

Bugfixes[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/3/#bugfixes "Direct link to Bugfixes")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The `boundVariables` property of the [ColorStop](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#colorstop)
     interface was erroneously annotated as non-nullable. A nullable annotation (`?`) has been added.

This change introduces `plugin-api-standalone.d.ts`, an experimental feature which allows for importing types explicitly.

Bug fixes

*   [Mouse Enter](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Trigger/#MOUSE_ENTER)
     and [Mouse Leave](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Trigger/#MOUSE_LEAVE)
     prototype events will now be created using the current functionality instead of the legacy functionality. Legacy Mouse Enter/Leave events would trigger every time the mouse moves inside (or outside) of a frame; current Mouse Enter/Leave events only trigger once when they cross the outer boundary of the frame. No migration needed for creating these events, the new behavior will always apply. Reading legacy events will return `deprecatedVersion: true` if present.

New

*   Added the `openInNewTab` property to the [URL](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/#URL)
     action type.

This version contains a partial revert of [update 1.98](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2024/08/15/version-1-update-98/)
.

In 1.98, we removed the nullable `?` modifier from the TypeScript type annotations for [`boundVariables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#bound-variables)
. `boundVariables` is indeed non-nullable when _reading_ objects from the Plugin API, but several of the affected types, such as [Paint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/)
 and [Effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/)
, are used for _writing_. In the write path, `boundVariables` is optional. Removing the nullable modifier caused typechecker errors in otherwise valid code. The nullable modifier has been added back in 1.99. Apologies to everyone who encountered difficulties, and apologies to anyone affected by the additional churn from this update!

Note that this version only affects TypeScript type definitions. No runtime behavior is affected. We intend to release a typings update in the future that makes `boundVariables` non-nullable in specific situations, but the second attempt will more carefully address the write path.

**Note**: The `boundVariables` bugfix in this version caused another typechecker bug. We strongly recommend skipping v1.98.

### Updates[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/3/#updates "Direct link to Updates")

*   [`StyledTextSegment`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StyledTextSegment/)
     objects now expose [text style overrides](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StyledTextSegment/#textstyleoverrides)
    .

### Bugfixes[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/3/#bugfixes "Direct link to Bugfixes")

*   The [`boundVariables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#bound-variables)
     property that appears on many node types has been updated so it is no longer nullable; the `?` annnotation was removed. If no bound variables are defined on a node, `boundVariables` will evaluate to an empty object.

New:

*   [CSS variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/css-variables/)
     docs no longer say that FigJam semantic color tokens are in beta.

New:

*   [DevStatus](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DevStatus/)
     can take on a new `COMPLETED` value, which indicates that dev work for the design is done
*   [FigJam](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-figjam/#pages)
     can now support multiple pages, and the documentation has been updated accordingly

### Updates[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/3/#updates "Direct link to Updates")

Fix plugin typings bug for prototype expressions. The `ExpressionFunction` enum has been updated to include a previously missing value: `NOT`.

### Updates[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/3/#updates "Direct link to Updates")

Fix plugin typings bug for typography variables. Instances of `keyof Omit<VariableBindableTextField, '...'>` have been updated to `Exclude<VariableBindableTextField, '...'>`, which correctly excludes values from the `VariableBindableTextField` enum.

Was this page helpful?

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/2/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

We've added a timeout to the `figma.codegen.on` callback. Now, if code generation takes longer than 3 seconds, it will automatically stop and an error message will be displayed in the codegen panel.

For more information, see:

*   [Codegen plugins](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/codegen-plugins/)
    
*   [`figma.codegen.on` callback](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/codegen-plugins/)
    

To help developers enhance decks and presentations, Figma Slides now supports plugins! You can use the new API features to effectively enable users to create beautiful, functional slide decks.

New:

*   Figma's Plugin API typings now support Figma Slides.
*   Figma Slides has four new node types: [SLIDE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-slides/#slide)
    , [SLIDE\_ROW](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-slides/#slide_row)
    , [SLIDE\_GRID](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-slides/#slide_grid)
    , and [INTERACTIVE\_SLIDE\_ELEMENT](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-slides/#interactive_slide_element)
    .
*   `figma.editorType` can now return `slides` when a plugin is running in Figma Slides.
*   `figma.viewport.slidesMode` can be used to toggle between `grid` view or `single-slide` view.
*   `figma.currentPage.focusedSlide` identifies the slide currently focused on in the interface.
*   `figma.getSlideGrid()` returns the slide grid, a 2D representation of slides within a deck.
*   `figma.setSlideGrid()` is used to reorder slides in the grid.
*   `getSlideTransition()` gets the current transition for a given `SLIDE` node.
*   `setSlideTransition()` is used to set the transition for a given `SLIDE` node.

For more information, see the [Working in Slides](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-slides/)
 guide.

Figma now supports proportional resizing via the `Lock aspect ratio` option. This replaces the legacy `constrainProportions` option. `constrainProportions` maintains backwards compatibility by setting the new [`targetAspectRatio`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-targetaspectratio/)
 field, but we encourage you to use the new property.

Previously, [createPage](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createpage/)
 could incorrectly create more pages than supported by Figma's Starter plan. This issue has been fixed.

**New**:

Figma now supports customizing underlines. The following changes were made to support getting and setting new underline features:

*   [textDecorationStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationstyle)
    , [textDecorationOffset](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationoffset)
    , [textDecorationThickness](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationthickness)
    , [textDecorationColor](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationcolor)
    , and [textDecorationSkipInk](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationskipink)
     are now supported on [TextNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/)
    
*   Added support for [textDecorationStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationstyle)
    , [textDecorationOffset](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationoffset)
    , [textDecorationThickness](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationthickness)
    , [textDecorationColor](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationcolor)
    , and [textDecorationSkipInk](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textdecorationskipink)
     in [getStyledTextSegments()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/TextNode-getstyledtextsegments/)
    
*   Added ranged getters [getRangeTextDecorationStyle()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangetextdecorationstyle)
    , [getRangeTextDecorationOffset()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangetextdecorationoffset)
    , [getRangeTextDecorationThickness()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangetextdecorationthickness)
    , [getRangeTextDecorationColor()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangetextdecorationcolor)
    , and [getRangeTextDecorationSkipInk()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangetextdecorationskipink)
    .
*   Added ranged setters [setRangeTextDecorationStyle()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangetextdecorationstyle)
    , [setRangeTextDecorationOffset()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangetextdecorationoffset)
    , [setRangeTextDecorationThickness()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangetextdecorationthickness)
    , [setRangeTextDecorationColor()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangetextdecorationcolor)
    , and [setRangeTextDecorationSkipInk()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangetextdecorationskipink)
    .

**New**:

Figma now supports per-paragraph fields. The following changes were made to support getting and setting indent and spacing per paragraph:

*   Added support for [paragraphSpacing](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#paragraphspacing)
    , [paragraphIndent](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#paragraphindent)
    , and [listSpacing](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#listspacing)
     in [getStyledTextSegments()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/TextNode-getstyledtextsegments/)
    
*   Added ranged getters [getRangeParagraphSpacing()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangeparagraphspacing)
    , [getRangeParagraphIndent()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangeparagraphindent)
    , and [getRangeListSpacing()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangelistspacing)
    .
*   Added ranged setters [setRangeParagraphSpacing()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangeparagraphspacing)
    , [setRangeParagraphIndent()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangeparagraphindent)
    , and [setRangeListSpacing()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangelistspacing)
    .
*   [paragraphSpacing](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#paragraphspacing)
     and [paragraphIndent](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#paragraphindent)
     are now supported on [boundVariables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#boundvariables)
    

**New**:

We’ve made updates to our Plugin and REST APIs so you can build more powerful plugins that increase efficiency and create more dynamic user experiences.

*   **Annotations and measurements**: Create and edit annotations and measurements from Dev Mode through the Plugin API. Generate bulk annotations, format annotations according to a style or template, add context or links from other tools to annotations, and more. Learn more about [annotations](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Annotation/)
     and [measurements](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Measurement/)
    .
    
*   **Rich text formatting for component description**: Build plugins that can read and edit the full rich-text version of the component description, giving users the same level of detail as Figma's UI. [Learn more](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-rich-text/)
    
*   **Advanced prototyping interactions**: Access all prototyping reactions through the Plugin and REST APIs to bring Figma prototypes to life in other interaction tools. [Learn more](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Reaction/)
    

**New**:

The Plugin API now contains support for page dividers:

*   Create a page divider node appended to the document node's list of children using [createPageDivider](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createpagedivider/)
    .
*   Change the [name](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-name/)
     of an empty [PageNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/)
     to a page divider name to turn the node into a page divider.
*   Read a page node's [isPageDivider](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#ispagedivider/)
     property to determine whether the node is a page divider.

This version of the plugin-typings package was skipped.

New

*   Component properties ([componentPropertyDefinitions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentPropertyDefinitions/)
    , [componentProperties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentProperties/)
    ) can now have bound variables for their value!

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

### New[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#new "Direct link to New")

[VariableScopes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableScope/)
 now have support for typography variables:

*   `STRING` variable scopes: `FONT_FAMILY`, `FONT_STYLE`, `TEXT_CONTENT`
*   `FLOAT` variable scopes: `FONT_SIZE`, `LINE_HEIGHT`, `LETTER_SPACING`, `PARAGRAPH_SPACING`, `PARAGRAPH_INDENT`

### New[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#new "Direct link to New")

*   Prototyping reactions now support [actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/)
     of type ['SET\_VARIABLE\_MODE'](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/#set_variable_mode-action)
    , which sets the page-level mode for the specified collection.

### New[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#new "Direct link to New")

*   [Paint.ColorStop](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#colorstop)
     now has a new field `boundVariables` that contains a `color` field and denotes whether a variable is bound to a gradient color stop.

### Updates:[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#updates "Direct link to Updates:")

*   Fix plugin typings bug where `figma.constants.colors` does not exist. This object, which remains exactly the same in the API, defines color palettes. We currently support the official FigJam colors, which can be found on this [Figma Learn](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500004291341-Apply-colors-in-FigJam)
     page.
    *   Added type `ColorPalettes` to hold all the available `ColorPalette` objects

With the launch of typography variables, the plugin API now supports getting and setting bound variables for text properties like font family, font style, font weight, and more. Please refer to the [Working with Variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#typography-variables)
 guide for how to use the variables API to control typography in Figma files.

**New:**

*   [`node.getRangeBoundVariable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getrangeboundvariable)
     and [`node.setRangeBoundVariable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangeboundvariable)
     for getting and setting bound variables on text ranges.

**Updated:**

*   [`node.boundVariables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#boundvariables)
     now includes bindable text fields.
*   [`node.setBoundVariable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setboundvariable)
     can bind variables to text fields for the full text range in a text node.
*   The above changes also apply to [`TextStyle`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextStyle/)
    .
*   Styled text segments from [`node.getStyledTextSegments`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/TextNode-getstyledtextsegments/)
     now include bound variables for text fields to let you easily identify bindings across segments of text in a text node.

### New[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#new "Direct link to New")

*   Private beta only: [Annotation.properties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Annotation/)
     can now contain a new value - `fontStyle`.

*   Adds a missing `openTypeFeatures` entry to the list of properties that may be reported in documentchange or nodechange events. [See NodeChangeProperty](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/NodeChangeProperty/)
    .
*   Fix invalid manifest examples for `networkAccess` in the plugin and widget docs.

### New[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#new "Direct link to New")

*   [DocumentNode.findWidgetNodesByWidgetId](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/DocumentNode-findwidgetnodesbywidgetid/)
     was previously undocumented, and has since been added to the documentation and type definitions.

APIs for dynamic page loading[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#apis-for-dynamic-page-loading "Direct link to APIs for dynamic page loading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Over the past few months, Figma has been rolling out dynamic page loading, which allows documents to load pages on demand, rather than all at once. For backward compatibility, Figma loads all pages in the document before attempting to run an extension (a plugin or widget), which can sometimes result in a noticeable delay.

We're pleased to announce a new set of APIs that allow extensions to work safely with dynamic page loading, and avoid triggering the loading delay. Alongside these new APIs, we are deprecating several existing methods and properties. This will help to ensure that new extensions will be compatible with dynamic page loading by default.

To mark an extension as compatible with dynamic page loading, add `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"` to `manifest.json`. Extensions with this manifest field will not encounter loading delays, but they'll be unable to access any of the deprecated methods and properties listed below. New extensions will include this manifest field by default.

To learn more about updating your extension, see our [comprehensive migration guide](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/migrating-to-dynamic-loading/)
. For TypeScript users, we've also provided an [ESLint plugin](https://github.com/figma/eslint-plugin-figma-plugins)
 that can help identify and automatically fix callsites that need to be updated.

### Timeline[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#timeline "Direct link to Timeline")

As of February 21st, dynamic page loading APIs are generally available (GA). You can update **existing extensions** and create **new extensions** using the dynamic page loading APIs and the manifest field `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"`.

Starting in April, the manifest field `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"` will be required for **all widgets**, and **all new plugins**.

### Net-new methods[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#net-new-methods "Direct link to Net-new methods")

*   [figma.loadAllPagesAsync()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#loadallpagesasync)
    
*   [figma.variables.createVariableAliasByIdAsync()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#createvariablealiasbyidasync)
    
*   [PageNode.loadAsync()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#loadasync)
    

### Deprecations and replacements[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#deprecations-and-replacements "Direct link to Deprecations and replacements")

Several methods and properties have been deprecated in favor of `async` replacements:

*   Use [`figma.getFileThumbnailNodeAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getfilethumbnailnodeasync)
     instead of [`figma.getFileThumbnailNode()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getfilethumbnailnode)
    .
*   Use [`figma.getLocalEffectStylesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocaleffectstylesasync)
     instead of [`figma.getLocalEffectStyles()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocaleffectstyles)
    .
*   Use [`figma.getLocalGridStylesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocalgridstylesasync)
     instead of [`figma.getLocalGridStyles()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocalgridstyles)
    .
*   Use [`figma.getLocalPaintStylesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocalpaintstylesasync)
     instead of [`figma.getLocalPaintStyles()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocalpaintstyles)
    .
*   Use [`figma.getLocalTextStylesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocaltextstylesasync)
     instead of [`figma.getLocalTextStyles()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getlocaltextstyles)
    .
*   Use [`figma.getNodeByIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getnodebyidasync)
     instead of [`figma.getNodeById()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getnodebyid)
    .
*   Use [`figma.getStyleByIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getstylebyidasync)
     instead of [`figma.getStyleById()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getstylebyid)
    .
*   Use [`figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getlocalvariablecollectionsasync)
     instead of [`figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollections()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getlocalvariablecollections)
    .
*   Use [`figma.variables.getLocalVariablesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getlocalvariablesasync)
     instead of [`figma.variables.getLocalVariables()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getlocalvariables)
    .
*   Use [`figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getvariablebyidasync)
     instead of [`figma.variables.getVariableById()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getvariablebyid)
    .
*   Use [`figma.variables.getVariableCollectionByIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getvariablecollectionbyidasync)
     instead of [`figma.variables.getVariableCollectionById()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getvariablecollectionbyid)
    .
*   Use [`setRangeFillStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangefillstyleidasync)
     instead of [`setRangeFillStyleId()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangefillstyleid)
    .
*   Use [`setRangeTextStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangetextstyleidasync)
     instead of [`setRangeTextStyleId()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setrangetextstyleid)
    .

In some specific cases, reading node properties has been deprecated in favor of an `async` getter:

*   Use [`ComponentNode.getInstancesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#getinstancesasync)
     instead of [`ComponentNode.instances`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#instances)
    .
*   Use [`getStyleConsumersAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/BaseStyle/#getstyleconsumersasync)
     instead of the [`consumers`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/BaseStyle/#consumers)
    .
*   Use [`InstanceNode.getMainComponentAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#getmaincomponentasync)
     instead of [`InstanceNode.mainComponent`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#maincomponent)
    .

In some specific cases, assigning values directly to node properties has been deprecated in favor of an `async` setter:

*   Use [`figma.setCurrentPageAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#setcurrentpageasync)
     instead of assigning to [`figma.currentPage`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#currentpage)
    .
*   Use [`setEffectStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#seteffectstyleidasync)
     instead of assigning to [`effectStyleId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#effectstyleid)
    .
*   Use [`setFillStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setfillstyleidasync)
     instead of assigning to [`backgroundStyleId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#backgroundstyleid)
    .
*   Use [`setFillStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setfillstyleidasync)
     instead of assigning to [`fillStyleId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#fillstyleid)
    .
*   Use [`setGridStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setgridstyleidasync)
     instead of assigning to [`gridStyleId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#gridstyleid)
    .
*   Use [`setReactionsAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setreactionsasync)
     instead of assigning to [`reactions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#reactions)
    .
*   Use [`setStrokeStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setstrokestyleidasync)
     instead of assigning to [`strokeStyleId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokestyleid)
    .
*   Use [`setVectorNetworkAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setvectornetworkasync)
     instead of assigning to [`vectorNetwork`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#vectornetwork)
    .
*   Use [`TextNode.setTextStyleIdAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#settextstyleidasync)
     instead of assigning to [`TextNode.textStyleId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#textstyleid)
    .

If an extension's manifest contains `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"`, accessing any of the deprecated items listed above will throw an exception.

### Method/property usage changes[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#methodproperty-usage-changes "Direct link to Method/property usage changes")

If an extension's manifest contains `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"`, calling any of the following methods will throw an exception unless you first call [figma.loadAllPagesAsync()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#loadallpagesasync)
:

*   [`DocumentNode.findAll()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DocumentNode/#findall)
    
*   [`DocumentNode.findAllWithCriteria()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DocumentNode/#findallwithcriteria)
    
*   [`DocumentNode.findOne()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DocumentNode/#findone)
    
*   [`DocumentNode.findWidgetNodesByWidgetId()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DocumentNode/#findwidgetnodesbywidgetid)
    

For the methods listed below, passing a string ID is now deprecated in favor of passing objects that the IDs refer to. If an extension's manifest contains `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"`, passing an ID will throw an exception.

*   [`figma.variables.createVariable()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#createvariable)
     - Pass a [`VariableCollection`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableCollection/)
     object instead of a collection ID.
*   [`clearExplicitVariableModeForCollection()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#clearexplicitvariablemodeforcollection)
     (present on multiple node types) - Pass a [`VariableCollection`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableCollection/)
     object instead of a collection ID.
*   [`setExplicitVariableModeForCollection()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setexplicitvariablemodeforcollection)
     (present on multiple node types) - Pass a [`VariableCollection`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableCollection/)
     object instead of a collection ID.
*   [`setBoundVariable()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#setboundvariable)
     (present on multiple node types) - Pass a [`Variable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/)
     object instead of a variable ID.

If an extension's manifest contains `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"`, some properties and methods of [`PageNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/)
 will throw an exception unless you explicitly load the page first using [`PageNode.loadAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#loadasync)
. These include:

*   [`PageNode.appendChild()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#appendchild)
    
*   [`PageNode.exportAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#exportasync)
    
*   [`PageNode.findAll()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#findall)
    
*   [`PageNode.findAllWithCriteria()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#findallwithcriteria)
    
*   [`PageNode.findChild()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#findchild)
    
*   [`PageNode.findChildren()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#findchildren)
    
*   [`PageNode.findOne()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#findone)
    
*   [`PageNode.findWidgetNodesByWidgetId()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#findwidgetnodesbywidgetid)
    
*   [`PageNode.insertChild()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#insertchild)
    

### Changes to events[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/4/#changes-to-events "Direct link to Changes to events")

*   If an extension's manifest contains `"documentAccess": "dynamic-page"`, the [`documentchange`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-on/#documentchange)
     event will not be available unless you first call [`figma.loadAllPagesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#loadallpagesasync)
    .
*   Where possible, prefer using the [`nodechange`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#on)
     and [`stylechange`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-on/#stylechange)
     events, which do not require triggering a full-document load.

**New**:

*   Add a [`figma.createComponentFromNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createcomponentfromnode/)
     function to create a component from an existing node, preserving all of its properties and children.

**New**:

*   Measurements can be added to pin distances between nodes in Dev Mode. See [Measurement](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Measurement/)
    .
*   Private beta only: annotations can be set on nodes to leave notes and pin properties in Dev Mode. See [Annotation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Annotation/)
    . More details on the public beta release of these APIs will be announced soon.
