# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/updates/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**New**:

*   Type definitions now ship with docstrings, so you can view Widget API documentation alongside your code in IDEs such as VSCode.

The type definitions for the [`BlendMode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-BlendMode/)
 enum now include a `pass-through` variant. Support for this variant is not new, but it was undocumented.

In February 2024, Figma [implemented dynamic page loading](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2024/02/21/version-1-update-87/#apis-for-dynamic-page-loading)
 for Figma design files. Now, dynamic page loading has been extended to FigJam files. If your widget handles FigJam files, you should follow the steps in [Migrating Plugins to Dynamically Load Pages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/migrating-to-dynamic-loading/)
. The same steps and considerations apply to widgets.

This change introduces `widget-api-standalone.d.ts`, an experimental feature which allows for importing types explicitly.

Version 1.9.1 was published to NPM, but does not contain any changes relative to 1.9.0.

*   Add a property [colorMapToOptions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-colormaptooptions/)
    , which takes in a [ColorPalette](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ColorPalette/)
     and returns a `WidgetPropertyMenuColorSelectorOption[]`. This allows widget developers to use predefined color palettes in widget color property menus
*   Add support for localhost in the manifest networkAccess allowedDomains field. See [manifest networkAccess docs](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-manifest/#networkaccess)
    .

This update exposes some new features introduced in AutoLayout v5.

*   All components that have a `height` and `width` property now allow you to specify maximum and minimum heights and widths.
*   The [AutoLayout](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/)
     component has a couple of property changes:
    *   The [spacing](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#spacing)
         property now accepts a [LayoutGap](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-LayoutGap/)
         object, which allows you to specify a horizontal or vertical gap.
    *   A new [wrap](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#wrap)
         property allows you to enable wrapping.
*   The [Text](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Text/)
     component has a new [truncate](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Text/#truncate)
     property for controlling text truncation behavior.

This update introduces the optional `networkAccess` key to the widget manifest.

Add the `networkAccess` to your widget's `manifest.json` file to limit the domains that your widget is permitted to access. When `networkAccess` is applied, if your widget makes a network request to a domain that is not specified in the list of permitted domains, that request is blocked.

For example:

    {  "name": "MyWidget",  "id": "737805260747778093",  "api": "1.0.0",  "widgetApi": "1.0.0",  "editorType": ["figma", "figjam"],  "containsWidget": true,  "main": "code.js",  "ui": "ui.html",  "networkAccess": {    "allowedDomains": ["https://my-app.cdn.com", "wss://socket.io", "*.example.com", "example.com/api/",  "exact-path.com/content"]  }}

When you publish your widget, the list of domains that you specify for `networkAccess` is displayed on your widget's Community page. This information is also visible for org admins when widgets are reviewed for approval.

To try this out:

1.  In the `manifest.json` file for your widget, add the following:
    
        "networkAccess": {  "allowedDomains": ["none"]}
    

`none` is a special keyword for `allowedDomains` that prevents any network access from your widget. 2. In Figma, create a new Figma or FigJam file and add your widget. Try to use the widget as normal. 3. Check the developer console. If your widget makes network requests, such as calls to an API or fetching images, Figma blocks the requests and throws [content-security policy (CSP)](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CSP)
 errors. 4. To fix the CSP errors, in your widget manifest, replace `["none"]` with the domains that your widget needs to access.

For more information about network access, see:

*   [How Widgets Run](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/how-widgets-run/)
    
*   [Making Network Requests](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/making-network-requests/)
    
*   [Widget Manifest](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-manifest/)
    

We updated the “New widget…” templates to use esbuild for bundling to make it easier for new widgets created to extend beyond a single source file.

To try this out:

1.  Log in to your account and open the Figma desktop app
2.  You can open any existing Figma / FigJam document or create a new one.
3.  Go to Menu > Widgets > Development > New widget...

*   You can now create widgets in Figma Design!
    *   Start by adding `figma` to the `editorType` array in your [`manifest.json`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-manifest/)
         file.
*   There are two new property menu options for [`usePropertyMenu`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usepropertymenu/)
    : `toggle` and `link`
    *   `toggle` lets you create a button in the property menu with an on and off state
    *   `link` will let you create a button that opens a link in a new tab
*   We've added a new [`Span`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Span/)
     component that lets you style inline ranges of text inside of a [`Text`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Text/)
     component.

Span example

    <Text  fill="#0F0"  fontSize={20}  fontFamily="Roboto"  fontWeight={400}  textCase="upper"  textDecoration="underline">  Hello{' '}  <Span    fontSize={50}    fontFamily="Poppins"    fontWeight={800}    textCase="original"    textDecoration="none"  >    World    <Span fontSize={30} fill="#F00" italic>      d    </Span>  </Span></Text>

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/7/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

New:

*   Added method [`figma.payments.getPluginPaymentTokenAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-payments/#getpluginpaymenttokenasync)
     to generate secure payment tokens for use in the [Payments REST API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/payments/)
    .

New:

*   [node.exportAsync](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-exportasync/)
     can now export directly to an SVG string, e.g. `vector.exportAsync({ format: 'SVG_STRING' })`.
*   Add `'PLAINTEXT'` to [code block languages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/CodeBlockNode/#codelanguage)
    

We now have plugin support for the text improvements launched with Little Big Updates!

New:

*   [leadingTrim](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#leadingtrim)
     text property.
*   [hangingPunctuation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#hangingpunctuation)
     to hang punctuation outside of text box.
*   [hangingList](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#hanginglist)
     to hang ordered list counters and unordered list bullets outside of text box.

We're introducing plugin support for tables in FigJam!

New:

*   [TableNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TableNode/)
     type. Create a TableNode with [createTable](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createtable/)
    

Following up on the [new video functionality](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/8878274530455#Prototype_with_video)
 recently added for prototyping, we have added some updates to our video plugin APIs, which include:

*   New `ON_MEDIA_HIT` and `ON_MEDIA_END` [prototyping triggers](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Trigger/)
     that fire when a video hits a certain timestamp and ends, respectively
*   Added support for [prototyping actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/)
     on the same Frame node as an existing [prototyping trigger](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Trigger/)
    
*   New `MUTE`, `UNMUTE`, and `TOGGLE_MUTE_UNMUTE` prototyping actions [prototyping actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/)
    
*   [`figma.createVideoAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createvideoasync/)
     now supports .MOV and .WebM videos up to 100MB in size.

As part of our launch to allow creators to sell resources directly on Figma Community, we're releasing a [Payments API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-payments/)
. This API will allow you to create custom time or usage-based free trials for plugins and widgets.

You can use this API to:

*   Read metadata about a user’s payment status and usage of your resource
*   Kick off a checkout flow so a user can pay for your resource

For more information on requiring payment in your plugins and widgets, please read [this guide](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/requiring-payment/)
.

New:

*   [componentPropertyReferences](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#componentpropertyreferences)
     now also apply to instance sublayers. For applicable instance sublayers, these will match the corresponding layer on the instance's main component.

New:

*   New method [createImageAsync](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createimageasync/)
     to create an image from a url string.
*   Add new field [consumers](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/BaseStyle/#consumers)
     to `BaseStyle`, which refers to the field where the style is applied.

Updates to [fetch](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/global-fetch/)
:

*   Add `headers` property to fetch request.
*   Add `json` method to fetch response.
*   `body` can now take a string.

In tandem with our launch of spell check in Figma and FigJam, we are adding support for text review plugins! A text review plugin is a plugin that can extend or replace the native spell check functionality to do things like grammar checking and brand guideline checking, or even check spelling in languages Figma doesn't support. And something unique about text review plugins compared to other plugins is that they start automatically anytime a user starts editing text in a document.

This comes with a few new APIs:

*   [`figma.on('textreview')`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-on/#textreview)
     listeners to suggest corrections while a user types
*   The [`figma.textreview`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-textreview/)
     object that has methods to manage if the plugin is enabled for text review by the user.

For more information on writing a text review plugin see [our guide](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/textreview-plugins/)
.

Other enhancements:

*   Add [`absoluteBoundingBox`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#absoluteboundingbox)
     property to `SectionNode`, `StickyNode`, `ShapeWithTextNode`, `CodeBlockNode`, `ConnectorNode`, `EmbedNode`, `LinkUnfurlNode`, `MediaNode`, and `WidgetNode`

New:

*   Added support for variant properties in [`addComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#addcomponentproperty)
    
*   Added a global [`fetch`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/global-fetch/)
     async function which closely mimics the browser API
*   Node `strokeWeight` can [now return `figma.mixed`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokeweight)
    

Fixed:

*   [HyperlinkTarget](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/HyperlinkTarget/)
     now works with more than just regular frames when using type `NODE`. For example, you can link to `SECTION`, `SYMBOL` and `INSTANCE` nodes too. The existing restriction where you can't link to a sublayer of an instance still applies.

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/9/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

New:

*   Added new `CIRCLE_FILLED` option to the [`ConnectorStrokeCap`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorStrokeCap/)
     type.

New:

*   New [`.stuckNodes`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StickyNode/#stucknodes)
     and [`.stuckTo`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StampNode/#stuckto)
     properties to read the nodes that are stuck on the reference node as well as the node current stuck to.
    *   `.stuckNodes` is available on all `SceneNode`
    *   `.stuckTo` is only available on “stickable” nodes (eg. [`StampNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StampNode/)
        , [`WidgetNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WidgetNode/)
        , [`HighlightNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/HighlightNode/)
        )
*   Added typings for [`HighlightNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/HighlightNode/)
     and [`WashiTapeNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WashiTapeNode/)
    .

New:

*   [`VectorRegion`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VectorNetwork/#vectorregion)
    s inside a `VectorNetwork` now have new `fills` and `fillStyleId` properties to let you get and set unique fills for each region in a vector network.

New:

*   Added [`node.getStyledTextSegments`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/TextNode-getstyledtextsegments/)
     for text nodes to make it much faster and easier to get text properties which may have mixed values, along with which characters these values apply to. Compared to retrieving text properties character-by-character, this new function can be 10x to 500x faster.
*   Drop events in [`figma.on`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-on/#drop)
     are now officially supported (was previously a [proposed API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
    ). This new API allows you to register a callback for drop events, which will trigger when objects outside Figma (such as elements from other browser windows, or files from the local filesystem) are dropped onto the canvas.
*   Added [`prototypeBackgrounds`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/#prototypebackgrounds)
     property to PageNode for configuring the background color of the prototype.
*   Added [`figma.getFileThumbnailNode()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getfilethumbnailnode)
     and [`figma.setFileThumbnailNodeAsync(node)`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#setfilethumbnailnodeasync)
     to get and set the thumbnail node for the current file.
*   Added [`figma.base64Encode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#base64encode)
     and [`figma.base64Decode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#base64decode)
     to convert between base64-encoded strings and `Uint8Array`s

New:

*   Added [`figma.skipInvisibleInstanceChildren`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-skipinvisibleinstancechildren/)
     to make all node properties and methods skip over invisible nodes (and their descendants) inside instances. This can make operations like document traversal much faster.
*   Added [`node.findAllWithCriteria`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-findallwithcriteria/)
     for much faster document traversal when searching by node types. The return value is narrowly typed to match the provided types, which makes it much easier to use node-type-specific properties. For example, `node.findAllWithCriteria({ types: ['TEXT'] })` will return `TextNode[]` instead of the more generic `SceneNode[]` from [`findAll`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-findall/)
    .
*   Added ability to enumerate plugin-data stored on a node:
    *   [`node.getPluginDataKeys()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#getplugindatakeys)
         Allows enumeration of plugin-data stored on a node
    *   [`node.getSharedPluginDataKeys(namespace)`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#getsharedplugindatakeys)
         Allows enumeration of shared plugin-data stored on a node in a given namespace
*   Style objects now support all plugin-data functionality
*   New functionality for the [`clientStorage`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-clientStorage/)
     API
    *   [`deleteAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-clientStorage/#deleteasync)
         allows deleting stored data
    *   [`keysAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-clientStorage/#keysasync)
         allows enumerating all stored data
*   [`node.getRelaunchData`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#getrelaunchdata)
     allows reading what relaunch data was stored using [`node.setRelaunchData`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#setrelaunchdata)
    

New:

*   Updated [`WidgetNode.cloneWidget`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/WidgetNode-clonewidget/)
     to support overriding synced maps.
*   Added [`WidgetNode.widgetId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WidgetNode/#widgetid)
     to provide a way to tell if a widget node belongs to your widget.
*   Added [`figma.pluginId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#pluginid)
     and [`figma.widgetId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#widgetid)
     for Plugins and Widgets respectively to get the value of the `manifest.json` "id" field at runtime. This is useful when paired with various WidgetNode apis as well as working with [non-null origin iframes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/creating-ui/#non-null-origin-iframes)
    .
*   Updated [`figma.showUI`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-showui/)
     to allow iframes to be positioned within the canvas at a specified x/y coordinate. If the position specified is outside of the user's viewport, the iframe will be moved so that it remains in the user's viewport.
*   Added [`createLinkPreviewAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createlinkpreviewasync/)
     which allows Plugin authors to create a link preview from the provided URL.
*   Added [`EmbedNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/EmbedNode/)
     and [`LinkUnfurlNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/LinkUnfurlNode/)
     which represent link previews
*   Add support for drop events in [`figma.on`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-on/#drop)
     as a [proposed API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
    . Drop events will trigger when objects outside Figma (such as elements from other browser windows, or files from the local filesystem) are dropped onto the canvas.

> NOTE: These APIs is only available in FigJam

New:

*   Added `getPluginData`, `setPluginData` and other pluginData related methods on [`WidgetNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WidgetNode/)
    
*   Added `exportSettings` and `exportAsync` on [`WidgetNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WidgetNode/)
    
*   Added [`CodeBlockNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/CodeBlockNode/)
     type. Create a code block with [`figma.createCodeBlock`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#createcodeblock)
    

New:

*   Added `absoluteRenderBounds` property for nodes to calculate the actual bounds of a node accounting for drop shadows, thick strokes, and anything else that may fall outside the node's regular bounding box defined in `x`, `y`, `width`, and `height`.
*   Added `fillGeometry` and `strokeGeometry` properties for nodes with shapes ([VectorNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VectorNode/)
    , [PolygonNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PolygonNode/)
    , etc...) to expose paths for object fills and strokes.

Bug Fixes:

*   Fix bug in [figma.showUI](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-showui/)
     that allowed plugins to open very small modals hiding the plugin icon and close button. The new minimum dimensions for the plugin UI is 70x0.

New:

*   Added `figma.currentUser` and `figma.activeUsers`, which can be viewed in [figma api](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/)
    
*   Added [WidgetNode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WidgetNode/)
    , which represents a widget in FigJam
*   Added `position` option to [showUI](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-showui/)
    

New:

*   Added [saveVersionHistoryAsync()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#saveversionhistoryasync)
    , which allows your plugin to manually add a checkpoint to the version history

Was this page helpful?

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/updates/page/2/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

*   We added a new [`positioning`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Frame/#positioning)
     to support absolutely positioned [AutoLayout](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/)
     children
    *   By default, positioning is set to `'auto'`, which preserves the existing behavior of layout out the child using auto layout.
*   We've updated [`AlignItems`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-AlignItems/)
     to accept a new `baseline` value.

Bug fixes:

*   Fixed a bug in undo/redo where multiple updates to synced state might clobber the values on the undo/redo stack.
*   Removed `flipVertical` from our docs and types since it is not implemented.

Additionally, we updated the Plugin API with some improvements to [`WidgetNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WidgetNode/)
. For more information see: [Plugin API Version 1, Update 46](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2022/05/04/version-1-update-46/)
.

*   We've extended the [`<Line />`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Line/)
     component with a [`strokeCap`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Line/#strokecap)
     prop, for specifying the line endpoint decoration. This can be one of the following:
    *   'none'
    *   'round'
    *   'square'
    *   'arrow-lines'
    *   'arrow-equilateral'
*   We've extended all components that support `stroke` with a [`strokeDashPattern`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#strokedashpattern)
     prop. This will give components a dashed stroke with the dash/gap lengths passed in.
*   We've added [`showShadowBehindNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Effect/#showshadowbehindnode)
     to the [`DropShadowEffect`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Effect/#dropshadoweffect)
     type, which controls whether drop shadows will be visible behind transparent layers.

Bug fixes:

*   Fixed a bug where the `spread` prop in [`ShadowEffect`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Effect/#spread)
     wasn't being mapped to the resulting shadow on the canvas.

*   We’re introducing a new [`<Input />` component](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/text-editing/)
     that lets users edit text directly in your widget.
*   We’re introducing 2 new hooks [`useStickable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usestickable/)
     and [`useStickableHost`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usestickablehost/)
     to allow widgets to interact more seamlessly with other objects on the canvas.
    *   Stickable widgets stick to other objects on the canvas
    *   Stickable Hosts allow other other stickable objects to be stuck on the widget (eg. Stamps)
*   Widgets now support undo/redo functionality. For more details, [see this guide](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/undo-redo/)
    .
*   Image URLs passed to [`<Image />`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/)
     no longer require CORs headers, any image URL just works!
*   Widgets now support [JSX fragments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Fragment/)
    ! You'll need to update your [tsconfig.json](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/typings/)
     to specify the new `jsxFragmentFactory`.
*   [`<Ellipse />`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Ellipse/)
     now supports an [`arcData` prop](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Ellipse/#arcdata)
     to enable rendering donuts and half circles.
*   We updated the visual appearance of a selected and hovered widget on the canvas
    *   We now show the widget’s icon as well as a link for users to learn more about the widget when the widget is hovered.
*   We added a [`<Line />`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Line/)
     component for drawing basic lines inside a widget.
*   [`<Frame />`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Frame/)
     children now support [x/y constraints](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Frame/#x)
     for absolute positioning within the frame.

Bug fixes:

*   Types for the `key` prop in custom components no longer need to be declared.
*   [Lazy default state initializer](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usesyncedstate/#lazy-initial-state)
     is allowed to use the Plugin API. This is useful for initializing synced state values to things like `figma.currentUser` or `figma.activeUsers`.

Additionally, we [updated the Plugin API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2022/04/06/version-1-update-44/)
 with new methods for `WidgetNode` to read the information of nodes stuck to the widget, or the node the widget is stuck to (if applicable). We hope these methods complement the new “stickable” hooks above.

*   We’re introducing 3 new [property menu item types](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usepropertymenu/)
    :
    
    | itemType | description |
    | --- | --- |
    | [`"separator"`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-PropertyMenu/#widgetpropertymenuseparatoritem) | A non-interactive element that can be used to organize different parts of the property menu. |
    | [`"color-selector"`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-PropertyMenu/#widgetpropertymenucoloritem) | A colorpicker menu item that allows users to select from a list of colors. |
    | [`"dropdown"`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-PropertyMenu/#widgetpropertymenudropdownitem) | A dropdown menu item that allows users to pick from a list of string options. |
    
*   We now return the x and y coordinates of where a user clicked in the [widget onClick handler](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/handling-user-events/#click-listeners)
    . This is useful for knowing where exactly a user clicked on your widget.
    
*   [`useSyncedState`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usesyncedstate/)
     now supports [lazy initial state](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usesyncedstate/#lazy-initial-state)
     and [functional updates](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usesyncedstate/#functional-updates)
    . This is equivalent to how setState behaves in React.
    
*   [`fill`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#fill)
     and [`stroke`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#stroke)
     props now support lists of `Paints`.
    

Bug fixes:

*   Our docs and typings now correctly show that `stroke`, `strokeWidth` and `strokeAlign` props exist on the [`Text component`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Text/)
    .
*   We improved the [`Paint`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Paint/)
     documentation page to add more examples of using `GradientPaint`.
*   Fixed a bug where certain `fontWeight` don't work for certain `fontFamily`.

Additionally, we updated the Plugin API with some improvements to [`WidgetNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/WidgetNode/)
. For more information see: [Plugin API Version 1, Update 39](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2021/11/11/version-1-update-39/)
.

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

New

*   Added [`VideoPaint`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#videopaint)
     type for adding video fills to nodes.
*   Added [`figma.createVideoAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createvideoasync/)
     to create a [`Video`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Video/)
     handle from an array of bytes.
*   Added [resetVideoPosition](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/)
     property to Prototyping Actions.
*   Added [UPDATE\_MEDIA\_RUNTIME](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/#update_media_runtime-action)
     action type for pausing, playing, and toggling a video's playback state

This release adds support for four new APIs:

*   [`InstanceNode.overrides`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#overrides)
     returns a list of overrides on an instance.
*   [`InstanceNode.resetOverrides`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#resetoverrides)
     resets all of the overrides on an instance.
*   [`editComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#editcomponentproperty)
     for component set nodes now supports renaming variant properties.
*   new `documentchange` option in `figma.on`

[`documentchange`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-on/#documentchange)
 will trigger when a node or style change is made to the currently open file.

The callback will be passed with a DocumentChangeEvent with the below interface:

    interface DocumentChangeEvent {  documentChanges: DocumentChange[]}

Example:

      figma.on("documentchange", (event) => {    for (const change of event.documentChanges) {      console.log("document changes: ", change.id, change.origin, change.type);      // Node changes also have change.node, style changes have change.style    }  })

This release adds support for two new APIs:

*   `figma.createNodeFromJSXAsync`
*   `ComponentNode.instances`

### `figma.createNodeFromJSXAsync`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#figmacreatenodefromjsxasync "Direct link to figmacreatenodefromjsxasync")

[`figma.createNodeFromJSXAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createnodefromjsxasync/)
 lets you create nodes using the same JSX API that widgets use. This is a declarative, HTML-like API that should be more ergonomic when using the plugin API to create deeply nested node structures. Here is a quick example:

    const { Image, AutoLayout } = figma.widgetconst node = await figma.createNodeFromJSXAsync(  <AutoLayout fill="#F00" padding={20}>    <Image src="https://picsum.photos/200" width={200} height={200} />  </AutoLayout>,)

You can pair `figma.createNodeFromJSXAsync` with our [Widget Code Generator plugin](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1096460041736534298/Widget-Code-Generator)
 to to turn designs from Figma files into code you can embed into your plugin.

Note that using this API requires setting up your build system to compile JSX. You can learn more about this by following along with [the documentation here](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-createnodefromjsxasync/)
.

### `ComponentNode.instances`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#componentnodeinstances "Direct link to componentnodeinstances")

[`ComponentNode.instances`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#instances)
 returns a list of instance nodes in the file for a given main component node.

This release adds support for the [component properties beta](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/4406787442711#component-properties)
 features and adds the the ability to read the fontWeight of text nodes.

### Component Properties[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#component-properties "Direct link to Component Properties")

Exposed Instances

*   [`isExposedInstance`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#isexposedinstance)
    : indicates whether this `InstanceNode` has been marked as exposed to its containing `ComponentNode` or `ComponentSetNode`
*   [`exposedInstances`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#exposedinstances)
    : all nested instances that have been exposed to this `InstanceNode`'s level

Preferred Values

*   [`componentPropertyDefinitions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#componentpropertydefinitions)
    : updated to return [`InstanceSwapPreferredValue`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceSwapPreferredValue/)
    `[]` for `'INSTANCE_SWAP'` component property definitions
*   [`addComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#addcomponentproperty)
    : updated to accept [`InstanceSwapPreferredValue`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceSwapPreferredValue/)
    `[]` when adding an `'INSTANCE_SWAP'` component property
*   [`editComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#editcomponentproperty)
    : updated to accept [`InstanceSwapPreferredValue`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceSwapPreferredValue/)
    `[]` when editing an `'INSTANCE_SWAP'` component property

### Font Weight[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#font-weight "Direct link to Font Weight")

*   [`fontWeight`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextNode/#fontweight)
    : The weight of the font (e.g. 400 for "Regular", 700 for "Bold"). This property is readonly and cannot be set directly.
*   [`getRangeFontWeight`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextSublayer/#getrangefontweight)
    : gets the fontWeight from characters in range start (inclusive) to end (exclusive).

New:

*   You can now add a button with a custom action to the notifications created by [`notify`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-notify/)
    . You can also add an onDequeue function that runs when the notification is removed. This function has a string parameter that represents the reason why the notification was removed.

### New[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#new "Direct link to New")

*   You can now retrieve the author of a [`StampNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StampNode/)
    , with [`getAuthorAsync`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StampNode/#getauthorasync)
    
*   `BASH` is now a supported [`codeLanguage`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/CodeBlockNode/#codelanguage)
     for [`CodeBlockNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/CodeBlockNode/)
    
*   A new [`absoluteBoundingBox`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#absoluteboundingbox)
     property has been added to several nodes
*   A new [`attachedConnectors`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#attachedconnectors)
     property has been added to nodes that can have connectors

### Bug fixes[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#bug-fixes "Direct link to Bug fixes")

Previously, [`stuckNodes`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-stucknodes/)
 were not marked `readonly` in the typings. It was a runtime error to try to set this property, and the typings now reflect this.

### Breaking interface rename[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/8/#breaking-interface-rename "Direct link to Breaking interface rename")

`LayerSublayerNode` has been renamed to `LabelSublayerNode`. The previous name was a typo.

This release adds support for the [component properties](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/5579474826519-Create-and-use-component-properties)
 feature with new properties and functions on each of the following node types:

ComponentSetNode

*   [`componentPropertyDefinitions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#componentpropertydefinitions)
    
*   [`addComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#addcomponentproperty)
    
*   [`editComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#editcomponentproperty)
    
*   [`deleteComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#deletecomponentproperty)
    

ComponentNode (non-variants only)

*   [`componentPropertyDefinitions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#componentpropertydefinitions)
    
*   [`addComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#addcomponentproperty)
    
*   [`editComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#editcomponentproperty)
    
*   [`deleteComponentProperty`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#deletecomponentproperty)
    

SceneNode (must be a component sublayer and not nested within an instance node)

*   [`componentPropertyReferences`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/#componentpropertyreferences)
    

InstanceNode

*   [`componentProperties`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#componentproperties)
    

The [`setProperties`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#setproperties)
 function on `InstanceNode` was also updated to work with component properties.

The [`variantGroupProperties`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#variantgroupproperties)
 property on `ComponentSetNode` is now deprecated. It will continue to the return the variant group properties but it is recommended to use the new [`componentPropertyDefinitions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/#componentpropertydefinitions)
 property.

The [`variantProperties`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#variantproperties)
 property will continue to be supported on `ComponentNode` but is now deprecated on `InstanceNode`s. It will continue to return the variant properties on an instance but it is now recommended to the use the new [`componentProperties`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/#componentproperties)
 property.

This release adds support for the [individual strokes](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/360049283914-Apply-and-adjust-stroke-properties#individual)
 feature with new properties for each side of a rectangle or frame-like node:

*   [`strokeTopWeight`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#stroketopweight)
    
*   [`strokeBottomWeight`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokebottomweight)
    
*   [`strokeLeftWeight`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokeleftweight)
    
*   [`strokeRightWeight`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokerightweight)
    

Assigning a value to the [`strokeWeight`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokeweight)
 property will apply the same weight to all four sides.

caution

⚠️ `strokeWeight` may return a stale value if the node is using individual stroke weights. In the near future, we will be changing this to return [`figma.mixed`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-mixed/)
 but are leaving it as an inaccurate number for now. If you want accurate stroke widths, please use the new properties above going forward.

New:

*   Support for individual strokes
*   Added [`figma.ungroup`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-ungroup/)
     for ungrouping nodes, which is roughly equivalent to Ctrl-Shift-G/⌘⇧G in the editor.
*   Added new autolayout properties:
    *   [`layoutPositioning`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-layoutpositioning/)
        : Determines whether a layer's size and position should be determined by auto-layout settings or manually adjustable
    *   [`strokesIncludedInLayout`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokesincludedinlayout)
        : Determines whether strokes are included in layout calculations
    *   [`itemReverseZIndex`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#itemreversezindex)
        : Determines the canvas stacking order of layers in this frame
*   Updated autolayout properties:
    *   [`counterAxisAlignItems`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-counteraxisalignitems/)
        : Added `"BASELINE"` option for aligning all children along the text baseline on horizontal auto-layout frames.
    *   [`textAutoResize`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/TextNode-textautoresize/)
        : Added `"TRUNCATE"` option for truncating overflowing text with an ellipse.

Fixes to the plugin typings:

*   Add missing `rotation` property to [`ShapeWithTextNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ShapeWithTextNode/)
     and [`ConnectorNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorNode/)
    
*   Fix `opacity` and `blendMode` properties incorrectly marked as optional and readonly in [`CodeBlockNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/CodeBlockNode/)
    , [`ConnectorNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorNode/)
    , [`ShapeWithTextNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ShapeWithTextNode/)
    , and [`StickyNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StickyNode/)
    .
*   Add missing `fills` and `fillStyleId` properties to [`TextSublayer`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/TextSublayer/)
    

New:

*   New `themeColors` option in [`figma.showUI()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-showui/)
     to allow plugin UIs to [support light and dark themes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/css-variables/)
    

New:

*   Added new API method [`setWidgetSyncedState`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/WidgetNode-setwidgetsyncedstate/)
     on widget nodes
*   Added new API method [`findWidgetNodesByWidgetId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-findwidgetnodesbywidgetid/)
     to find all widget nodes by a specified `node.widgetId`

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/5/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

*   `null` is now supported for the `variable` field in [setBoundVariableForPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#setboundvariableforpaint)
    , [setBoundVariableForEffect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#setboundvariableforeffect)
    , and [setBoundVariableForLayoutGrid](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#setboundvariableforlayoutgrid)
    . Passing in `null` will detach the bound variables.
    
*   For plugins that can run in VSCode, we now recommend replacing `alert()` and `confirm()` dialogs in the plugin UI with custom UI, since VS Code blocks native dialogs. The [Working in Dev Mode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-dev-mode/#dev-mode-plugins-in-visual-studio-code)
     page is updated accordingly.
    

Updates:

*   Plugins can now view and set explicit modes for each variable collection on [PageNodes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PageNode/)
    .
*   The [BaseStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/BaseStyle/)
     type is now defined as a union of all possible derived style interfaces, such as [PaintStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/PaintStyle/)
     and [GridStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/GridStyle/)
    . This allows you to use [type narrowing](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/narrowing.html)
     on the the `type` property to go from an instance of `BaseStyle` to a child style type.
*   Updated the color token values in the [CSS Variables and Theming page](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/css-variables/)
     for the “Figma Design: Light” and “Figma Design: Dark” modes to reflect recent formatting changes to token values injected into the plugin iframe (rgba() values were changed to #rrggbbaa format).
*   Variables can now be bound to Effect and Layout Grid styles. On a selection, variables bound to a given style are populated in `boundVariables` keyed by style type (`effects` or `layoutGrids`).

This update includes changes to the Variables API to support binding variables to effects, layout grids, stroke weights, and layer opacity. We are also releasing support to run Dev Mode plugins in the Figma for VS Code extension.

Changes to the Variables API:

*   `FLOAT` and `COLOR` variables can be bound to [Effects](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Effect/)
     via the [`setBoundVariablesForEffect()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#setboundvariableforeffect)
     helper.
*   `FLOAT` variables can be bound to [LayoutGrids](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/LayoutGrid/)
     via the [`setBoundVariablesForLayoutGrid()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#setboundvariableforlayoutgrid)
     helper.
*   New [VariableBindableNodeField](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableBindableNodeField/)
     types, `FLOAT` variables can be bound to layer opacity, stroke weights, and individual stroke weights.
*   New [VariableScope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableScope/)
     types for scoping variables to layer opacity, stroke weights, and effects.

Dev Mode plugins can now run in the Figma for VS Code extension with some minor manifest and code changes:

*   [manifest.json](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#capabilities)
     changes - added new `vscode` capability
*   [figma.vscode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#vscode)
     - this new namespace allows the plugin to determine whether it's running in the VS Code Extension or not.
*   [figma.openExternal](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-openexternal/)
     - this API enables opening a URL externally and is necessary to use in the VS Code Extension.
*   See all of the details in [Dev Mode Plugins in VS Code](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-dev-mode/#dev-mode-plugins-in-visual-studio-code)
    

Fixes:

*   [`fillGeometry`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#fillgeometry)
     and [`strokeGeometry`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#strokegeometry)
     properties are correctly marked as `readonly`

**New**:

*   We are introducing `NEGATE` as a Prototyping ExpressionFunction type!

**New**:

*   Plugins are now able to explicitly unbind variables from nodes. See [setBoundVariable](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#setboundvariable)
    .
*   `variableIds` are now returned in a similar order to the Figma Design UI. See [VariableCollection#variableIds](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableCollection/#variableids)
    
*   Adds ability to set and query pluginData on [Variable](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/)
     and [Variable Collection](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableCollection/)
     nodes.
*   Adds `textRangeFills` to the `boundVariables` structure. This will be populated on text nodes which have color variables bound to substrings.
*   Plugins/widgets that touch straight connectors and set endpoint magnets other than CENTER or NONE will now error in development. Please update your code to use the CENTER or NONE magnets for straight connectors before 2024. See [Version 1, Update 79](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/2023/10/04/version-1-update-79/)
     for more details.
*   Added support for the [devStatus](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#devstatus)
     field on [Frames](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/FrameNode/)
    , [Components](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentNode/)
    , [Component Sets](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentSetNode/)
    , and [Instances](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/)
    .

The Plugin API runtime has been updated to ensure proper usage of [`figma.showUI`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-showui/)
.

It is unsafe to call `figma.showUI` within an event handler for the [`figma.codegen.on("generate")`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-codegen-on/)
 event. In development, `figma.showUI` will now **throw an error** when called as a result of a `figma.codegen.on("generate")`

If you need to trigger code inside an iframe as the result of a generate callback, you should instead move the call to `showUI` outside of the event handler and use [`figma.ui.postMessage`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-ui-postmessage/)
 to communicate with the iframe from within the callback. This will ensure that your plugin can to handle concurrent `generate` events. Here's a [code example](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/codegen-plugins/#using-figmashowui-for-code-generation)
 for how to do this.

We're introducing an improved way to attach [straight connectors](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorNode/#connectorlinetype)
 to nodes in FigJam with a new [CENTER magnet](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorEndpoint/)
. Starting in 2024, straight connectors will only be able to attach to CENTER and NONE magnets. The magnets for elbowed connectors will remain unchanged.

**Notice of breaking change:**

caution

To support CENTER magnets as the improved way for [straight connectors](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorNode/#connectorlinetype)
 to attach to nodes, we are making a **breaking change** for plugins that set straight connector [endpoint magnets](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ConnectorEndpoint/)
 to AUTO, TOP, LEFT, BOTTOM, or RIGHT.

Who is affected: Anyone with a plugin/widget that touches straight connectors and sets an endpoint magnet to AUTO, TOP, LEFT, BOTTOM, or RIGHT.

What you must do: Find and update the code that sets the endpoints of straight connectors to use the CENTER or NONE magnet.

What will happen now:

*   In-development plugins/widgets will see a non-blocking warning if a magnet other than CENTER or NONE is set for a straight connector.

What will happen starting October 25, 2023:

*   In-development plugins/widgets will always throw an error if a magnet other than CENTER or NONE is set for a straight connector.

What will happen in 2024:

*   Published plugins/widgets will always throw an error if a magnet other than CENTER or NONE is set for a straight connector.

**New**

*   Add better documentation for resolving variable values. See [resolveForConsumer](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/#resolveforconsumer)
     and [resolvedVariableNodes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-resolvedvariablemodes/)
    
*   Add support for localhost in the manifest networkAccess allowedDomains field. See [manifest networkAccess docs](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#networkaccess)
    .
*   Add the [maskType](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/MaskType/)
     property. For nodes that are used as masks (where `node.isMask == true`), `maskType` can be used to get/modify the masking type used (alpha, vector, or luminance) for the mask operation.

**New**:

*   Add [detachedInfo](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DetachedInfo/)
     to detached instances to indicate if a node is detached from the main component, and to identify its original main component.
*   Add [figma.constants.colors](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-constants-colors/)
    , which maps color palette names to [ColorPalette](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ColorPalette/)
    . We provide `figJamBase` and `figJamBaseLight`, a set of official FigJam colors.
*   Each [`Reaction`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Reaction/)
     object has an `actions` array replacing the existing `action` property for executing [multiple actions](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/15253220891799)
     on the same trigger
*   The [`Action`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/)
     object supports two new action types:
    *   [`"SET_VARIABLE"`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/#set_variable-action)
         for [modifying the value of a variable](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/14506587589399)
         based on a static value, another variable, or an expression
    *   [`"CONDITIONAL"`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/#conditional-action)
         for checking if a condition is met before performing an action
    *   For a general guide on using the new action types, see the updated guide for the [Action](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/)
         object.

See the [`reactions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-reactions/)
 property page for more details and code samples.

**Notice of breaking change:**

caution

To support multiple actions per trigger in the [`Reaction`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Reaction/)
 object, we are making a **breaking change** for plugins that set the `reactions` property.

Who is affected: Anyone with a plugin/widget with code that sets the `reactions` property.

What you must do: Check and update your code to use the new `actions` property in each `Reaction` object.

What will happen now:

*   In-development plugins/widgets that set `reactions` with only the `action` object without the new `actions` array will always throw an error. This is to prevent data loss on nodes that already have reactions with multiple actions.
*   Code that sets `reactions` with both `action` and `actions` are fine. `actions` will take precedence and `action` will be ignored.

What will happen starting November 9:

*   Published plugins/widgets will always throw an error if the `actions` array is not present when setting `reactions`.

This update fixes a bug with the optional `networkAccess` key in the plugin manifest. Previously, we had an error where we would treat local worker scripts as well as `blob:` URLs as external resources. If you were affected by this, you can now update your `networkAccess` field.

New:

*   Figma will now return [inferred variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-inferredvariables/)
     when there are variables in the file that match the raw values used in your design.
*   Add [openTypeFeatures](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/TextNode-opentypefeatures/)
     property for text nodes to expose OpenType features that have been explicitly enabled or disabled.

# Updates | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/updates/page/6/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The [Variable object](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/)
 has been extended with with the ability to read and define a variable's code syntax field. Code syntax allows you to represent design variables in code using custom, syntactically correct variable names to support a seamless handoff experience. It can be configured for different contexts, including CSS, SwiftUI, and Compose. A variable's code syntax will appear in [Dev Mode](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/15023124644247)
's code snippets when inspecting elements using the variable.

New:

*   [Variable.codeSyntax](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/#codesyntax)
     describes the current code syntax of a variable.
*   [Variable.setVariableCodeSyntax](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/Variable-setvariablecodesyntax/)
     and [Variable.removeVariableCodeSyntax](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/#removevariablecodesyntax)
     let you modify the the `codeSyntax` field.

This update contains a small change for users of the Variables API public beta.

When getting or assigning elements to the [Variable.scopes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/Variable-scopes/)
 array, please update your code to use `'STROKE_COLOR'` instead of `'STROKE'`. The meaning is the same. The value `'STROKE'` is deprecated and will no longer be supported in our type definitions.

For a full list of possible values, see [VariableScope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableScope/)
.

[Color management](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039825114)
 is now supported within Figma! This version introduces properties for the color profiles of documents and exports.

New:

*   Read-only access to the [document's color profile](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/DocumentNode/#documentcolorprofile)
    .
*   Read/write access to [colorProfile](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ExportSettings/#colorprofile)
     for [ExportSettings](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ExportSettings/)
    .

This version introduces utility functions that allow you to set color properties using a variety of familiar encodings, including CSS hex strings and `rgb()`/`hsl()`\-style function strings. The new functions include:

*   [figma.util.rgb()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-util-rgb/)
    : converts a color encoding into an [RGB](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RGB/)
     object.
*   [figma.util.rgba()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-util-rgba/)
    : converts a color encoding into an [RGBA](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RGB/)
     object.
*   [figma.util.solidPaint()](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-util-solidpaint/)
    : converts a color encoding into a [SolidPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#solidpaint)
     object.

Examples:

    const paint = {  type: 'SOLID',  color: figma.util.rgb('#FF0')}const layoutGrid = {  pattern: 'GRID',  sectionSize: 1,  color: figma.util.rgba('rgb(25% 25% 25%)')}

You can alias these functions for more concise code:

    const rgb = figma.util.rgbconst rgba = figma.util.rgbaconst paint = {  type: 'SOLID',  color: rgb('#FF0')}const layoutGrid = {  pattern: 'GRID',  sectionSize: 1,  color: rgba('rgb(25% 25% 25%)')}

A common use case is to set a node fill. Use the `figma.util.solidPaint()` function when you want to update the color of a fill while preserving other properties of the fill object:

    if (node.fills[0].type === 'SOLID') {  node.fills = [    // Merge the original fill with a new color and opacity    figma.util.solidPaint("#FF00FF88", node.fills[0])  ]}

Other changes in this version:

*   Add new assignable values for [ShapeWithTextNode.shapeTypes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ShapeWithTextNode/#shapetype)
    : `TRAPEZOID`, `PREDEFINED_PROCESS`, `SHIELD`, `DOCUMENT_SINGLE`, `DOCUMENT_MULTIPLE`, `MANUAL_INPUT`, `HEXAGON`, `CHEVRON`, `PENTAGON`, `OCTAGON`, `STAR`, `PLUS`, `ARROW_LEFT`, `ARROW_RIGHT`, `SUMMING_JUNCTION`, `OR`, `SPEECH_BUBBLE`, `INTERNAL_STORAGE`

New:

*   [`node.findAllWithCriteria`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-findallwithcriteria/)
     now supports searching by pluginData as well as sharedPluginData. This should make it much more performant if you're trying to find all nodes that have pluginData / sharedPluginData.
*   Add `hiddenFromPublishing` as a property on [`Variable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/)
     and [`VariableCollection`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableCollection/#hiddenfrompublishing)
    .
*   Add `getPublishStatusAsync()` as a method on [`Variable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Variable/#getpublishstatusasync)
     and [`VariableCollection`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/VariableCollection/#getpublishstatusasync)
     which returns a promise.
*   Add `sectionContentsHidden` as a property on [`SectionNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/SectionNode/#sectioncontentshidden)
    .

New:

*   `StickyNode` interface now has an [`isWideWidth`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/StickyNode/#iswidewidth)
     property, indicating whether the sticky note uses a [wide rectangular shape](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500004414322-Sticky-notes-in-FigJam#Size)
    , or the regular square shape.

Coinciding with the roll-out of AutoLayout v5 in Figma, we're excited to bring you plugin support for these new enhancements! This update includes various new properties to our Plugin API which will allow for more precise control of layout and positioning parameters using AutoLayout v5.

New:

*   Nodes that support `width` and `height` now include optional properties like `minWidth`, `maxWidth`, `minHeight`, and `maxHeight`.
*   `LayoutMixin` interface has been supplemented with `'layoutSizingHorizontal'` and `layoutSizingVertical` properties supporting `'FIXED'`, `'HUG'`, and `'FILL'` values. This means that most nodes that accept children and can be made the child of an auto layout node support these properties.
*   `FrameNode`s with auto layout enabled now support `layoutWrap`, `counterAxisAlignContent`, and `counterAxisSpacing`.
*   `TextNode` interface has been expanded to include `textTruncation` and `maxLines`.

Coinciding with the launch of Figma design's new [Dev Mode](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/15023124644247)
, and Figma's launch of variables, this update adds a number of new APIs and methods for plugins to extend Dev Mode and interact with variables!

[Variables](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/15339657135383)
 in Figma design store reusable values that can be applied to all kinds of design properties and prototyping actions. They help save time and effort when building designs, managing design systems, and creating complex prototyping flows.

And Dev Mode is a mode in Figma design files that is dedicated to giving front-end developers everything they need to navigate design files and implement designs into code. Plugins in Dev Mode can be used for inspection and code generation.

**Inspection:** Plugins can take over the Inspect panel in Dev Mode and pull in relevant context and information from other tools or APIs.

**Code generation:** Plugins can customize code generation using languages and frontend frameworks that Figma doesn’t support natively, and Figma will handle things like syntax highlighting for these plugins out of the box.

For a general overview of how to build plugins for Dev Mode, check out the new [Working in Dev Mode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-in-dev-mode/)
 guide.

**New:**

*   [`figma.editorType`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#editortype)
     can now return `dev`, when a plugin is running in Dev Mode.
*   [`figma.getSelectionColors()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma/#getselectioncolors)
     will tell you all of the colors and paint styles in the user’s current selection.
*   [`node.isAsset`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#isasset)
     tells you if Figma detects that a node is an icon or image.
*   [`node.getCSSAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#getcssasync)
     will give you the CSS that Figma generates for that node.
*   [`node.getDevResourcesAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-getdevresourcesasync/)
    , [`node.addDevResourceAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-adddevresourceasync/)
    , [`node.editDevResourceAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-editdevresourceasync/)
    , and [`node.deleteDevResourceAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-deletedevresourceasync/)
     methods for interacting with [Dev resources](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/15023124644247#Add_external_links_and_resources_for_developers)
    .
*   [`manifest.capabilities`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#capabilities)
     can now include `"inspect"` when you want your plugin to run in the Plugins panel in Dev Mode, or `"codegen"` when you want to build a plugin for code generation, which runs in the Code section of the Inspect panel in Dev Mode.
*   `figma.mode` can now return `"inspect"` when your plugin runs in the Plugins panel in Dev Mode, or `"codegen"` when your plugin runs in the Code section of the Inspect panel in Dev Mode.
*   [`manifest.codegenLanguages`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#codegenlanguages)
    , which specifies the languages your plugin supports for code generation.
*   [`manifest.codegenPreferences`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#codegenpreferences)
    , allows plugins for code generation to define custom commands that show up in the native language dropdown in Dev Mode. In these commands, you can run plugin code, including the ability to open an iFrame using the new `figma.codegen.preferences` method. This is helpful for allowing users to customize the generated code from your plugin.
*   [`node.inferredAutoLayout`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#inferredautolayout)
     will let you get estimated auto layout properties on a `FrameNode` and its children, even if they do not use auto layout. If you are building a plugin for code generation, this will help you generate better code.
*   A bunch of other methods and events specific to [plugins for code generation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/codegen-plugins/)
    .
*   [`node.exportAsync()`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-exportasync/)
     now accepts `format: "JSON_REST_V1"`, which returns the same REST formatted JSON for a node as the [Figma REST API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/)
    .

**Variables:** Plugins can create, modify, and bind variables to various node properties and styles.

For a general guide on the features that variables can unlock in your design files, see the new [Working with variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/)
 guide.

**New:**

*   [`figma.variables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/)
     defines several functions to create and manipulate local variables.
*   [`SceneNode.boundVariables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#boundvariables)
     enumerates the variables bound on a given `Scene node`.
*   [`SceneNode.setBoundVariable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#setboundvariable)
     can set a bound variable on various node properties.
*   [`SceneNode.explicitVariableModes`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/RectangleNode/#explicitvariablemodes)
     enumerates the explicit (non-inherited) variable modes for a given node.
*   [`SolidPaint.boundVariables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Paint/#boundvariables)
     enumerates the variables bound on a given `SolidPaint`.
*   [`ComponentProperties.boundVariables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/ComponentProperties/)
     enumerates the variables controlling component properties on an `InstanceNode`.

**Team library API:** Plugins can now query the available library variable collections and variables via the Plugin API. See [figma.teamLibrary](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-teamlibrary/)
 for more info.

**New:**

*   [`figma.teamLibrary`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-teamlibrary/)
     defines several functions to query your library assets and files
*   [`manifest.permissions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/#permissions)
     now includes a permission to control the `figma.teamLibrary` Plugin API.

This update adds two parameters for the ["NODE" prototyping `Action`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/#node-action)
:

*   `resetScrollPosition`: Whether to reset the scroll position of any scrollable elements. The parameter only applies if the current node and the destination have the same layout.
*   `resetInteractiveComponents`: Whether to reset the state of any interactive components. The parameter only applies if the destination has interactive components.

Use the parameters to manage the scroll behavior and component state of nodes in your prototype.

The `resetScrollPosition` parameter replaces `preserveScrollPosition`, which remains supported only for backwards compatibility. Instead of `preserveScrollPosition`, if you want to preserve the current scroll position in the prototype, set `resetScrollPosition` to `false`.

For more information about the parameters and state management for prototypes, see:

*   ["NODE" Action](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/Action/#node-action)
    
*   [State management for prototypes](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/14397859494295)
    

This update introduces the optional `networkAccess` key to the plugin manifest.

Add the `networkAccess` to your plugin's `manifest.json` file to specify the domains that your plugin is permitted to access. When `networkAccess` is applied, if your plugin makes a network request to a domain that is not specified in the list of permitted domains, that request is blocked.

For example:

    {  "name": "MyPlugin",  "id": "737805260747778092",  "api": "1.0.0",  "main": "code.js",  "ui": "ui.html",  "networkAccess": {    "allowedDomains": ["https://my-app.cdn.com", "wss://socket.io", "*.example.com", "example.com/api/",  "exact-path.com/content"]  }}

When you publish your plugin, the list of domains that you specify for `networkAccess` is displayed on your plugin's Community page. This information is also visible for org admins when plugins are reviewed for approval.

To try this out:

1.  In the `manifest.json` file for your plugin, add the following:
    
        "networkAccess": {  "allowedDomains": ["none"]}
    

`none` is a special keyword for `allowedDomains` that prevents any network access from your plugin. 2. In Figma, create a new Figma or FigJam file and add your plugin. Try to use the plugin as normal. 3. Check the developer console. If your plugin makes network requests, such as calls to an API or fetching images, Figma blocks the requests and throws [content-security policy (CSP)](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CSP)
 errors. 4. To fix the CSP errors, in your plugin manifest, replace `["none"]` with the domains that your plugin needs to access.

For more information about network access, see:

*   [How Plugins Run](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/how-plugins-run/)
    
*   [Making Network Requests](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/making-network-requests/)
    
*   [Plugin Manifest](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/)
