# Working with Variables | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Variables in Figma design store reusable values that can be applied to all kinds of design properties and prototyping actions. They help save time and effort when building designs, managing design systems, and creating complex prototyping flows.

Use your plugin to interact with variables in Figma, including creating and reading variables. The Plugin API provides methods using the [`figma.variables`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/)
 global object to interact with variables.

This guide contains the following sections:

*   [Get variable collections and variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#get-variables)
    
*   [Create variable collections and variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#create-variables)
    
*   [Nodes and bound variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#bound-variables)
    
*   [Typography variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#typography-variables)
    
*   [Helpers](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#helpers)
    
*   [Samples](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#samples)
    

note

This guide assumes that you’re familiar with the basics of creating Figma plugins. If you’re new to developing plugins, check out the [Build your first plugin](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/4407260620823--BYFP-1-Overview)
 course — you can copy and paste the code from this guide into the plugin you build.

* * *

Get variable collections and variables[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#get-variables "Direct link to Get variable collections and variables")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use one of the following methods to let your plugin get variables and variable collections.

### [getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#getlocalvariablecollectionsasync)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#getlocalvariablecollectionsasync "Direct link to getlocalvariablecollectionsasync")

The `figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync` method returns all local variable collections in the current file.

Example usage:

    const localCollections = await figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync();

### [getVariableCollectionByIdAsync](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-variables-getvariablecollectionbyidasync/)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#getvariablecollectionbyidasync "Direct link to getvariablecollectionbyidasync")

The `figma.variables.getVariableCollectionByIdAsync` method finds a variable collection by ID. If not found or the provided ID is invalid, returns `null`.

Example usage:

    const collection = await figma.variables.getVariableCollectionByIdAsync('VariableCollectionId:257c3beb2/57:13');

### [getLocalVariablesAsync](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-variables-getlocalvariablesasync/)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#getlocalvariablesasync "Direct link to getlocalvariablesasync")

The `figma.variables.getLocalVariablesAsync` method returns all local variables in the current file, optionally filtering by resolved type.

Example usage:

    const localVariables = await figma.variables.getLocalVariablesAsync('STRING'); // filters local variables by the 'STRING' type

### [getVariableByIdAsync](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-variables-getvariablebyidasync/)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#getvariablebyidasync "Direct link to getvariablebyidasync")

The `figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync` method finds a variable by ID. If not found or the provided ID is invalid, returns `null`.

Example usage:

    const variable = await figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync(variableId);

* * *

Create variable collections and variables[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#create-variables "Direct link to Create variable collections and variables")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use one of the following methods to create variables or variable collections.

### [createVariableCollection](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-variables-createvariablecollection/)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#createvariablecollection "Direct link to createvariablecollection")

To create a variable collection, use the `figma.variables.createVariableCollection` method.

Example usage:

    const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection('Example Collection');

### [createVariable](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/figma-variables-createvariable/)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#createvariable "Direct link to createvariable")

To create a variable, use the `figma.variables.createVariable` method.

Example usage:

    const variable = figma.variables.createVariable(  'ExampleVariableName',  collection,  'STRING');

This example creates a variable with the name `ExampleVariableName` of the `STRING` type. The variable is created inside a collection with the ID `57:13`.

* * *

Nodes and bound variables[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#bound-variables "Direct link to Nodes and bound variables")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For a variable to apply its value to a node, the variable must be bound to the node. In the context of the Figma user interface, when a variable is applied to a node (for example, if you set a color variable for a stroke on a rectangle), the alias for that variable is captured in the `boundVariables` property of the node.

You can also use the Plugin API to bind variables to nodes with our [helper methods](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#helpers)
, and the `setBoundVariable` method that is available for many types of nodes. For a list of the supported nodes, see [`setBoundVariable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-setboundvariable/)
.

To use `setBoundVariable`, you must provide a [valid bindable field](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/node-properties/#boundvariables)
 and the variable that you want to bind to the field. The variable that you're binding must match the data type of the field. For example, the `SHAPE_WITH_TEXT` node has an `opacity` property that accepts numbers. In this case, the variable you bind to `opacity` must be a number variable.

The following example creates a variable collection and variable, and then applies that variable to an example node.

setBoundVariable example

    const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("example-collection")const widthVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("width-variable", collection, "FLOAT")const exampleNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")// Simple fields can be bound using setBoundVariableexampleNode.setBoundVariable("width", widthVariable)

To get the aliases for variables that are bound to a node, use the `boundVariables` property on nodes.

Get bound variables example

    const exampleNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")const exampleNodeVariables = exampleNode.boundVariables/* Example boundVariables object:  {    "strokes": [      {        "type": "VARIABLE_ALIAS",        "id": "VariableID:1:7"      }    ]  }*/

To unbind or remove a variable from a node, set the bound field to `null`.

Unbind a variable example

    const exampleNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")// Simple fields can be unbound using setBoundVariable and nullexampleNode.setBoundVariable("width", null)

To learn more about binding variables, check out the [code samples](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#samples)
.

* * *

Typography variables[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#typography-variables "Direct link to Typography variables")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Typography variables are used to control complex type across your Figma files and design systems. You can use typography variables to manage everything from typeface to the spacing between characters and paragraphs.

Variables can be used to control text styling for `TextNodes`, `TextSublayers` (substrings of styled text), and `TextStyles`. Paragraph spacing and indentation cannot be applied to `TextSublayers`.

The following properties are bindable:

*   **`fontFamily`**: Accepts string variables. The value for the variable must correspond to a font name, such as `Inter`.
*   **`fontStyle`**: Accepts string variables. The value for the variable must map to a valid style for the font. The specific style values depend on the font you are using. For example, when `fontFamily` is `Inter`, `fontStyle` can be set to values such as `Light`, `Regular`, or `Semi Bold`, among other styles supported by the font.
*   **`fontWeight`**: Accepts number variables. If the number value doesn't correspond to a valid weight for the font, the closest valid weight is used.
*   **`lineHeight`**: Accepts number variables.
*   **`letterSpacing`**: Accepts number variables.
*   **`paragraphSpacing`**: Accepts number variables. Not valid for substrings.
*   **`paragraphIndent`**: Accepts number variables. Not valid for substrings.

**Apply typography variables**

To apply typography variables to a `TextNode`, use the `setBoundVariable` method as you would with [other variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#bound-variables)
.

TextNode.setBoundVariable example

    const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("example-collection")const weightVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("weight-variable", collection, "FLOAT")const exampleTextNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")// Typography variables can be bound using setBoundVariableexampleTextNode.setBoundVariable("fontWeight", weightVariable)

To apply typography variables to a substring, use the `setRangeBoundVariable` method that is available for `TextNodes`.

TextNode.setRangeBoundVariable example

    const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("example-collection")const weightVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("weight-variable", collection, "FLOAT")const exampleTextNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")// Typography variables can be bound to ranges of text with setRangeBoundVariableexampleTextNode.setRangeBoundVariable(0, 5, "fontWeight", weightVariable)

To apply typography variables to a `TextStyle`, use the `setBoundVariable` method, similar to how you bind typography variables to `TextNodes`.

TextStyle.setBoundVariable example

    const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("example-collection")const weightVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("weight-variable", collection, "FLOAT")// Uses the first available local style as an exampleconst localTextStyles = await figma.getLocalTextStylesAsync()const exampleTextStyle = localTextStyles[0]// Typography variables can be bound using setBoundVariableexampleTextStyle.setBoundVariable("fontWeight", weightVariable)

**Get typography variables**

To get the aliases for typography variables that are bound to a `TextNode`, use the `boundVariables` property of the `TextNode`.

Get bound typography variables example

    const exampleTextNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")const exampleTextNodeVariables = exampleTextNode.boundVariables

To get the typography variables for segments of text, use the `TextNode.getStyledTextSegments` method with `boundVariables`.

TextNode.getStyledTextSegments example

    const exampleTextNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")exampleTextNode.getStyledTextSegments(['boundVariables'])/* Example output[  {    characters: "hello",    start: 0,    end: 5,    boundVariables: {      fontFamily: { type: "VARIABLE_ALIAS", id: "VariableID:1:7" }    }  },  {    characters: " world",    start: 5,    end: 11,    boundVariables: {      fontFamily: { type: "VARIABLE_ALIAS", id: "VariableID:2:8" }    }  }]*/

You can also use the `TextNode.getRangeBoundVariable` method to get a specific bound variable based on the character range and field (`fontWeight`, for example).

TextNode.getRangeBoundVariable example

    const exampleTextNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")// Get typography variables that are bound to ranges of text with getRangeBoundVariableexampleTextNode.getRangeBoundVariable(0, 5, "fontWeight")

To get typography variables that are bound to `TextStyles`, use the `boundVariables` property of the `TextStyle`, similar to `TextNodes`.

Get bound typography variables for TextStyle example

    // Uses the first available local style as an exampleconst localTextStyles = await figma.getLocalTextStylesAsync()const exampleTextStyle = localTextStyles[0]const exampleTextStyleVariables = exampleTextStyle.boundVariables

**Remove typography variables**

To unbind or remove a typography variable, set the bound field to `null`.

Unbind a typography variable example

    const exampleTextNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync("1:4")// Simple fields can be unbound using setBoundVariable and nullexampleTextNode.setBoundVariable("fontWeight", null)

To learn more about binding typography variables, check out the [code samples](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#samples)
.

* * *

Helpers[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#helpers "Direct link to Helpers")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `figma.variables` global object also provides some helper methods to make certain operations easier.

### [createVariableAlias](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#createvariablealias)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#createvariablealias "Direct link to createvariablealias")

The `figma.variables.createVariableAlias` method is a helper function to create a variable alias. Variable aliases are used to bind variables to other variables, or to bind variables to various properties on a node or style.

### [createVariableAliasByIdAsync(variableId)](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#createvariablealiasbyidasync)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#createvariablealiasbyidasyncvariableid "Direct link to createvariablealiasbyidasyncvariableid")

The `figma.variables.createVariableAliasByIdAsync(variableId)` method is a helper function that accepts a variable ID and returns an alias that references that variable.

### [setBoundVariableForPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#setboundvariableforpaint)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#setboundvariableforpaint "Direct link to setboundvariableforpaint")

The `figma.variables.setBoundVariableForPaint` method is a helper function to bind a variable to a SolidPaint.

### [setBoundVariableForEffect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#setboundvariableforeffect)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#setboundvariableforeffect "Direct link to setboundvariableforeffect")

The `figma.variables.setBoundVariableForEffect` method is a helper function to bind a variable to an Effect.

### [setBoundVariableForLayoutGrid](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#setboundvariableforlayoutgrid)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#setboundvariableforlayoutgrid "Direct link to setboundvariableforlayoutgrid")

The `figma.variables.setBoundVariableForLayoutGrid` method is a helper function to bind a variable to a LayoutGrid.

### [importVariableByKeyAsync](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/figma-variables/#importvariablebykeyasync)
[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#importvariablebykeyasync "Direct link to importvariablebykeyasync")

The `figma.variables.importVariableByKeyAsync` method loads a variable from the team library.

* * *

Samples[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#samples "Direct link to Samples")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This section contains some functional plugin samples that use variables.

### Example: Authoring a new variable collection[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-authoring-a-new-variable-collection "Direct link to Example: Authoring a new variable collection")

This example creates a new variable collection with two modes, and a color variable that defines a value for each of those two modes.

authoring.js

    const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("new-collection")collection.renameMode(collection.modes[0].modeId, "light")const colorVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("color-variable", collection, "COLOR")// rename our new variable and collection because naming is hard!colorVariable.name = "text-primary"collection.name = "semantic colors"const lightModeId = collection.modes[0].modeIdconst darkModeId = collection.addMode("dark")// Sets the color to #000 in light mode and #fff in dark modecolorVariable.setValueForMode(lightModeId, {r: 0, g: 0, b: 0})colorVariable.setValueForMode(darkModeId, {r: 1, g: 1, b: 1})

### Example: Determining if a node is bound to a variable[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-determining-if-a-node-is-bound-to-a-variable "Direct link to Example: Determining if a node is bound to a variable")

Variables consumed by a given node are enumerated in the `boundVariables` map.

detect-if-bound.js

    const rectangle = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync(rectangleId)// rectangle.width is a numberif (rectangle.boundVariables['width'] !== undefined) {  // Width fields can only have one bound variable, but fills and strokes might have multiple.  const widthVariableId = rectangle.boundVariables['width'].id  const widthVariable = await figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync(widthVariableId)  // At this point we know that `widthVariable` is bound to the `width` property of `rectangle`.  assert(widthVariable.resolveForConsumer(rectangle).value === rectangle.width)}

### Example: Binding a variable to a node or style[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-binding-a-variable-to-a-node-or-style "Direct link to Example: Binding a variable to a node or style")

Whether binding to a node or style, the procedure is roughly the same.

binding.js

    const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("new-collection")const widthVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("width-variable", collection, "FLOAT")const colorVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("color-variable", collection, "COLOR")const node = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync(...)// Simple fields can be bound via setBoundVariablenode.setBoundVariable('width', widthVariable)const fillsCopy = clone(node.fills)// Fills and strokes must be set via their immutable arraysfillsCopy[0] = figma.variables.setBoundVariableForPaint(fillsCopy[0], 'color', colorVariable)node.fills = fillsCopyconst style = await figma.getStyleByIdAsync(...)const paintsCopy = clone(style.paints)paintsCopy[0] = figma.variables.setBoundVariableForPaint(paintsCopy[0], 'color', colorVariable)style.paints = paintsCopy// Effects and LayoutGrids must also be set by their immutable arraysconst radiusVariable = figma.variables.createVariable("radius-variable", collection, "FLOAT")const effectsCopy = clone(node.effects)effectsCopy[0] = figma.variables.setBoundVariableForEffect(effectsCopy[0], 'radius', radiusVariable)node.effects = effectsCopyconst countVar = figma.variables.createVariable("count-variable", collection, "FLOAT")const layoutGridsCopy = clone(node.layoutGrids)layoutGridsCopy[0] = figma.variables.setBoundVariableForLayoutGrid(layoutGridsCopy[0], 'count', countVar)node.layoutGrids = layoutGridsCopy

### Example: Binding a variable to a variant property[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-binding-a-variable-to-a-variant-property "Direct link to Example: Binding a variable to a variant property")

This sample binds a string variable to the variant property on a [`InstanceNode`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/InstanceNode/)

code.js

    const instanceNode = await figma.getNodeByIdAsync(...);const stringVariable = await figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync(...);node.setProperties({  "variantPropName":    figma.variables.createVariableAlias(stringVariable)})// To clearnode.setProperties({  "variantPropName": "Default", // or other string value})

### Example: Binding a variable to a text node[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-binding-a-variable-to-a-text-node "Direct link to Example: Binding a variable to a text node")

This sample binds a font family variable to text node.

code.js

    (async () => {  // Setup variables  const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("new-collection")  const modeId = collection.modes[0].modeId  const fontFamilyVar = figma.variables.createVariable("fontFamily", collection, "STRING")  fontFamilyVar.setValueForMode(modeId, "Roboto")  // Load necessary fonts  await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: "Inter", style: "Regular" })  // current font  await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: "Roboto", style: "Regular" }) // new font  // Create text node  const text = figma.createText()  text.characters = "Hello world!"  // Apply variables  text.setBoundVariable("fontFamily", fontFamilyVar)})()

### Example: Binding a variable to a text style[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-binding-a-variable-to-a-text-style "Direct link to Example: Binding a variable to a text style")

This sample binds a font family variable to a text style.

code.js

    (async () => {  // Setup variables  const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("new-collection")  const modeId = collection.modes[0].modeId  const fontFamilyVar = figma.variables.createVariable("fontFamily", collection, "STRING")  fontFamilyVar.setValueForMode(modeId, "Roboto")  // Load necessary fonts  await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: "Inter", style: "Regular" })  // current font  await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: "Roboto", style: "Regular" }) // new font  // Create style  const textStyle = figma.createTextStyle()  // Create text node  const text = figma.createText()  text.characters = "Hello world!"  text.setTextStyleIdAsync(textStyle.id)  // Apply variables  textStyle.setBoundVariable("fontFamily", fontFamilyVar)  console.log(textStyle.boundVariables)  // Output:  // {  //   fontFamily: [{  //     type: "VARIABLE_ALIAS",  //     variableId: fontFamilyVar.id  //   }]  // }})()

### Example: Binding a variable to a text substring[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-binding-a-variable-to-a-text-substring "Direct link to Example: Binding a variable to a text substring")

This example binds font family and font style variables to a substring of text.

code.js

    (async () => {  // Setup variables  const collection = figma.variables.createVariableCollection("new-collection")  const modeId = collection.modes[0].modeId  const fontStyleVar = figma.variables.createVariable("fontFamily", collection, "STRING")  fontStyleVar.setValueForMode(modeId, "Bold")  // Load necessary fonts  await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: "Inter", style: "Regular" }) // current font  await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: "Inter", style: "Bold" })    // new font  // Create text node  const text = figma.createText()  text.characters = "Hello world!"  // Apply variables  text.setRangeBoundVariable(0, 5, "fontStyle", fontStyleVar)  console.log(text.getRangeBoundVariable(0, 5, "fontStyle"))  // Output:  // {  //   type: "VARIABLE_ALIAS",  //   variableId: fontStyleVar.id  // }  console.log(text.getRangeBoundVariable(0, 6, "fontStyle"))  // Output:  // Symbol(figma.mixed)  console.log(text.getStyledTextSegments(["boundVariables", "fontName"]))  // Output:  // [  //   {  //     characters: "Hello",  //     start: 0,  //     end: 5,  //     fontName: { family: "Inter", style: "Bold" },  //     boundVariables: {  //       fontStyle: { type: "Variable_ALIAS", variableId: fontStyleVar.id }  //     }  //   },  //   {  //     characters: " world!",  //     start: 5,  //     end: 12,  //     fontName: { family: "Inter", style: "Regular" },  //     boundVariables: {}  //   }  // ]})()

### Example: Extracting variables from fill styles[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-extracting-variables-from-fill-styles "Direct link to Example: Extracting variables from fill styles")

Sample code to convert existing styles into variables is available on our [GitHub](https://github.com/figma/plugin-samples/tree/master/styles-to-variables)
.

### Example: Import and export variables[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/working-with-variables/#example-import-and-export-variables "Direct link to Example: Import and export variables")

Sample code to help import design tokens from other products or export variables into JSON format. This can further help with migration to variables or syncing design with development. Note that this sample only handles basic import/export and developers are encouraged to extend and customize this code to suit their specific needs.

[Sample code on our GitHub](https://github.com/figma/plugin-samples/tree/master/variables-import-export)

# Global properties | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/files/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Every file in Figma consists of a tree of nodes. At the root of every file is a `DOCUMENT` node, and from that node stems any `CANVAS` nodes. Every canvas node represents a `PAGE` in a Figma file. A canvas node can then have any number of nodes as its children. Each subtree stemming from a canvas node will represent a layer (e.g an object) on the canvas.

Nodes have a number of properties associated with them. Some of these are **global properties**, that exist on every node, whereas other **node properties** will be specific to the type of node.

Global properties[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/files/#global-properties "Direct link to Global properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following are properties that exist on every node. These give us some basic information about identifying and viewing the node.

| Node type | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### Node[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/files/#node-type "Direct link to Node") | `id`String<br><br>A string uniquely identifying this node within the document.<br><br>`name`String<br><br>The name given to the node by the user in the tool.<br><br>`visible`Booleandefault: true<br><br>Whether or not the node is visible on the canvas.<br><br>`type`String<br><br>The type of the node; refer to table below for details.<br><br>`rotation`Number<br><br>The rotation of the node, if not 0.<br><br>`pluginData`Any<br><br>Data written by plugins that is visible only to the plugin that wrote it. Requires the `pluginData` to include the ID of the plugin.<br><br>`sharedPluginData`Any<br><br>Data written by plugins that is visible to all plugins. Requires the `pluginData` parameter to include the string "shared".<br><br>`componentPropertyReferences`Map<String, String><br><br>A mapping of a layer's property to component property name of component properties attached to this node. The component property name can be used to look up more information on the corresponding component's or component set's componentPropertyDefinitions.<br><br>`boundVariables`Map<String, [VariableAlias](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablealias-type)<br> \| [VariableAlias](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablealias-type)<br>\[\] \| Map<String, [VariableAlias](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablealias-type)<br>\>><br><br>A mapping of field to the variables applied to this field. Most fields will only map to a single [VariableAlias](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablealias-type)<br>. However, for fills, strokes, size, and component properties, it is possible to have multiple variables bound to the field.<br><br>`explicitVariableModes`Map<String, String><br><br>A mapping of variable collection ID to mode ID representing the explicitly set modes for this node. |

*   [Global properties](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/files/#global-properties)
    *   [Node](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/files/#node-type)

# Introduction | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Welcome to Figma, the world's first collaborative interface design tool. Figma allows designers to create and prototype their digital experiences - together in real-time and in one place - helping them turn their ideas and visions into products, faster. Figma's mission is to make design accessible to everyone. The Figma API is one of the ways we aim to do that.

What can I do with the Figma API?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#what-can-i-do-with-the-figma-api "Direct link to What can I do with the Figma API?")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Figma API supports read access and interactions with Figma files. This gives you the ability to view and extract any objects or layers, and their properties, so you can render them as images outside of Figma. You can then present your designs, connect them to other applications, or use them to expand on your vision. Future versions of the API will unlock even greater functionality around Files.

How does it work?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#how-does-it-work "Direct link to How does it work?")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Figma API is based on the [REST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Representational_state_transfer)
 structure. We support authentication via [access tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#access-tokens)
 and [OAuth2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#oauth2)
. Requests are made via **HTTP endpoints** with clear functions and appropriate response codes. Endpoints allow you to request [files](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/)
, [images](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-images-endpoint)
, [file versions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/version-history-endpoints/)
, [users](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-endpoints/)
, [comments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/)
, [team projects](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#get-team-projects-endpoint)
 and [project files](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#get-project-files-endpoint)
.

Once granted access, you can use the Figma API to inspect a **JSON** representation of the file. Every layer or object in a file will be represented within the file by a node (subtree). You will then be able to access and isolate the object and any properties associated with it. In addition to accessing files and layers, you will be able to GET and POST comments to files.

Getting started[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#getting-started "Direct link to Getting started")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you’re not already using Figma, the first step is to sign up and [create an account](https://www.figma.com/signup)
.

Once you have a Figma account, the next step is to authenticate with the API. This can be done using either [OAuth2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#oauth2)
 or [access tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#access-tokens)
.

You can then browse our endpoints and start making queries against the Figma API. We recommend starting with the basics by learning about [Figma files](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/)
, before moving on to more advanced topics such as comments, users, version history, and projects.

If you plan on building a fully-fledged app, that others can share and use, then you can register your app by heading to [My apps](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
 in your Figma account.

OpenAPI specification[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#openapi-specification "Direct link to OpenAPI specification")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Figma REST API is fully described in an OpenAPI specification in the open source [figma/rest-api-spec](https://github.com/figma/rest-api-spec)
 repository.

OpenAPI is a specification for describing HTTP APIs in a language-agnostic manner. It has a large ecosystem of tools to let you generate API documentation, client SDKs, and more. We also provide custom Typescript types generated from the OpenAPI specification for those of you with Typescript codebases to make it easy to write type-safe code out of the box. For more information, see the [README](https://github.com/figma/rest-api-spec)
.

*   [What can I do with the Figma API?](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#what-can-i-do-with-the-figma-api)
    
*   [How does it work?](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#how-does-it-work)
    
*   [Getting started](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#getting-started)
    
*   [OpenAPI specification](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/#openapi-specification)

# Action types | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Below are the action types you can find under the `action` field in the API response:

| Action name | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### ai\_content\_training\_disable[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ai_content_training_disable "Direct link to ai_content_training_disable")<br><br>AI content training was disabled for the organization. |     |
| ### ai\_content\_training\_enable[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ai_content_training_enable "Direct link to ai_content_training_enable")<br><br>AI content training was enabled for the organization. |     |
| ### ai\_features\_disable[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ai_features_disable "Direct link to ai_features_disable")<br><br>AI features were disabled for the organization. |     |
| ### ai\_features\_enable[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ai_features_enable "Direct link to ai_features_enable")<br><br>AI features were enabled for the organization. |     |
| ### autogen\_password\_controls\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#autogen_password_controls_setting_change "Direct link to autogen_password_controls_setting_change")<br><br>Choose what type of passwords can be added to files -- autogenerated only or custom passwords. | `old_autogen_password_controls_setting`Boolean<br><br>The old autogen password enforcement setting<br><br>`new_autogen_password_controls_setting`Boolean<br><br>The new autogen password enforcement setting |
| ### branch\_archive[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#branch_archive "Direct link to branch_archive")<br><br>A particular branch within a file was archived. | `main_file_key`String<br><br>Key of the file that the branch was created from. |
| ### branch\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#branch_create "Direct link to branch_create")<br><br>A new branch was created within a file. | `main_file_key`String<br><br>Key of the file that the branch was created from. |
| ### branch\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#branch_delete "Direct link to branch_delete")<br><br>A particular branch within a file was deleted. | `main_file_key`String<br><br>Key of the file that the branch was deleted from. |
| ### branch\_member\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#branch_member_add "Direct link to branch_member_add")<br><br>A user was invited to a branch. | `resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. Can only be `branch` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The ID of the branch.<br><br>`permission`String<br><br>The permission level for the user on the branch. |
| ### branch\_member\_permission\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#branch_member_permission_change "Direct link to branch_member_permission_change")<br><br>A user's permission level in a branch changed. | `resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. Can only be `branch` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The ID of the branch.<br><br>`old_permission`String<br><br>The old permission level for the user on the branch before the change.<br><br>`new_permission`String<br><br>The new permission level for the user on the branch after the change. |
| ### branch\_member\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#branch_member_remove "Direct link to branch_member_remove")<br><br>A user was removed from a branch. | `permission`String<br><br>The permission level for the user on the file.<br><br>`resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. Can only be `branch` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The ID of the branch. |
| ### branch\_unarchive[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#branch_unarchive "Direct link to branch_unarchive")<br><br>A particular branch within a file was restored from archive. | `main_file_key`String<br><br>Key of the file that the branch was created from. |
| ### buzz\_unsplash\_disabled\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#buzz_unsplash_disabled_change "Direct link to buzz_unsplash_disabled_change")<br><br>Unsplash in Buzz setting was changed for the organization. | `old_buzz_unsplash_disabled`Boolean<br><br>Whether Unsplash in Buzz was disabled (false = enabled, true = disabled).<br><br>`new_buzz_unsplash_disabled`Boolean<br><br>Whether Unsplash in Buzz is disabled after the change (false = enabled, true = disabled). |

| ### community\_plugin\_update[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#community_plugin_update "Direct link to community_plugin_update")<br><br>A specific plugin published to the Figma Community was updated. |     |

| ### community\_widget\_publish[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#community_widget_publish "Direct link to community_widget_publish")<br><br>A specific widget was published to the Figma Community. |     |
| ### community\_widget\_update[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#community_widget_update "Direct link to community_widget_update")<br><br>A specific widget published to the Figma Community was updated. |     |
| ### configurable\_upgrade\_request\_message\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#configurable_upgrade_request_message_change "Direct link to configurable_upgrade_request_message_change")<br><br>The message for configured upgrade request flow was changed. | `old_configured_upgrade_request_message`String<br><br>Old message sent to users in the configured upgrade request flow.<br><br>`new_configured_upgrade_request_message`String<br><br>New message sent to users in the configured upgrade request flow. |

| ### fig\_file\_link\_access\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_link_access_change "Direct link to fig_file_link_access_change")<br><br>The permissions for a Figma or FigJam file changed. | `old_link_access`String<br><br>The link access policy for the file before the change. Can be `edit`, `view`, `org_edit`, `org_view` or `inherit`.<br><br>`new_link_access`String<br><br>The link access policy for the file after the change.<br><br>`old_org_browsable`Boolean<br><br>The org access policy for the file before the change.<br><br>`new_org_browsable`Boolean<br><br>The org access policy for the file after the change. |
| ### fig\_file\_link\_expiration\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_link_expiration_change "Direct link to fig_file_link_expiration_change")<br><br>Expiration was changed or added for a Figma file's public link access. | `old_expires_at`String<br><br>Timestamp when public link access expires and the file's link access is returned to a private setting. Timestamp is before the expiration change.<br><br>`new_expires_at`String<br><br>Timestamp when public link access expires and the file's link access is returned to a private setting. Timestamp is after the expiration change. |
| ### fig\_file\_member\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_member_add "Direct link to fig_file_member_add")<br><br>A user was invited to a Figma or FigJam file. | `resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. | ### fig\_file\_member\_permission\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_member_permission_change "Direct link to fig_file_member_permission_change")<br><br>A user's permission level in a Figma or FigJam file changed. | `resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. Can only be `file` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The key of the file.<br><br>`old_permission`String<br><br>The old permission level for the user on the file before the change.<br><br>`new_permission`String<br><br>The new permission level for the user on the file after the change. |
| ### fig\_file\_member\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_member_remove "Direct link to fig_file_member_remove")<br><br>A user was removed from a Figma or FigJam file. | `permission`String<br><br>The permission level for the user on the file.<br><br>`resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. | ### fig\_file\_view[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_view "Direct link to fig_file_view")<br><br>A Figma or FigJam file was viewed. | `access`String<br><br>The viewer's access to the file. Can be `edit`, `view` or `view_prototypes`. |
| ### fig\_file\_view\_external[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_view_external "Direct link to fig_file_view_external")<br><br>A Figma or FigJam file external to the organization was viewed. Only logged for Enterprise organizations with Domain Capture enabled. | `org_name`String<br><br>The name of the external org that the user viewed a file in, or `null` if it is not part of an org.<br><br>`team_name`String<br><br>The name of the external team that the user viewed a file in, or `null` if it is part of an org or another user's drafts. |
| ### fig\_file\_view\_prototype[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_view_prototype "Direct link to fig_file_view_prototype")<br><br>A Figma or FigJam file prototype was viewed. |     |
| ### fig\_file\_view\_prototype\_external[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_view_prototype_external "Direct link to fig_file_view_prototype_external")<br><br>A Figma or FigJam file prototype external to the organization was viewed. Only logged for Enterprise organizations with Domain Capture enabled. | `org_name`String<br><br>The name of the external org that the user viewed a prototype in, or `null` if it is not part of an org.<br><br>`team_name`String<br><br>The name of the external team that the user viewed a prototype in, or `null` if it is part of an org or another user's drafts. |
| ### fig\_file\_viewer\_access\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#fig_file_viewer_access_change "Direct link to fig_file_viewer_access_change")<br><br>A change was made to permissions that allows/disallows viewers to copy, share or export a particular file. | `viewer_export_restricted`Boolean<br><br>If viewers are disallowed to copy, share or export the file. |

| ### idp\_config\_update[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#idp_config_update "Direct link to idp_config_update")<br><br>An IdP configuration was updated. | `sp_tenant_id`String<br><br>The tenant id of the idp configuration.<br><br>`idp_name`String<br><br>The name of the idp configuration.<br><br>`idp_entity_id`String<br><br>The entity id of the idp configuration. |
| ### idp\_group\_connection\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#idp_group_connection_add "Direct link to idp_group_connection_add")<br><br>A workspace or billing group was added to an idp group. | `connection_type`String<br><br>The type e.g workspace or billing group that was added to the idp group.<br><br>`connection_name`String<br><br>The workspace or billing group name that was added to the idp group.<br><br>`idp_group_name`String<br><br>The name of the idp group the connection was added to. |

| ### idp\_user\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#idp_user_create "Direct link to idp_user_create")<br><br>A new user was added to an organization via SCIM. | `user_name`String<br><br>The username of the idp user that was created. | ### idp\_user\_deactivate[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#idp_user_deactivate "Direct link to idp_user_deactivate")<br><br>An org user was deactivated via SCIM. | `user_name`String<br><br>The username of the idp user that was deactivated. | ### idp\_user\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#idp_user_delete "Direct link to idp_user_delete")<br><br>A user was deleted from an organization via SCIM. | `user_name`String<br><br>The username of the idp user that was deleted. This is the email address.<br><br>`display_name`String<br><br>The display name of the idp user that was deleted. |
| ### idp\_user\_update[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#idp_user_update "Direct link to idp_user_update")<br><br>An org user's paid status was updated via SCIM. | `user_name`String<br><br>The username of the idp user that was updated. This is the email address.<br><br>`display_name`String<br><br>The display name of the idp user that was updated.<br><br>`old_figma_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's Figma Design seat before the change.<br><br>`new_figma_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's Figma Design seat after the change.<br><br>`old_figjam_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's FigJam seat after the change.<br><br>`new_figjam_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's FigJam seat after the change. |
| ### ip\_allowlist\_range\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ip_allowlist_range_create "Direct link to ip_allowlist_range_create")<br><br>An IP address was added to the allowlist of networks which Figma can be accessed on. | `ip_address`String<br><br>The IP range that was allowlisted. |
| ### ip\_allowlist\_range\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ip_allowlist_range_delete "Direct link to ip_allowlist_range_delete")<br><br>An IP address was removed from the allowlist of networks which Figma can be accessed on. | `ip_address`String<br><br>The IP range that was removed from the allowlist |
| ### ip\_allowlist\_rejected[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ip_allowlist_rejected "Direct link to ip_allowlist_rejected")<br><br>A user was rejected from accessing Figma due to an IP address not being in the allowlist. |     |
| ### ip\_allowlist\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ip_allowlist_setting_change "Direct link to ip_allowlist_setting_change")<br><br>Blocking access to Figma on non-corporate networks was enabled or disabled. | `ip_allowlist_setting`Boolean<br><br>Whether or not the org has the IP allowlist feature enabled. |
| ### ip\_restriction\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#ip_restriction_change "Direct link to ip_restriction_change")<br><br>Limiting personal accounts on the corporate network was enabled or disabled. | `ip_restriction_setting`Boolean<br><br>If orgs are limiting or not limiting personal accounts on the corporate network. |
| ### license\_group\_admins\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_admins_add "Direct link to license_group_admins_add")<br><br>A new billing group admin was added to the organization's billing group. Note: due to a data structure change, this action may also include legacy workspace events before mid-December 2023. | `new_admin_emails`String\[\]<br><br>The emails of the users added to the billing group as admins. |
| ### license\_group\_admins\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_admins_remove "Direct link to license_group_admins_remove")<br><br>A billing group admin was removed from the organization's billing group. Note: due to a data structure change, this action may also include legacy workspace events before mid-December 2023. | `removed_admin_emails`String\[\]<br><br>The emails of the admins removed from the billing group. |
| ### license\_group\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_create "Direct link to license_group_create")<br><br>A new billing group was created in the organization. Note: due to a data structure change, this action may also include legacy workspace events before mid-December 2023. | `admin_emails`String\[\]<br><br>The emails of the users added to the billing group as admins. |
| ### license\_group\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_delete "Direct link to license_group_delete")<br><br>A billing group was deleted from the organization. Note: due to a data structure change, this action may also include legacy workspace events before mid-December 2023. |     |
| ### license\_group\_library\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_library_setting_change "Direct link to license_group_library_setting_change")<br><br>A legacy workspace changed a library's settings. Note: due to a data structure change, this action does not include new workspace events. See [workspace\_library\_setting\_change](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_library_setting_change)<br>. | `library_file_key`String<br><br>File key of the library the workspace is changing the setting for.<br><br>`library_file_name`String<br><br>File name of the library the workspace is changing the setting for.<br><br>`license_group_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace that is changing a library setting.<br><br>`workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace that is changing a library setting.<br><br>`old_figma_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library was previously enabled in the workspace for Figma design files.<br><br>`old_figjam_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library was previously enabled in the workspace for FigJam files.<br><br>`old_use_org_settings`Boolean<br><br>Library's previous setting on the workspace was inherited from the org.<br><br>`new_figma_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library is now enabled in the workspace for Figma design files.<br><br>`new_figjam_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library is now enabled in the workspace for FigJam files.<br><br>`new_use_org_settings`Boolean<br><br>Library's new setting on the workspace will be inherited from the org. |
| ### license\_group\_membership\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_membership_change "Direct link to license_group_membership_change")<br><br>A user was moved to a new billing group in the organization. Note: due to a data structure change, this action may also include legacy workspace events before mid-December 2023. | `old_license_group_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the user's old billing group before it was changed.<br><br>`new_license_group_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the user's new billing group after it was changed.<br><br>`new_idp_group_id`String<br><br>The ID of the idp group linked to the new billing group<br><br>`old_idp_group_id`String<br><br>The ID of the idp group linked to the old billing group |
| ### license\_group\_membership\_self\_select[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_membership_self_select "Direct link to license_group_membership_self_select")<br><br>A user chose to join a particular workspace in the organization. Note: due to a data structure change, this action does not include new workspace events. See [workspace\_membership\_self\_select](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_membership_self_select)<br> for newer events. | `license_group_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace that the user selects when they join the org. |
| ### license\_group\_rename[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_rename "Direct link to license_group_rename")<br><br>A billing group was renamed. Note: due to a data structure change, this action may also include legacy workspace events before mid-December 2023. | `old_name`String<br><br>The name of the billing group before it was renamed.<br><br>`new_name`String<br><br>The name of the billing group after it was renamed. |
| ### mfa\_required\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#mfa_required_setting_change "Direct link to mfa_required_setting_change")<br><br>An org admin changed the setting for requiring multi-factor authentication for users. | `old_mfa_required_setting`String<br><br>The old MFA required setting.<br><br>`new_mfa_required_setting`String<br><br>The new MFA required setting. |
| ### network\_access\_restriction\_range\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#network_access_restriction_range_create "Direct link to network_access_restriction_range_create")<br><br>An IP address was added to the list of corporate networks that prevent access to personal Figma accounts. | `ip_address`String<br><br>The IP range that was added for network access restrictions. |
| ### network\_access\_restriction\_range\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#network_access_restriction_range_delete "Direct link to network_access_restriction_range_delete")<br><br>An IP address was removed from the list of corporate networks that prevent access to personal Figma accounts. | `ip_address`String<br><br>The IP range that was removed from network access restrictions. |
| ### network\_access\_restriction\_range\_update[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#network_access_restriction_range_update "Direct link to network_access_restriction_range_update")<br><br>An IP address was enabled or disabled for preventing access to personal Figma accounts. | `ip_address`String<br><br>The IP range that was enabled or disabled for network access restrictions.<br><br>`enabled`Boolean<br><br>Whether the IP range for network access restriction was enabled or disabled. |
| ### oauth\_token\_grant[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#oauth_token_grant "Direct link to oauth_token_grant")<br><br>A user granted a new OAuth token to a Figma application. | `client_id`String<br><br>The ID of the app that this token is granted to.<br><br>`client_name`String<br><br>The name of the app that this token is granted to.<br><br>`is_org_level_token`Boolean<br><br>If the token is granted on behalf of an org instead of a user.<br><br>`scope`String<br><br>The type of access that this token has. |
| ### oauth\_token\_revoke[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#oauth_token_revoke "Direct link to oauth_token_revoke")<br><br>A user revoked an OAuth token previously granted to a Figma application. | `client_id`String<br><br>The ID of the app that owns the token.<br><br>`client_name`String<br><br>The name of the app that owns the token.<br><br>`is_org_level_token`Boolean<br><br>If the token is granted on behalf of an org instead of a user. |
| ### open\_sessions\_end[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#open_sessions_end "Direct link to open_sessions_end")<br><br>An Open Session was ended manually. |     |
| ### open\_sessions\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#open_sessions_setting_change "Direct link to open_sessions_setting_change")<br><br>The settings for open sessions were turned on or off for an organization. | `workshop_disabled`Boolean<br><br>If open session is disabled for the org. |
| ### open\_sessions\_start[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#open_sessions_start "Direct link to open_sessions_start")<br><br>An Open Session was started. |     |
| ### org\_default\_license\_type\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_default_license_type_change "Direct link to org_default_license_type_change")<br><br>The settings for default roles changed for Figma design or FigJam files. | `editor_type`String<br><br>The product type whose default paid status changes. Can be `figma`, `figjam`, or `dev_mode`.<br><br>`old_paid_status`String<br><br>The default paid status of the product before the change. Can be `editor` for a full seat, `viewer`, or `viewer_restricted`.<br><br>`new_paid_status`String<br><br>The default paid status of the product after the change. |
| ### org\_domain\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_domain_add "Direct link to org_domain_add")<br><br>A domain was added to an organization. | `domain`String<br><br>The domain that was added to the org. |
| ### org\_domain\_capture\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_domain_capture_change "Direct link to org_domain_capture_change")<br><br>Domain capture was updated in an organization. | `domain_capture`Boolean<br><br>Domain capture enabled in org. |
| ### org\_domain\_idp\_mapping\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_domain_idp_mapping_change "Direct link to org_domain_idp_mapping_change")<br><br>An IdP mapping was changed for a domain. | `domain`String<br><br>The domain that was mapped to the idp configuration<br><br>`old_idp_mapping`String<br><br>The idp mapping that was changed from<br><br>`new_idp_mapping`String<br><br>The idp mapping that was changed to |
| ### org\_domain\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_domain_remove "Direct link to org_domain_remove")<br><br>A domain was removed from an organization. | `domain`String<br><br>The domain that was removed from the org. |
| ### org\_domain\_verify[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_domain_verify "Direct link to org_domain_verify")<br><br>A domain was verified in an organization. | `domain`String<br><br>The domain that was verified within the org. |
| ### org\_guest\_invite\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_guest_invite_setting_change "Direct link to org_guest_invite_setting_change")<br><br>The settings for org guest invite policy changed. | `old_guest_invite_setting`String<br><br>Org guest setting before the change. Can be `banned`, `require_approval` or `null`.<br><br>`new_guest_invite_setting`String<br><br>Org guest setting after the change. |
| ### org\_invite\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_invite_create "Direct link to org_invite_create")<br><br>A user was invited to join a particular organization. | `invitee_email`String<br><br>Email of the user invited to the org |
| ### org\_join\_request\_approve[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_join_request_approve "Direct link to org_join_request_approve")<br><br>An admin approved a user's request to join an organization in order to access a particular file. | `resource_type`String<br><br>Type of the resource that the user can view once they join the org.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>ID of the resource that the user can view once they join the org. |
| ### org\_join\_request\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_join_request_create "Direct link to org_join_request_create")<br><br>A user requested to join an organization so they can access a particular file. | `resource_type`String<br><br>Type of the resource that the user requests to join the org to view.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>ID of the resource that the user requests to join the org to view. |

| ### org\_library\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_library_setting_change "Direct link to org_library_setting_change")<br><br>The org changed an enabled library's settings. | `org_name`String<br><br>The name of the Org that is changing a library setting.<br><br>`library_file_key`String<br><br>File key of the library the org is changing the setting for.<br><br>`library_file_name`String<br><br>File name of the library the org is changing the setting for.<br><br>`old_figma_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library was previously enabled in the org for Figma design files<br><br>`old_figjam_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library was previously enabled in the org for FigJam files<br><br>`new_figma_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library is now enabled in the org for Figma design files<br><br>`new_figjam_enabled`Boolean<br><br>Library is now enabled in the org for FigJam files |
| ### org\_library\_unapprove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_library_unapprove "Direct link to org_library_unapprove")<br><br>A library is marked as not approved for an organization. |     |
| ### org\_merge[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_merge "Direct link to org_merge")<br><br>An org was converted into a team under another org. | `child_org_id`Number<br><br>ID of the org that gets merged into the target org |
| ### org\_team\_creation\_controls[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_team_creation_controls "Direct link to org_team_creation_controls")<br><br>An org's setting for who can create teams was changed. | `new_team_creation_controls_setting`String<br><br>The new setting of team creation controls setting<br><br>`old_team_creation_controls_setting`String<br><br>The old setting of team creation controls setting |
| ### org\_team\_csv\_exported[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_team_csv_exported "Direct link to org_team_csv_exported")<br><br>The teams list of an organization was exported as a CSV file. |     |
| ### org\_user\_account\_type\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_account_type_change "Direct link to org_user_account_type_change")<br><br>A user's paid status for a product was changed. | `editor_type`String<br><br>The product type whose default paid status changes. Can be `figma`, `figjam`, or `dev_mode`.<br><br>`old_figma_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's Figma Design seat before the change.<br><br>`new_figma_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's Figma Design seat after the change.<br><br>`old_figjam_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's FigJam seat after the change.<br><br>`new_figjam_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's FigJam seat after the change.<br><br>`old_seat_type`String<br><br>The seat type the user was previously occupying.<br><br>`new_seat_type`String<br><br>The seat type the user now occupies. |
| ### org\_user\_account\_type\_change\_to\_full[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_account_type_change_to_full "Direct link to org_user_account_type_change_to_full")<br><br>A user was upgraded to the editor role in an org. | `editor_type`String<br><br>The product type whose default paid status changes. Can be `figma`, `figjam`, or `dev_mode`.<br><br>`reason`String<br><br>The triggering condition that upgrades the user to a paid seat.<br><br>`old_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's seat before the change. |
| ### org\_user\_account\_type\_upgrade\_approved[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_account_type_upgrade_approved "Direct link to org_user_account_type_upgrade_approved")<br><br>An admin approved a request for someone to upgrade to an editor role. | `editor_type`String<br><br>The product type whose default paid status changes. Can be `figma`, `figjam`, or `dev_mode`.<br><br>`seat_type`String<br><br>The seat type that the user had requested and was now approved for. Can be `Full`, `Collab` or `Dev`. |
| ### org\_user\_account\_type\_upgrade\_denied[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_account_type_upgrade_denied "Direct link to org_user_account_type_upgrade_denied")<br><br>An admin denied a request for someone to upgrade to an editor role. | `editor_type`String<br><br>The product type whose default paid status changes. Can be `figma`, `figjam`, or `dev_mode`.<br><br>`seat_type`String<br><br>The seat type that the user had requested and was rejected for. Can be `Full`, `Collab` or `Dev`. |
| ### org\_user\_account\_type\_upgrade\_requested[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_account_type_upgrade_requested "Direct link to org_user_account_type_upgrade_requested")<br><br>A user requested to have their role upgraded to an editor role. | `editor_type`String<br><br>The product type whose default paid status changes. Can be `figma`, `figjam`, or `dev_mode`.<br><br>`requested_user_license_group_id`Number<br><br>Workspace id of the user who requested the upgrade, or null if the user is not assigned to any workspace.<br><br>`seat_type`String<br><br>The seat type that the user requested an upgrade for. Can be `Full`, `Collab` or `Dev`. |
| ### org\_user\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_create "Direct link to org_user_create")<br><br>A user was added to an organization. | `permission`String<br><br>The permission level of the user in the org. Can be `guest`, `member` or `admin`.<br><br>`seat_type`String<br><br>The user's seat type. Can be `full`, `collab`, or `dev`.<br><br>`reason`String<br><br>The reason the user joined the org.<br><br>`actor_id`Number<br><br>The user that was responsible for the user joining the org.<br><br>`resource_type`String<br><br>The type of resource that was responsible for the user joining the org.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The ID or key of resource that was responsible for the user joining the org.<br><br>`figma_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's Figma Design seat. Can be `editor` for a full seat, `viewer`, or `viewer_restricted`.<br><br>`figjam_paid_status`String<br><br>The paid status of the user's FigJam seat. Can be `editor` for a full seat, `viewer`, or `viewer_restricted`. |
| ### org\_user\_create\_external[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_create_external "Direct link to org_user_create_external")<br><br>A user was added to an external organization as a guest. Only logged for Enterprise organizations with Domain Capture enabled. | `org_name`String<br><br>The name of the external organization that the user joined. |
| ### org\_user\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_delete "Direct link to org_user_delete")<br><br>A user was removed from an organization. |     |
| ### org\_user\_delete\_external[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#org_user_delete_external "Direct link to org_user_delete_external")<br><br>A user was removed from an external organization.
