| ### private\_widget\_publish[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#private_widget_publish "Direct link to private_widget_publish")<br><br>A widget was published privately to an organization. |     |
| ### private\_widget\_update[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#private_widget_update "Direct link to private_widget_update")<br><br>A widget published privately to an organization was updated. |     |
| ### project\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_create "Direct link to project_create")<br><br>A project was created in a particular team. |     |
| ### project\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_delete "Direct link to project_delete")<br><br>A project was deleted. |     |
| ### project\_export[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_export "Direct link to project_export")<br><br>The contents of a project were exported somewhere outside of the organization. |     |
| ### project\_import[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_import "Direct link to project_import")<br><br>The contents of a project were imported into an organization. |     |
| ### project\_member\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_member_add "Direct link to project_member_add")<br><br>A user was invited to a project. | `resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. Can only be `project` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The ID of the project.<br><br>`permission`String<br><br>The permission level for the user on the project. |
| ### project\_member\_permission\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_member_permission_change "Direct link to project_member_permission_change")<br><br>A user's permission level in a team changed. | `resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. Can only be `project` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The ID of the project.<br><br>`old_permission`String<br><br>The old permission level for the user on the project before the change.<br><br>`new_permission`String<br><br>The new permission level for the user on the project after the change. |
| ### project\_member\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_member_remove "Direct link to project_member_remove")<br><br>A user was removed from a project. | `permission`String<br><br>The permission level for the user on the project.<br><br>`resource_type`String<br><br>The type of the resource. Can only be `project` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>The ID of the project. |
| ### project\_move[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_move "Direct link to project_move")<br><br>A project was moved to another team. | `old_team_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the project' old parent team before it was moved.<br><br>`new_team_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the project' new parent team after it was moved. |
| ### project\_rename[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_rename "Direct link to project_rename")<br><br>A project was renamed. | `old_name`String<br><br>The old name of the project before it was renamed.<br><br>`new_name`String<br><br>The new name of the project after it was renamed. |
| ### project\_restore[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_restore "Direct link to project_restore")<br><br>A project was restored. |     |
| ### project\_team\_access\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_team_access_change "Direct link to project_team_access_change")<br><br>Team members' access to a project was changed. | `old_team_access`String<br><br>Team members' access to the project before the change. Can be `invite_only`, `view_only`, and `edit`.<br><br>`new_team_access`String<br><br>Team members' access to the project after the change. |
| ### project\_transfer\_approved[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_transfer_approved "Direct link to project_transfer_approved")<br><br>A request to transfer a project to a team in another organization was approved. |     |
| ### project\_transfer\_copy\_approved[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_transfer_copy_approved "Direct link to project_transfer_copy_approved")<br><br>A request to transfer a copy of a project to a team in another organization was approved. |     |
| ### project\_transfer\_copy\_received[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_transfer_copy_received "Direct link to project_transfer_copy_received")<br><br>A request was received to transfer a copy of a project to a team in another organization. | `team_id`String<br><br>ID of the team the project currently belongs to and initiated the request. |
| ### project\_transfer\_copy\_sent[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_transfer_copy_sent "Direct link to project_transfer_copy_sent")<br><br>A request was sent to transfer a copy of a project to an external pro or org team. | `destination_team_id`String<br><br>ID of the team the user is trying to transfer the copy of the project to. |
| ### project\_transfer\_received[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_transfer_received "Direct link to project_transfer_received")<br><br>A request was received to transfer a project to a team in another organization. | `team_id`String<br><br>ID of the team the project currently belongs to and initiated the request. |
| ### project\_transfer\_sent[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_transfer_sent "Direct link to project_transfer_sent")<br><br>A request was sent to transfer a project to an external pro or org team. | `destination_team_id`String<br><br>ID of the team the user is trying to transfer the project to. |
| ### project\_trash[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#project_trash "Direct link to project_trash")<br><br>A project was trashed. |     |
| ### provisional\_access\_end\_new\_request[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#provisional_access_end_new_request "Direct link to provisional_access_end_new_request")<br><br>A user requested a new provisional access, ending their previous provisional access. | `seat_type`String<br><br>What seat type the provisional access was for before it was ended because the user created a new request.<br><br>`auto_approval_setting`String<br><br>What the auto approval settings were at the time this activity log occurred |
| ### provisional\_access\_end\_request\_approved[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#provisional_access_end_request_approved "Direct link to provisional_access_end_request_approved")<br><br>An admin approved a user's request to upgrade, ending their provisional access. | `seat_type`String<br><br>What seat type the provisional access was for before it was approved.<br><br>`auto_approval_setting`String<br><br>What the auto approval settings were at the time this activity log occurred |
| ### provisional\_access\_end\_request\_denied[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#provisional_access_end_request_denied "Direct link to provisional_access_end_request_denied")<br><br>An admin denied a user's request to upgrade, ending their provisional access. | `seat_type`String<br><br>What seat type the provisional access was for before it was denied.<br><br>`auto_approval_setting`String<br><br>What the auto approval settings were at the time this activity log occurred |
| ### provisional\_access\_start[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#provisional_access_start "Direct link to provisional_access_start")<br><br>A user requested an upgrade, which started their provisional access. | `seat_type`String<br><br>What seat type the provisional access was started for.<br><br>`auto_approval_setting`String<br><br>What the auto approval settings were at the time this activity log occurred |
| ### public\_link\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#public_link_setting_change "Direct link to public_link_setting_change")<br><br>An admin changed the public link setting policy for the org. | `public_links_banned`Boolean<br><br>If org members cannot share files via public links.<br><br>`public_links_require_password`Boolean<br><br>If org members cannot share files via public links without passwords<br><br>`public_links_require_expiration`Boolean<br><br>If org members cannot share files via public links without expiration<br><br>`public_links_max_expiration_in_hours`Number<br><br>The max expiration in hours org members can set on public links |
| ### repo\_merge\_from\_source[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#repo_merge_from_source "Direct link to repo_merge_from_source")<br><br>A particular branch within a file was updated. | `main_file_key`String<br><br>Key of the file that the branch was created from. |
| ### repo\_merge\_to\_source[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#repo_merge_to_source "Direct link to repo_merge_to_source")<br><br>A particular branch within a file was merged with another branch. | `main_file_key`String<br><br>Key of the file that the branch was created from. |
| ### resource\_connection\_disconnected[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#resource_connection_disconnected "Direct link to resource_connection_disconnected")<br><br>A user disconnected a project connection. | `folder_name`String<br><br>Name of the resource that is being connected<br><br>`folder_id`String<br><br>ID of the resource that is being connected<br><br>`host_plan_type`String<br><br>Type of the plan that is initiating the connection<br><br>`host_plan_name`String<br><br>Name of the plan that is initiating the connection<br><br>`connected_plan_type`String<br><br>Type of the plan that is being connected<br><br>`connected_plan_name`String<br><br>Name of the plan that is being connected |

| ### team\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_create "Direct link to team_create")<br><br>A team was created in an organization with specific access settings to files and resources. | `org_access`String<br><br>Org member's access to the team. Can be `public`, `private`, or `secret`.<br><br>`sharing_audience_control`String<br><br>Org member's access to the team. Can be `org_view`, `org_edit`, or `invite_only`.<br><br>`org_browsable`String<br><br>Discoverability of the team in search. Applicable for invite\_only teams. If not browsable, it won't show up in search and users have to be explictily invited. |

| ### team\_license\_group\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_license_group_change "Direct link to team_license_group_change")<br><br>A team was moved to another workspace. Note: due to a data structure change, this action does not include new workspace events. See `team_workspace_change` for newer events. | `old_workspace_id`Number<br><br>ID of the workspace that the team was assigned to before the change.<br><br>`new_workspace_id`Number<br><br>ID of the workspace that the team is assigned to after the change. |
| ### team\_member\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_member_add "Direct link to team_member_add")<br><br>A user was added to a team. | `resource_type`String<br><br>Type of the resource that the user is added to. Can only be `team` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>ID of the team.<br><br>`permission`String<br><br>User's permission level in the team. |
| ### team\_member\_add\_external[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_member_add_external "Direct link to team_member_add_external")<br><br>A user was added to an external team. Only logged for Enterprise organizations with Domain Capture enabled. | `team_name`String<br><br>The name of the external team that the user joined. |
| ### team\_member\_permission\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_member_permission_change "Direct link to team_member_permission_change")<br><br>A user's permission level in a team changed. | `resource_type`String<br><br>Type of the resource that the user has a different permission on. Can only be `team` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>ID of the team.<br><br>`old_permission`String<br><br>User's permission level in the team before the change.<br><br>`new_permission`String<br><br>User's permission level in the team after the change. |
| ### team\_member\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_member_remove "Direct link to team_member_remove")<br><br>A user was removed from a team. | `permission`String<br><br>User's permission level in the team.<br><br>`resource_type`String<br><br>Type of the resource that the user is removed from. Can only be `team` for this event.<br><br>`resource_id_or_key`String<br><br>ID of the team. |
| ### team\_member\_remove\_external[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_member_remove_external "Direct link to team_member_remove_external")<br><br>A user was removed from an external team. Only logged for Enterprise organizations with Domain Capture enabled. | `team_name`String<br><br>The name of the external team that the user left. |
| ### team\_org\_access\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_org_access_change "Direct link to team_org_access_change")<br><br>Org members' access to a team is changed. | `old_org_access`String<br><br>Org member's access to the team before the change. Can be `public`, `private`, or `secret`.<br><br>`new_org_access`String<br><br>Org member's access to the team after the change. |
| ### team\_rename[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_rename "Direct link to team_rename")<br><br>A team's name was changed. |     |
| ### team\_restore[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_restore "Direct link to team_restore")<br><br>A previously deleted team was restored. |     |
| ### team\_sharing\_audience\_control\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_sharing_audience_control_change "Direct link to team_sharing_audience_control_change")<br><br>Org members' access to a team is changed. | `new_sharing_audience_control`String<br><br>Org member's access to the team. | ### team\_transfer\_sent[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_transfer_sent "Direct link to team_transfer_sent")<br><br>A request was sent to transfer a team to another organization | `destination_org_name`String<br><br>Name of the org the user is trying to transfer the team to. |
| ### team\_workspace\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#team_workspace_change "Direct link to team_workspace_change")<br><br>A team was moved to another workspace. | ### widget\_approvelist\_request\_approve\_org[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_approvelist_request_approve_org "Direct link to widget_approvelist_request_approve_org")<br><br>A widget request for the org was approved for the approved widget list. |     |
| ### widget\_approvelist\_request\_approve\_workspace[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_approvelist_request_approve_workspace "Direct link to widget_approvelist_request_approve_workspace")<br><br>A widget request for a specific workspace was approved for the approved widget list. |     |
| ### widget\_approvelist\_request\_org[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_approvelist_request_org "Direct link to widget_approvelist_request_org")<br><br>A widget was requested for the approved widget list. |     |
| ### widget\_approvelist\_request\_reject\_org[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_approvelist_request_reject_org "Direct link to widget_approvelist_request_reject_org")<br><br>A widget request for the org was rejected for the approved widget list across the entire org. |     |
| ### widget\_approvelist\_request\_reject\_workspace[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_approvelist_request_reject_workspace "Direct link to widget_approvelist_request_reject_workspace")<br><br>A widget request for a specific workspace was rejected for the approved widget list. |     |
| ### widget\_install[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_install "Direct link to widget_install")<br><br>A widget was installed for everyone in the organization to use. |     |
| ### widget\_ownership\_transfer[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_ownership_transfer "Direct link to widget_ownership_transfer")<br><br>A widget was transferred to another owner. |     |
| ### widget\_publisher\_accept\_invite[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_publisher_accept_invite "Direct link to widget_publisher_accept_invite")<br><br>A publisher invite for a widget was accepted. |     |
| ### widget\_publisher\_invite[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_publisher_invite "Direct link to widget_publisher_invite")<br><br>A widget publisher was invited. |     |
| ### widget\_publisher\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_publisher_remove "Direct link to widget_publisher_remove")<br><br>A widget publisher was removed. |     |
| ### widget\_publisher\_remove\_invite[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_publisher_remove_invite "Direct link to widget_publisher_remove_invite")<br><br>A publisher invite for a widget was removed. |     |
| ### widget\_uninstall[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widget_uninstall "Direct link to widget_uninstall")<br><br>A widget that was previously installed for everyone in the organization to use has been uninstalled. |     |
| ### widgets\_disable[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widgets_disable "Direct link to widgets_disable")<br><br>An admin turned off a setting where only widgets on the approved widget list can be used in that organization's files. |     |
| ### widgets\_enabled[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#widgets_enabled "Direct link to widgets_enabled")<br><br>An admin turned on a setting where only widgets on the approved widget list can be used in that organization's files. |     |
| ### workspace\_admins\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_admins_add "Direct link to workspace_admins_add")<br><br>A new workspace admin was added to the organization's workspace. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_admins\_add](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_admins_add)<br>. | `new_admin_emails`String\[\]<br><br>The emails of the users added to the workspace as admins. |
| ### workspace\_admins\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_admins_remove "Direct link to workspace_admins_remove")<br><br>A workspace admin was removed from the organization's workspace. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_admins\_remove](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_admins_remove)<br>. | `removed_admin_emails`String\[\]<br><br>The emails of the admins removed from the workspace. |
| ### workspace\_ai\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_ai_setting_change "Direct link to workspace_ai_setting_change")<br><br>A workspace in an org had its AI setting changed | `new_setting`String<br><br>The new AI setting for the workspace.<br><br>`old_setting`String<br><br>The old AI setting for the workspace.<br><br>`workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace.<br><br>`workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace. |
| ### workspace\_create[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_create "Direct link to workspace_create")<br><br>A new workspace was created in the organization. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_create](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_create)<br>. |     |
| ### workspace\_delete[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_delete "Direct link to workspace_delete")<br><br>A workspace was deleted from the organization. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_delete](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_delete)<br>. |     |
| ### workspace\_file\_export\_controls\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_file_export_controls_setting_change "Direct link to workspace_file_export_controls_setting_change")<br><br>The workspace level file export setting for the file was changed. | `file_export_setting`String<br><br>The workspace level file export setting (`allowed`, `banned`, or `members_only`).<br><br>`workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace.<br><br>`workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace. |
| ### workspace\_library\_approve[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_library_approve "Direct link to workspace_library_approve")<br><br>A library was marked as approved for a workspace | `workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace that is marking a library as approved.<br><br>`workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace that is marking a library as approved. |
| ### workspace\_library\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_library_setting_change "Direct link to workspace_library_setting_change")<br><br>A workspace changed a library's settings. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_library\_setting\_change](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_library_setting_change)<br>. | `library_file_key`String<br><br>The file key of the library the workspace was changing the setting for.<br><br>`library_file_name`String<br><br>The file name of the library the workspace was changing the setting for.<br><br>`workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace that was changing a library setting.<br><br>`workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace that was changing a library setting.<br><br>`old_figma_enabled`Boolean<br><br>If the library was previously enabled for the workspace for Figma design files.<br><br>`old_figjam_enabled`Boolean<br><br>If the library was previously enabled for the workspace for FigJam files.<br><br>`old_use_org_settings`Boolean<br><br>If the library's previous setting on the workspace was inherited from the org.<br><br>`new_figma_enabled`Boolean<br><br>If the library is now enabled in the workspace for Figma design files.<br><br>`new_figjam_enabled`Boolean<br><br>If the library is now enabled in the workspace for FigJam files.<br><br>`new_use_org_settings`Boolean<br><br>If the library's new setting on the workspace will be inherited from the org. |
| ### workspace\_library\_unapprove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_library_unapprove "Direct link to workspace_library_unapprove")<br><br>A library is marked as not approved for a workspace. | `workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace that is marking a library as not approved.<br><br>`workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace that is marking a library as not approved. |
| ### workspace\_member\_add[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_member_add "Direct link to workspace_member_add")<br><br>A user was added to the organization's workspace. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_membership\_change](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_membership_change)<br>. | `workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace the user was added to.<br><br>`workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace the user was added to.<br><br>`idp_group_id`String<br><br>The ID of the idp group linked to the workspace |
| ### workspace\_member\_remove[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_member_remove "Direct link to workspace_member_remove")<br><br>A user was removed from the organization's workspace. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_membership\_change](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_membership_change)<br>. | `workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace the user was removed from.<br><br>`workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace the user was removed from.<br><br>`idp_group_id`String<br><br>The ID of the idp group linked to the workspace |
| ### workspace\_membership\_self\_select[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_membership_self_select "Direct link to workspace_membership_self_select")<br><br>A user chose to join a particular workspace in the organization. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_membership\_self\_select](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_membership_self_select)<br>. | `workspace_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the workspace the user chose to join.<br><br>`workspace_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace the user chose to join. |
| ### workspace\_public\_link\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_public_link_setting_change "Direct link to workspace_public_link_setting_change")<br><br>A workspace in an org had its public link controls setting changed. | `public_links_banned`Boolean<br><br>If workspace members cannot share files via public links.<br><br>`public_links_require_password`Boolean<br><br>If workspace members cannot share files via public links without passwords.<br><br>`public_links_require_expiration`Boolean<br><br>If workspace members cannot share files via public links without expiration.<br><br>`public_links_max_expiration_in_hours`Number<br><br>The max expiration in hours workspace members can set on public links. |
| ### workspace\_rename[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_rename "Direct link to workspace_rename")<br><br>A workspace was renamed. Note: due to a data structure change, this action is only included for newer workspace events. For legacy workspace events, see [license\_group\_rename](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#license_group_rename)<br>. | `old_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace before it was renamed.<br><br>`new_name`String<br><br>The name of the workspace after it was renamed. |
| ### workspace\_team\_csv\_exported[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_team_csv_exported "Direct link to workspace_team_csv_exported")<br><br>The teams list of a workspace was exported as a CSV file. |     |
| ### workspace\_visibility\_setting\_change[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs-action-types/#workspace_visibility_setting_change "Direct link to workspace_visibility_setting_change")<br><br>A workspace's visibility setting was modified. | `old_org_access`String<br><br>Org member's access to the workspace before the change. Can be `hidden` or `visible`.<br><br>`new_org_access`String<br><br>Org member's access to the workspace after the change. Can be `hidden` or `visible`. |

# waitForTask | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The `waitForTask` function is useful for doing asynchronous work (eg. data fetching) in `useEffect`. It takes a promise and keeps the widget alive until the promise is resolved (or if there’s an explicit call to `figma.closePlugin`).

Signature[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#signature "Direct link to Signature")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [waitForTask](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/)
(task: Promise<any>): void

Parameters[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `task` | The widget will only be terminated when the given task is resolved |

Remarks[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#remarks "Direct link to Remarks")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

One of the main use cases of `waitForTask` is doing data fetching when a widget is inserted onto the canvas. When paired with `useEffect`, you can make a network request via an iframe and persist the response in a widget state. The `waitForTask` call will prevent the widget from being terminated until the given promise has resolved.

### Usage Example:[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#usage-example "Direct link to Usage Example:")

waitForTask example

    const { widget } = figmaconst { Text, useEffect, waitForTask, useSyncedState } = widgetfunction WaitForTaskExample() {  const [textContent, setTextContent] = useSyncedState("text", "Initial")  useEffect(() => {    waitForTask(new Promise(resolve => {      // Simulate async work      setTimeout(() => {        if (textContent !== "Final") {          setTextContent("Final")        }        // Resolve the task        resolve()      }, 1000)    }))  })  return <Text>{textContent}</Text>}widget.register(WaitForTaskExample)

*   [Signature](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#signature)
    
*   [Parameters](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#parameters)
    
*   [Remarks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#remarks)
    *   [Usage Example:](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/#usage-example)

# Authentication | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

When you make requests to endpoints in the Figma REST API, you must authenticate the requests you are sending. You can authenticate requests to the Figma REST API in two ways:

*   **Recommended:** Create an OAuth app to use OAuth 2, which lets you make requests with access tokens that you obtain on behalf of the users of your app. The advantage of an OAuth app is that you can make requests on behalf of other users, and the requests themselves aren’t made using your personal Figma account. Additionally, if for some reason you leave a company or team, OAuth apps are able to be reassigned by team or organization admins.
    
    Some parts of the REST API, such as the Activity Logs API and Discovery API, require you to use an OAuth app for authentication.
    
    note
    
    **Note:** If you’re working with the [Embed API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/embeds/embed-api/)
    , you’ll also need to create an OAuth app even if you won’t be authenticating users.
    
*   Generate a personal access token for your Figma account. A personal access token allows you to make REST API requests using your personal Figma account.
    

For both OAuth apps and personal access tokens, you’ll specify what [scopes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
 you want to permit. Scopes define what resources the OAuth 2 access token or personal access token. For example, to read the content of files, you would grant the `file_content:read` scope to the OAuth app or personal access token.

OAuth apps[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#oauth-apps "Direct link to OAuth apps")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An OAuth app is used to implement authenticated access from your existing application to the Figma REST API. In this context, “existing application” can mean a lot of things. For example:

*   A script or internal tool you use in your organization
*   A platform or app that serves users of Figma
*   Automation for developer systems

…and any other software interface that you want to use to access the Figma REST API.

An OAuth app enables you to use OAuth 2 for authentication. [OAuth 2](https://oauth.net/2/)
 is a web security protocol that allows 3rd party applications to establish a link between a user’s account and their access to a given API, on behalf of that user.

For example, suppose you have an app that lets users review library usage of their design system. You’d create an OAuth app, configure OAuth 2, exchange tokens on behalf of the user, and then use the access token for that user to make requests to the Library Analytics API. Because you’re acting on behalf of the user, the analytics data they get from the REST API would be limited to what’s available to their Figma account.

Regardless of whether your existing application is only used internally by your organization or is a public platform available generally to users, you’ll need to register an OAuth app in order to access the REST API.

To start using OAuth 2 to access the Figma REST API:

1.  Create and configure an OAuth app
2.  Authenticate users
3.  Make requests to the REST API
4.  Refresh access tokens

**Prerequisites**

To use OAuth 2 to authenticate users, you’ll need a URL or endpoint on an external server that can be used as a callback for exchanging and refreshing tokens.

important

**Important:** We recently announced some updates for Figma's developer platform that impact requirements for registering OAuth apps. If you previously created an OAuth app, you will need to take some steps to update and publish it. To learn more, see [Re-publish OAuth Apps](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/republish-oauth-apps/)
.

### Create an OAuth App[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#create-an-oauth-app "Direct link to Create an OAuth App")

Creating an OAuth app lets you use OAuth 2 to establish links between existing Figma user accounts and your application, which allows you to access data on the user's behalf with Figma's APIs.

To create an OAuth app:

1.  Click **My Apps** in the top toolbar, or go to [figma.com/developers/apps](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
    .
    
2.  In the upper-right corner of the page, click **Create a new app**.
    
3.  Enter a name for your OAuth app, and select a team or organization. The OAuth app must be associated with a team or organization.
    
    note
    
    **Note:** Users of your application don’t need to be a part of the team or organization you select.
    
4.  Click **Create**.
    
5.  Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** for your OAuth app and store them securely. This is the only opportunity you have to obtain the Client Secret. The secret is required for token exchange and refreshing access tokens.
    
6.  Click **Done**.
    
    Your OAuth app is created in a draft state and the configuration modal for your OAuth app appears. Continue with the next steps to configure your OAuth app.
    

note

**Draft, private, and public OAuth apps**

OAuth apps can be in a draft state, private, or public.

*   When you create an OAuth app, it starts in a **draft** state. While in draft, you and admins for your plan can test your OAuth app. The app can’t be used to make requests on behalf of other users, including those in your team or organization.
*   **Public** OAuth apps can be used to make requests on the behalf of any user that authorizes with your app. When you publish a public OAuth app, Figma reviews it. When Figma approves your public OAuth app, you can then start authorizing users.
*   **Private** OAuth apps can be used to make requests on behalf of users in team or organization you associated your app with when you created it. Unlike public OAuth apps, private OAuth apps don’t require review by Figma when you publish them.

You’ll select whether your OAuth app is private (default) or public when you [configure it](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#configure-an-oauth-app)
.

### Configure an OAuth app[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#configure-an-oauth-app "Direct link to Configure an OAuth app")

You can configure your OAuth app:

*   Immediately after you create the OAuth app
*   Any time while the OAuth app is in a draft state
*   Any time after you’ve published the OAuth app. In the case of published public apps, changes to configuration will require your OAuth app to be re-reviewed.

You’re not required to configure your OAuth app until you’re ready to publish it privately (only for your team or organization) or publicly (available to users of Figma in general). However, you won’t be able to set up OAuth 2 or start making requests to the Figma REST API with your application until you finish the configuration steps.

If you start to configure your app and decide to come back later, changes you’ve made will be saved.

**Configuring and publishing**

To configure and publish your OAuth app, you go to [figma.com/developers/apps](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
 and click an OAuth app to go through the configuration flow. During the flow, you choose whether your OAuth app is private or public and select the scopes your OAuth app needs. You publish your app at the end of the configuration flow.

If your OAuth app is public, it undergoes review by Figma. Once approved, you’re be able to use your OAuth app to authenticate users.

You can close the configuration flow at any time and your OAuth app will remain in draft if it hasn’t been previously published.

The following section describes the steps in detail.

To configure a OAuth app:

1.  In the list of your OAuth apps at [figma.com/developers/apps](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
    , click the OAuth app that’s in a draft state. If you’ve just created your OAuth app, the configuration modal will already be open.
    
    The configuration modal has several pages:
    
    *   General
        
    *   OAuth credentials
        
    *   OAuth scopes
        
    *   Embed API
        
        note
        
        **Note:** These steps cover working with the REST API. For an explanation of the Embed API page, see the [Embed API documentation](https://www.figma.com/developers/embed#embed-api)
        .
        
    *   Publish (for private apps)
        
    *   Testing instructions (for public apps)
        
    *   Review and submit (for public apps)
        
2.  On the **General** page you can change the name you entered when you created the app and upload a logo.
    
3.  On the **OAuth credentials** page, click **Add a redirect URL** and add one or more redirect URLs for your OAuth app.
    
    Redirect URLs correspond to the callback URLs you use for exchanging and refreshing tokens. The Figma REST API will only allow token exchange with the redirect URLs you specify.
    
4.  On the **OAuth scopes** page, select one or more scopes that you want to use for your OAuth app. For example, if your application needs to read the layers and content of a Figma file, select the `file_content:read` scope under **Files**.
    
    important
    
    **Important:** This step is critical for ensuring your OAuth app correctly serves your users. You need to make sure the scopes you select for your app match the endpoints you’re making requests to.
    
5.  On the **Publish** page, select whether you want your app to be public or private.
    
    tip
    
    If you select **Public** and you want to publish your app to the Figma Community, select **List this app on community**. For detailed steps, see [Publish an app to the Figma Community](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/35074258201495)
    
6.  On the **Describe your app** page, add the required logo and description for your OAuth app.
    
7.  On the **Review scopes** page, review the scopes that you selected for your OAuth app on the **OAuth scopes** page.
    
8.  For private OAuth apps, click the **Publish** button in the lower-right corner of the configuration modal. You’re finished! Your private OAuth app is published and available use in your team or organization.
    
    For public OAuth apps, continue to the next step.
    
9.  On the **Testing instructions** page (public OAuth apps only):
    
    1.  Enter all the steps that are required for in order for the OAuth app reviewer to successfully test your application and understand its features.
    2.  Optionally, add a link to a testing video.
    3.  If necessary for working with your application, provide a free trial URL or login credentials that the OAuth app reviewer can use to access your application.
10.  On the **Review and submit** page (public OAuth apps only), read the summary of what to expect during the review process. You should also read the [Figma Community Apps Review Guidelines](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/34963247780247)
     to ensure your application meets the requirements.
    
    When you’re ready, click the **By submitting my app for review…** checkbox, and then **Submit for review**.
    
    You’re done! When your OAuth app has been reviewed and approved, you’re ready to use your OAuth app to authenticate users and make REST API requests on their behalf.
    

note

### Example: Public OAuth app submission[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#example-public-oauth-app-submission "Direct link to Example: Public OAuth app submission")

Let's walk through a good example of a detailed public OAuth app submission.

**Example app**: TaskBridge is a project management platform that syncs Figma comments and dev resources with external task tracking systems like Jira, Linear, and Asana. It allows teams to manage design feedback and development handoffs from within their existing project management workflows.

Here's how the configuration fields would be filled out for this example:

**General page**:

*   **App name**: "TaskBridge - Manage Comments & Resources"
*   **Logo**: A 512x512 pixel logo for the TaskBridge brand

**OAuth credentials page**:

*   **Redirect URLs**:
    *   `https://app.taskbridge.io/auth/figma/callback`
    *   `https://staging.taskbridge.io/auth/figma/callback` (for testing)

**OAuth scopes page**:

*   `current_user:read`: To identify users and associate their Figma activity with their project management accounts
*   `file_comments:read`: To synchronize comments in Figma files with the comments in TaskBridge, and notify users when a comment is added
*   `file_comments:write`: To post status updates and responses back to Figma from the external platform
*   `file_dev_resources:read`: To pull in dev resources and specs attached to designs so they are stored in a centralized location for TaskBridge users
*   `file_dev_resources:write`: To update dev resources with implementation status and technical notes
*   `file_content:read`: To understand file context and link comments to specific design elements

**Describe your app page**:

*   **Description**: "TaskBridge seamlessly connects your Figma design workflow with your project management tools. It automatically syncs comments, dev resources, and design feedback between Figma and platforms like Jira, Linear, and Asana, ensuring your team stays aligned throughout the design-to-development process."

**Testing instructions page**:

*   **Testing steps**:
    1.  Visit [https://taskbridge.io](https://taskbridge.io/)
         and create a free account
    2.  Connect both your Figma account and your preferred project management tool (Jira/Linear/Asana)
    3.  Select a Figma file with existing comments and dev resources
    4.  Configure sync rules (e.g., "Create Jira ticket when comment mentions @dev")
    5.  Add a comment in Figma mentioning development work to see the automatic ticket creation
    6.  Update the dev resource status in TaskBridge to see it reflected back in Figma
*   **Testing video**: A 3-minute demo that shows the bi-directional sync in action
*   **Trial access**: Free 14-day trial available - no credit card required. Test credentials for Jira sandbox available upon request.

This example demonstrates how a real-world OAuth app would be configured for review by Figma, with appropriate scopes for its functionality and clear instructions for reviewers.

### Authenticate users[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#authenticate-users "Direct link to Authenticate users")

When you’re ready to authenticate users, you need to do the following:

1.  **Provide an authentication URL to the user.** This step gets you the authentication code you’ll need in order to generate an access token for the user.
2.  **Exchange the resulting authentication code for an access token.** The access token you receive from this step is what you’ll use to make API requests.

**Provide an authentication URL**

To get the access token you’ll use to make requests to the Figma REST API on behalf of a user, you need to provide the user an authentication URL. When the user follows the URL, they’ll have the opportunity to review what scopes your OAuth app is requesting, and to consent to giving your application access.

The exact method you use to provide the URL is up to you. For example, you could provide the URL as a part of a web app’s workflow for connecting to Figma.

important

**Important:** Regardless of how you provide the URL itself, the user must access the authentication URL using a web browser, not an embedded WebView inside your application. WebView access to the Figma OAuth flow is not supported.

To authenticate a user, ask them to visit the following URL:

    GET https://www.figma.com/oauth?  client_id=:client_id&  redirect_uri=:callback&  scope=:scope&  state=:state&  response_type=code

The following table describes the parameters in the URL.

| **Parameter** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| client\_id | The client ID of your OAuth app. |
| redirect\_uri | This must be one of the redirect URLs you set as a callback for your OAuth app. The auth page will call this URL if the user allows access to your app. |
| scope | A space-separated or comma-separated list of [scopes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)<br> that you need for your application. Scopes determine which endpoints can be accessed. For example, if your application needs to read files and post comments, your scope value would be files:read,file\_comments:write.  <br>  <br>You can provide the `scope` query parameter a subset of the scopes you selected when you configured your OAuth app. |
| state | This is a value that you should randomly generate and store. When we call back to your callback endpoint, you should check that the state value we pass back to you matches the state value that you initially used in your request. |
| response\_type | Currently we only support the authorization code flow for OAuth 2, so the only valid value here is code. We may support other values in the future. |
| code\_challenge | Optional but highly recommended. If you are using PKCE (proof key for code exchange), you must provide a code challenge. Only the S256 method for generating the code challenge is supported. |

Once the user navigates to this address, they will see a screen like the following:

![An OAuth app authorizon page that requests access to read email, file contents, and other scopes. A blue allow access button and a switch accounts link are at the bottom.](https://developers.figma.com/img/rest-api/ExampleAppOAuth.png)

If the user rejects the request, no callback is triggered.

When the user clicks **Allow access**, the client is redirected to the callback that was passed for `redirect_uri`.

For example, suppose the URL you provided for `redirect_uri` was the following: `https://example.com/oauth/callback`. The expected callback request would look like: `https://example.com/oauth/callback?code=:code&state=:state`

Where:

*   **:code** is the authentication code that can be traded in for an access token.
*   **:state** should correspond to the state value you provided in the authentication URL.

You should check that the `state` value passed back to you is the same as the one originally generated.

**Exchange the authentication code for an access token**

After the user allows access and you get the activation code from the response, you can pass the code back to the OAuth API to get the user's access token.

**Note:** Authentication codes expire **30 seconds** after being issued. You must exchange the code for an access token by calling `https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/token` within 30 seconds of the user completing the OAuth grant. You will receive an error if you attempt to exchange the code after this window, even if the code appears valid.

To get an access token, make a `POST` request to `https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/token`. Include the `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` header.

Use the `HTTP Basic Authentication` scheme to send your `client_id` and `client_secret` in the request. First, concatenate your client ID and client secret with a colon: `client_id:client_secret`. Then, Base64 encode the resulting string and include it in the Authorization header. The header should be formatted like:

`Authorization: Basic <BASE64_ENCODED_CLIENT_ID_AND_SECRET>`

The request body should use the following format:

`redirect_uri=:callback&code=:code&grant_type=authorization_code`

Make sure to [URL-encode](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Percent-encoding)
 the parameter values. The list of parameters is:

| **Parameter** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| redirect\_uri | This must match the must match the redirect URI originally provided, and code must match the authentication code provided to your callback. |
| code | This is the code given to you in the callback request. |
| grant\_type | This must be authorization\_code. |
| code\_verifier | If you used the PKCE flow, you must provide the verifier that you used to generate the code challenge. |

If the parameters are valid, you will receive the user access token and token expiration (in seconds) in a JSON response with the following structure:

    {  "user_id_string": <USER ID for the user who authorized the app>,  "access_token": <TOKEN>,  "token_type": "bearer",  "expires_in": <EXPIRATION (in seconds)>,  "refresh_token": <REFRESH TOKEN>}

**Note:** This response may contain a numeric `user_id` property. This property is deprecated. Please use `user_id_string` instead. Figma user IDs appear to be numeric, but many cannot be represented in common number formats such as JavaScript numbers and Go's float64.

Access tokens[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#access-tokens "Direct link to Access tokens")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A personal access token gives the holder access to an account through the API as if they were the user who generated the token.

### Generate a personal access token[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#generate-a-personal-access-token "Direct link to Generate a personal access token")

1.  Login to your Figma account.
2.  From the [file browser](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/14381406380183-Guide-to-the-file-browser)
    , click the account menu in the top-left corner and select **Settings**.
3.  Select the **Security** tab.
4.  In the **Personal access tokens** section, click **Generate new token** to open the configuration modal.
5.  Set the expiration and [scopes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
     for the token. The scopes will determine which endpoints the token has access to.
6.  Click **Generate token**. This will be your only chance to copy the token, so make sure you keep a copy of this in a secure place.

From the **Security** tab of your account settings, you can hover over the name of an existing token to see what scopes it has. You can also see when the token was last used (give or take a few minutes). If you see any activity on a token that you did not initiate, revoke the token immediately. Revocations are instant and can be made at any time.

You can also generate an access token from this page to explore our endpoints. The token generated here will expire in 24 hours.

### Use the personal access token[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#use-the-personal-access-token "Direct link to Use the personal access token")

You can now use the token to make requests to the Figma API. You can do so by passing the token to the API in the `X-Figma-Token` header of your request.

*   [OAuth apps](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#oauth-apps)
    *   [Create an OAuth App](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#create-an-oauth-app)
        
    *   [Configure an OAuth app](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#configure-an-oauth-app)
        
    *   [Example: Public OAuth app submission](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#example-public-oauth-app-submission)
        
    *   [Authenticate users](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#authenticate-users)
        
*   [Access tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#access-tokens)
    *   [Generate a personal access token](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#generate-a-personal-access-token)
        
    *   [Use the personal access token](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#use-the-personal-access-token)

# Scopes | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Scopes for [personal access tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#access-tokens)
 and [OAuth 2 tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#oauth-apps)
 determine which endpoints can be accessed.

The following table lists the scopes that are available.

**Important**: Scopes do not supersede the permissions granted to you by an organization or the owner of a project, team, or file.

For example, even if your token uses the `files:read` scope, you can only access files that you've created or that have been shared with you (whether shared directly, or because you belong to a corresponding project or team). Similarly, if you listed projects and teams, you'd only see the projects and teams that you can access in Figma's file browser.

| Scope | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `current_user:read` | Read your name, email, and profile image. |
| `file_comments:read` | Read the comments for files. |
| `file_comments:write` | Post and delete comments and comment reactions in files. |
| `file_content:read` | Read the contents of files, such as nodes and the editor type. |
| `file_dev_resources:read` | Read dev resources in files. |
| `file_dev_resources:write` | Write dev resources to files. |
| `file_metadata:read` | Read metadata of files. |
| `file_variables:read` | Read variables in files. Note: Enterprise plan only. |
| `file_variables:write` | Write variables and collections in files. Note: Enterprise plan only. |
| `file_versions:read` | Read the version history for files you can access. |
| `files:read` | **Deprecated**. Read files, projects, users, versions, comments, components, styles, and webhooks. While this scope will continue to work, it's highly recommended you use the granular scopes Figma provides. Because `files:read` is extremely permissive, more limited scopes such as `file_content:read` and `file_comments:read` provide enhanced security and stability by reducing the surface of access to your files. |
| `library_analytics:read` | Read your design system analytics. Note: Enterprise plan only. |
| `library_assets:read` | Read data of individual published components and styles. |
| `library_content:read` | Read published components and styles of files. |
| `org:activity_log_read` | Read organization [activity logs](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/activity-logs/)<br>. Note: Enterprise plan only. Must be an organization admin. |
| `org:discovery_read` | Read [text event data](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery/)<br> in the organization. Note: Enterprise plans with Governance+ only. Must be an organization admin. |
| `projects:read` | List projects and files in projects. |
| `selections:read` | Read most recent selection in files you can access. |
| `team_library_content:read` | Read published components and styles of teams. |
| `webhooks:read` | Read metadata of webhooks. |
| `webhooks:write` | Create and manage webhooks. |

note

**Note**: the `file_read` scope is deprecated for OAuth 2 tokens. Please migrate your application to use the scopes above.

# Rate Limits | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

important

**Important:** As part of our efforts to make Figma’s app ecosystem more consistent and reliable, we recently announced new rate limits. See our [recent announcement](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/)
 about the adjusted rate limits that will go into effect on **November 17**.

caution

**Note:** Figma reserves the right to change rate limits. Changes may affect specific endpoints, tiers, or plans.

Figma applies rate limits to requests to the Figma REST API to provide a consistent and reliable experience for users. Requests to the Figma REST API are limited based on three factors:

*   The plan of the resource that the user is requesting
*   The seat type of the user
*   The rate limit tier of the endpoint

Rate limit tiers are determined based on a number of factors, including the infrastructure and cost required to support the endpoint.

Rate limits apply to both OAuth apps and personal access tokens.

*   For OAuth apps, rate limits are tracked on a per-user, per-plan, per-app basis. This means that a user gets a unique rate limit budget for each app they’re using. A user’s usage of app A won’t affect their usage of app B.
    
    For example, Jasmine uses two apps, Figlet and Twigma. Figlet and Twigma both make requests to `GET file metadata`, a Tier 2 endpoint. Figlet frequently refreshes data so she sometimes gets rate-limited. However, because Twigma and Figlet use separate OAuth apps, Jasmine is able to use Twigma normally. Since rate limits are only applied per app, Figlet’s frequent rate-limiting issue doesn’t impact Twigma.
    
    David is on the same plan as Jasmine. David sometimes uses Twigma so much that his usage is rate-limited. However, because David and Jasmine have separate Figma user accounts, David’s rate-limited usage of Twigma doesn’t impact Jasmine’s usage.
    
*   For personal access tokens, rate limits are tracked on a per-user, per-plan basis, where the user is whoever generated the token.
    
    For example, Mei builds a script for her team that uses `GET images`, a Tier 1 endpoint. To run the script, Mei uses a personal access token to authenticate with the REST API. When multiple people on her team are running the script at the same time, occasionally the script gets rate-limited. Because the requests are being made with Mei’s personal access token, the requests all count toward the same limit.
    

The following table describes rate limits by API tier, seat, and plan. Rate limits are per-minute unless otherwise specified.

| API tier | Seat | Starter | Professional | Organization | Enterprise |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Tier 1 | View, Collab | Up to 6/month | Up to 6/month | Up to 6/month | Up to 6/month |
| Dev, Full | 10/min | 15/min | 20/min |
| Tier 2 | View, Collab | Up to 5/min | Up to 5/min | Up to 5/min | Up to 5/min |
| Dev, Full | 25/min | 50/min | 100/min |
| Tier 3 | View, Collab | Up to 10/min | Up to 10/min | Up to 10/min | Up to 10/min |
| Dev, Full | 50/min | 100/min | 150/min |

For View and Collab seats, requests are limited up to the given amount. Depending on traffic and demand, the actual limit may be lower. For example, a user with a View seat who tries to query a Tier 1 endpoint might only be able to make 2 requests in a month to get a file when traffic and demand to the REST API are high.

How rate limiting works[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#how-rate-limiting-works "Direct link to How rate limiting works")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For managing rate limits, Figma uses a leaky bucket algorithm. When the REST API is unable to fulfill requests due to the bucket being full or your rate limit being exceeded, the endpoint returns a `429` error.

**429 errors**

`429` errors have the following fields:

    "Retry-After": Integer"X-Figma-Plan-Tier": String enum"X-Figma-Rate-Limit-Type": String enum"X-Figma-Upgrade-Link": String

The following table describes the fields in the error.

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Retry-After` | Integer | In seconds, how long before you should retry sending the request. |
| `X-Figma-Plan-Tier` | String enum | The current plan tier of the resource the user is requesting. The value will be one of the following strings:  <br><br>*   `enterprise`<br>*   `org`<br>*   `pro`<br>*   `starter`<br>*   `student` |
| `X-Figma-Rate-Limit-Type` | String enum | The type of rate limit the user is encountering, based on their seat type. The value will be one of the following:  <br><br>*   `low` for Collab and Viewer seats<br>*   `high` for Full and Dev seats |
| `X-Figma-Upgrade-Link` | String | A link to either the `/pricing` or `/settings` pages depending on the plan/seat of the user. |

What if my app is hitting rate limits?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#hitting-rate-limits "Direct link to What if my app is hitting rate limits?")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you see your app makes requests that are getting rate-limited:

*   **Review the [rate limit table](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
     and check the tier of the rate-limited endpoint.** Also check the `X-Figma-Rate-Limit-Type` value in the [`429` error](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#how-rate-limiting-works)
     returned when the endpoint is rate-limited. The table, endpoint tier, and rate limit type will help you identify why a given user is encountering rate limits.
*   **Batch requests whenever possible.** For example, suppose you have an app that downloads images from a Figma file. Instead of sending individual requests for each image, you’d want to send a single request that includes a list of all the node ids you want to download as images.
*   **Cache results.** Rather than sending frequent requests, such as a new requests whenever a part of an app’s UI is navigated to, instead cache the data from an earlier request. Then, either refresh the data when requested (such as when a user explicitly wants to refresh data in the app UI), or on a periodic basis that fits the rate limits.
*   **Make sure your app is correctly [handling rate-limited requests](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#handling-rate-limited-requests)
    .** Make sure your app gracefully retries user requests after the `Retry-After` value in the `429` error.

Handling rate-limited requests[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#handling-rate-limited-requests "Direct link to Handling rate-limited requests")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When a request has been rate-limited, the endpoint you’re querying will start to return `429` errors. The `429` error describes why the request has been rate-limited.

The error includes a [`Retry-After` value](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#429-error-table)
, which you can use to resend a request after the given period.

The error also includes a `X-Figma-Upgrade-Link` value, which includes a URL you can surface to users of your app. This is helpful when the demand they have for requests exceeds their current seat and plan. For example, a user with a Viewer seat on a Pro plan who wants to frequently query files may benefit from having a Full seat instead so they’re no longer limited to a maximum of 5 requests daily to that endpoint.

**Example: Schedule and resend**

Depending on the implementation of your application, you can build logic to retry the request after the period returned in the error.

The following example demonstrates one way you could handle retrying the request.

    const TOKEN = // <ACCESS_TOKEN>const FILE_KEY = // <FILE_KEY>const sleep = (ms) => new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));async function exampleRetryAfter429(url, opts = {}, { maxRetries = 5 } = {}) {  let attempts = 0;  while (true) {    const res = await fetch(url, {      ...opts,      headers: { ...(opts.headers || {}), Authorization: `Bearer ${TOKEN}` },    });    if (res.status !== 429) return res;    if (attempts++ >= maxRetries) {      throw new Error(`429 Too Many Requests after ${attempts} attempts`);    }    const retryAfterSec = Number(res.headers.get("retry-after")) || 1; // integer seconds    await sleep(retryAfterSec * 1000);  }}(async () => {  const url = `https://api.figma.com/v1/files/${encodeURIComponent(FILE_KEY)}`;  const res = await exampleRetryAfter429(url, { method: "GET" }, { maxRetries: 6 });  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status} ${res.statusText}`);  console.log(JSON.stringify(await res.json(), null, 2));})().catch((err) => {  console.error(err.message || String(err));  process.exit(1);});

In the example, we handle the `429` error and capture the `Retry-After` value.

We also set an arbitrary number of retry attempts, 6 in the case of this example. In a practical implementation, you might prefer an approach like a backoff.

When we get the `Retry-After` value, we set a timer. When the timer expires, we then attempt to send the request again. In this way, we’re able to retry the request without manual intervention while also respecting the rate limit.

note

**Note:** The example only demonstrates handling and retrying 429 errors. The code doesn’t demonstrate a complete solution for sending requests and handling all errors.

**Example: Surface errors to a user**

For OAuth apps, depending on the way your application is built, you may want to surface errors to your users. For example, if a user is rate-limited because of the plan they’re on, you may want to surface the documentation URL that’s provided by the error.

The following example demonstrates a simple way to handle a `429` error and return the URL from `X-Figma-Upgrade-Link` to the requester.

    const express = require("express");const app = express();const TOKEN = // <ACCESS_TOKEN>app.get("/api/file/:key", async (req, res) => {  const key = req.params.key;  const r = await fetch(`https://api.figma.com/v1/files/${encodeURIComponent(key)}`, {    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${TOKEN}` },  });  if (r.status === 429) {    const upgradeUrl = r.headers.get("x-figma-upgrade-link") || null;    const retryAfterSec = Number(r.headers.get("retry-after")) || null;    return res.status(429).json({ error: "rate_limited", upgradeUrl, retryAfterSec });  }  if (!r.ok) {    return res.status(r.status).send(await r.text());  }  res.json(await r.json());});app.listen(3000, () => {});

For this example, let’s imagine we have a client where a user wants to get the data from a Figma file, and a server that handles making the requests and returning the responses to the user.

In the example, we send a `GET` request. Let’s assume the file key is a value provided by the user via the client. We then handle any `429` error by extracting the URL from `x-figma-upgrade-link` and sending that back to the client.

In the client, we’d then catch the incoming URL and surface it to the user. You might use an error message like: `We can't get that file right now. If you need to access files more frequently, you may need to upgrade your seat. See: <URL>`

The exact method and message you use to surface the URL to your users in a client will depend on the implementation and what information you want to share with the user.

note

**Note:** The example only demonstrates returning the URL from `X-Figma-Upgrade-Link`. The code doesn’t demonstrate comprehensive error handling or how you’d surface the URL in the client.

*   [How rate limiting works](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#how-rate-limiting-works)
    
*   [What if my app is hitting rate limits?](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#hitting-rate-limits)
    
*   [Handling rate-limited requests](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#handling-rate-limited-requests)

# Comments | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Figma’s Commenting mode allows users to leave comments directly on layers or objects on the canvas. This is useful for collaboration when you want to suggest an adjustment, provide feedback, or critique a specific object or layout. You can use the Figma API to access a whole host of information about a comment, from when the comment was made and by whom, to its exact location on the canvas.

Commenting types[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#commenting-types "Direct link to Commenting types")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are a number of different properties associated with a comment and comment reactions, that help to give us more context. The following contains all of the properties comments and their reactions will have:

| Type | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### Comment[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#comment-type "Direct link to Comment")<br><br>A comment or reply left by a user. | `id`String<br><br>Unique identifier for comment.<br><br>`client_meta`[Vector](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#vector-type)<br> \| [FrameOffset](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#frameoffset-type)<br> \| [Region](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#region-type)<br> \| [FrameOffsetRegion](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#frameoffsetregion-type)<br><br>Positioning information of the comment. Includes information on the location of the comment pin, which is either the absolute coordinates on the canvas or a relative offset within a frame. If the comment is a region, it will also contain the region height, width, and position of the anchor in regards to the region.<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The file in which the comment lives.<br><br>`parent_id`String<br><br>If present, the id of the comment to which this is the reply.<br><br>`user`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user who left the comment.<br><br>`created_at`String<br><br>The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the comment was left.<br><br>`resolved_at`String<br><br>If set, the UTC ISO 8601 time the comment was resolved.<br><br>`order_id`Number<br><br>Only set for top level comments. The number displayed with the comment in the UI.<br><br>`reactions`[Reaction](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#reaction-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>An array of reactions to the comment. |
| ### Reaction[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#reaction-type "Direct link to Reaction")<br><br>A reaction left by a user. | `user`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user who left the reaction.<br><br>`emoji`String<br><br>The emoji type of reaction as shortcode (e.g. `:heart:`, `:+1::skin-tone-2:`). The list of accepted emoji shortcodes can be found in [this file](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/missive/emoji-mart/main/packages/emoji-mart-data/sets/14/native.json)<br> under the top-level `emojis` and `aliases` fields, with optional skin tone modifiers when applicable.<br><br>`created_at`String<br><br>The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the reaction was left. |

*   [Commenting types](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#commenting-types)
    *   [Comment](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#comment-type)
        
    *   [Reaction](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#reaction-type)

# Users | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

A user is a Figma account of an individual that has signed up for Figma and created an account.

Types[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#types "Direct link to Types")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every user will have recorded ― and can be identified by ― the following four properties:

| Type | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### User[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type "Direct link to User") | `id`String<br><br>Unique stable id of the user<br><br>`handle`String<br><br>Name of the user<br><br>`img_url`String<br><br>URL link to the user's profile image<br><br>`email`String<br><br>Email associated with the user's account. This will only be present on the `/v1/me` endpoint |

*   [Types](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#types)
    *   [User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)

# Version history | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/version-history-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Figma allows you to distinguish different stages or **Versions** of a file, as the file evolves over time. This is all recorded in the **Version history**, which allows users to view, track and restore previous versions of a file.

warning

**IMPORTANT:** The order of versions returned by GET file versions has changed. Versions are now ordered explicitly by when they were created. As a part of this change, the URL format for next\_page and prev\_page has also changed.

For more information, see the [changelog](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/)
.

Types[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/version-history-types/#types "Direct link to Types")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figma will record some basic information about the file, like the who and when, as well as allow the user to determine a title and description for the version. These form the basis of the properties associated with version history.

| Type | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### Version[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/version-history-types/#version-type "Direct link to Version")<br><br>A version of a file. | `id`String<br><br>Unique identifier for version.<br><br>`created_at`String<br><br>The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the version was created.<br><br>`label`String<br><br>The label given to the version in the editor.<br><br>`description`String<br><br>The description of the version as entered in the editor.<br><br>`user`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user that created the version. |

*   [Types](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/version-history-types/#types)
    *   [Version](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/version-history-types/#version-type)
