# Changelog | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

September 23, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-09-23 "Direct link to September 23, 2025")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**New**

*   New OAuth app publishing flow for all public and private OAuth apps built on Figma’s REST API. All apps need to complete this flow by **November 17, 2025** in order to stay active. [Read more about the changes here](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/)
     and check out the new [Apps page](https://figma.com/developers/apps)
    .
*   Documentation about the new [Figma MCP Server](https://figma.com/blog/design-context-everywhere-you-build/)
    , now in beta, is available [here](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/)
    .

**Updates**

*   Published and adjusted REST API rate limits will go into effect on **November 17, 2025**. [Read more about rate limits here](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/)
    .
*   Developer documentation is now consolidated into a single instance. The REST API docs are split across multiple pages instead of a single scrollable page. As a result, the “try it out” and personal access token (PAT) generation functionality in the docs is not available right now. You can instead use our [Postman public workspace](https://www.postman.com/figma-dev/figma-public-workspace/overview)
     to test REST API functionality and use [this reference](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#access-tokens)
     to generate PATs

July 7, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-07-07 "Direct link to July 7, 2025")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The [GET team components](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-team-components-endpoint)
     endpoint `page_size` parameter has a new maximum value of 1000. Values exceeding the maximum will automatically be capped to 1000.
*   Added API methods to obtain information about nodes or children of nodes with layout mode `GRID`. These updates include:
    *   `gridRowCount` & `gridColumnCount`
    *   `gridRowGap` & `gridColumnGap`
    *   `gridRowsSizing` & `gridColumnsSizing`
    *   `gridRowAnchorIndex` & `gridColumnAnchorIndex`
    *   `gridRowSpan` & `gridColumnSpan`
    *   `gridChildHorizontalAlign` & `gridChildVerticalAlign`

June 27, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-06-27 "Direct link to June 27, 2025")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figma is introducing the [Discovery API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery/)
 for Enterprise plans with Governance+.

The Discovery API is used to obtain text events that have occurred in Figma files throughout your organization.

The text events returned by the Discovery API include:

*   **In-file text**: Text that's been added to layers (text layers and shapes with text) in Figma Design, as well as stickies and tables in FigJam.
*   **Cursor chat**: Chat messages associated with the cursor.
*   **File comments and reactions**: Comments and reactions that appear in files.
*   **Component documentation descriptions and links**: Text and links that have been added to component documentation.
*   **Annotations and developer-related links in Dev Mode**: Annotations and developer-related links (dev resources) that have been added in Dev Mode.

Requests to the Discovery API return links to JSON files that can be downloaded. The API provides one JSON file for each hour of data in the timeframe covered by the request, up to 24 hours.

For details about using the API, see the [Discovery API documentation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery/)
.

May 28, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-05-28 "Direct link to May 28, 2025")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We've introduced some changes to how [Webhooks V2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks/)
 works that enable additional ways you can utilize webhooks.

The improvements include:

*   In addition to teams, you can now attach webhooks to specific files and projects. When you create a webhook, you now specify a context (`team`, `file`, or `project`) and the id of the context you want to attach the webhook to. This allows you to receive events related to a specific file or project, in addition to team-level events.
*   We've introduced a new [GET /v2/webhooks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-endpoint)
     endpoint, along with the `plan_api_id` property. The endpoint can be used to get all webhooks for a given context, or, using `plan_api_id`, all existing webhooks that you have access to across all contexts.
*   We've added a new [DEV\_MODE\_STATUS\_UPDATE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#dev-mode-status-update-event)
     event type. The `DEV_MODE_STATUS_UPDATE` event is triggered when the Dev Mode status of a layer changes. This can be used to track when layers are marked Ready for Dev, Completed, or a Dev Status is cleared in Figma Design, and includes information like a change message if one was provided when the status changed.

Because we are introducing context-based webhooks, we're deprecating the old `GET /v2/teams/:team_id/webhooks` endpoint.

For now, the old endpoint will continue to work, but we recommend you migrate to the new `GET /v2/webhooks` endpoint and use the `team` context: `GET v2/webhooks?context=team&context_id=:team_id`

To learn more about the changes, see the [Webhooks V2 documentation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks/)
.

May 16, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-05-16 "Direct link to May 16, 2025")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To align with the OAuth 2.0 specification, Figma is migrating the endpoint for refreshing OAuth tokens.

Previously, you used the `https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/refresh` endpoint to refresh existing OAuth tokens.

Now, when you refresh your OAuth tokens, you should use the `https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/token` endpoint.

The legacy endpoint will continue to be supported for now, but we recommend you migrate to using the new endpoint. For specific instructions about how to refresh your OAuth tokens, see [Refreshing OAuth tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#refresh-oauth-token)
.

May 7, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-05-07 "Direct link to May 7, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added the following new node types and properties in beta:

*   [TEXT\_PATH](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#text-path-props)
     node type supporting [TextPathTypeStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#textpathtypestyle-type)
     properties.
*   [TRANSFORM\_GROUP](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#transform-group-props)
     node type.
*   `TEXTURE` and `NOISE` [effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#effect-type)
     property types.
*   `PROGRESSIVE` blur [effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#effect-type)
    .
*   `PATTERN` [paint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#paint-type)
     property type.

The node types and properties in beta can be used with Figma Design and Figma Draw.

April 29, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-04-29 "Direct link to April 29, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added a [GET file metadata](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-file-metadata-endpoint)
 endpoint. This endpoint provides the same file metadata provided by the [GET file](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-files-endpoint)
 endpoint but does not contain the file content. Requires the `file_metadata:read` scope.

April 28, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-04-28 "Direct link to April 28, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   We have updated the policy for generating Personal Access Tokens (PATs). Users can now create PATs with a maximum expiry of 90 days. Non-expiring PATs can no longer be created. Please update your token management practices accordingly to accommodate this change.
*   Approval is now required to use the [Projects](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/)
     endpoints. If you're building a new app and would like to use these endpoints, you can [request access](https://forms.gle/xoWgCx5K25iYci5L6)
    .

April 17, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-04-17 "Direct link to April 17, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added new [scopes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#scopes)
 for the REST API. The `files:read` scope is no longer recommended and will be deprecated in the future. Instead of `files:read`, use a more specific scope like `file_content:read` or `file_comments:read`. Using a more specific scope helps Figma customers understand what data your app is accessing.

April 15, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-04-15 "Direct link to April 15, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [POST /v1/oauth/token](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#post-oauth-token-endpoint)
 endpoint now returns user IDs in string format via the `user_id_string` property. The numeric `user_id` property is deprecated.

March 19, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-03-19 "Direct link to March 19, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added `containingComponentSet` to [FrameInfo](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-types/#frameinfo-type)
, and deprecated `containingStateGroup` which retains the same data.

February 24, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-02-24 "Direct link to February 24, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The sunset date of the OAuth security migration has been extended to February 26th, 2025.

February 13, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-02-13 "Direct link to February 13, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added support for `targetAspectRatio` in the [GET files](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-files-endpoint)
 endpoint. `targetAspectRatio` allows for proper proportional resizing on the canvas, unlike the legacy `preserveRatio` field. `preserveRatio` is kept for backwards-compatibility, but will now be powered by `targetAspectRatio` under the hood.

February 11, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-02-11 "Direct link to February 11, 2025")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Library Analytics API is now available. Users on an Enterprise plan can leverage the API to fetch analytics data about how your organization's design system libraries are being used, including usage of components, styles, and variables.

For more information, see the [Library Analytics API documentation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-intro/)
.

January 23, 2025[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2025-01-23 "Direct link to January 23, 2025")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`TypeStyle` objects, which describe text formatting properties, now contain a `fontStyle` property that indicates whether text is bold, italicized, etc.

December 5, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-12-05 "Direct link to December 5, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the [GET files](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-files-endpoint)
 endpoint, we occasionally return the error message `Request timeout, try a smaller request`. Moving forwards, we will return the error message `Request too large. If applicable, filter by query params`. We recommend filtering by using the `ids` and `depth` query parameters to reduce the size of the request.

November 25, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-11-25 "Direct link to November 25, 2024")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Beginning December 9th, 2024, requests to `http://api.figma.com` will no longer be supported. This change enforces HTTPS-only communication for improved security. HTTP requests will fail with a `403 Forbidden` status code instead of automatically redirecting to HTTPS.

To prepare for this change, please ensure all API request URLs use `https://api.figma.com` instead of `http://api.figma.com`

October 29, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-10-29 "Direct link to October 29, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Introduced styles and variables to the beta Library Analytics API. Get action time series data and usage data for styles and variables.
*   Added new endpoints for the Library Analytics API
    *   [GET styles actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-styles-actions-endpoint)
        
    *   [GET styles usages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-styles-usages-endpoint)
        
    *   [GET variables actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-variables-actions-endpoint)
        
    *   [GET variables usages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-variables-usages-endpoint)
        
    *   [GET component actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-component-actions-endpoint)
        
    *   [GET component usages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-component-usages-endpoint)
        
*   New component analytics endpoints cover the same data as the prior Library Analytics endpoints but have been updated for consistency with styles and variables. While the legacy beta endpoints will continue to work in the short-term, we recommend switching over to the new component endpoints. The new component endpoints have the following breaking changes:
    *   `num_instances` has been renamed to `usages`
    *   `num_teams_using` has been renamed to `teams_using`
    *   num\_files\_using has been renamed to files\_using
    *   `num_files_using` has been renamed to `files_using`
    *   Breakdowns by component now include a `component_set_key` and `component_set_name` for components that belong to a component set. The component set name is no longer appended to the front of the `component_name`.
    *   We no longer support the order query parameter
*   Analytics data is now updated daily rather than weekly

October 28, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-10-28 "Direct link to October 28, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Updated OAuth token exchange documentation to recommend sending client credentials in the `Authorization` header rather than in the request body.

October 25, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-10-25 "Direct link to October 25, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The [GET local variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-local-variables-endpoint)
     endpoint has been updated to include variables that have been deleted in the editor, but may still be referenced by contents in the document. This can occur if you bind a property or variable alias to a variable, and then use the "Local variables" menu to delete the variable. Variables in this state will be annotated with a `deletedButReferenced` field set to `true`.

October 22, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-10-22 "Direct link to October 22, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The response from the [GET file versions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/version-history-endpoints/#get-file-versions-endpoint)
 endpoint has changed.

**Previously**

Versions returned by the `GET file versions` endpoint were ordered by an internal identifier that was logically equivalent to when a version was created.

**Now**

Versions returned by the `GET file versions` endpoint are now ordered explicitly by when they were created. New calls to the GET API return updated `next_page` and `prev_page` URLs that use the new ordering. Old URLs that use the previous method of ordering will stop working on November 22, 2024.

October 8, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-10-08 "Direct link to October 8, 2024")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Updated recommended endpoints for OAuth token exchange to `https://api.figma.com`.

September 24, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-09-24 "Direct link to September 24, 2024")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added the `freeText` property to the [Measurement](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#measurement-type)
 object. This is the displayed value of the measurement when it's manually overridden.

September 12, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-09-12 "Direct link to September 12, 2024")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added an interactions field to the [TransitionSourceTrait](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#transition-source-trait-type)
 attribute in the [GET file](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-files-endpoint)
 endpoint. This field contains full data about prototyping interactions on the node, equivalent to the [reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/properties/nodes-reactions/)
 field of the Plugin API.

August 15, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-08-15 "Direct link to August 15, 2024")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added several new properties to the [TypeStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#typestyle-type)
 object: `isOverrideOverTextStyle`, `semanticWeight`, and `semanticItalic`.

May 30, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-05-30 "Direct link to May 30, 2024")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Added new [VariableScope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#variable-scope-type)
 options for scoping variables to typography fields such as font family, font style and weight, font size, and other text fields.

April 29, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-04-29 "Direct link to April 29, 2024")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add new Enterprise beta endpoints for reading [Library Analytics](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-intro/)
. Fetch action time series data and library usage grouped by different dimensions.

April 24, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-04-24 "Direct link to April 24, 2024")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Add variable support to `gradientStops` in [ColorStop](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#colorstop-type)
    . This affects Paints.
*   Update [ColorStop](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#colorstop-type)
     documentation to reflect the addition of the `boundedVariables` field.

February 14, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-02-14 "Direct link to February 14, 2024")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We are excited to announce the beta release of the OpenAPI specification and Typescript types for the Figma REST API in the open source [figma/rest-api-spec](https://github.com/figma/rest-api-spec)
 repository.

OpenAPI is a specification for describing HTTP APIs in a language-agnostic manner. It has a large ecosystem of tools to let you generate API documentation, client SDKs, and more. We also provide custom Typescript types generated from the OpenAPI specification for those of you with Typescript codebases to make it easy to write type-safe code out of the box.

January 25, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-01-25 "Direct link to January 25, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   New `annotations` field containing an [Annotation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#annotation-type)
     with notes and pinned properties of nodes in Dev Mode. Available in private beta; stay tuned for public release.
*   New `measurements` field on [CANVAS](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#canvas-props)
     nodes containing an array of type [Measurement](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#measurement-type)
    , which display pinned distances between nodes in Dev Mode.

January 17, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-01-17 "Direct link to January 17, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Fix bugs related to `region_width` and `region_height` in [FrameOffsetRegion](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#frameoffsetregion-type)
. This affects [Comments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/)
 endpoints.

January 10, 2024[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2024-01-10 "Direct link to January 10, 2024")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `as_md` parameter in the [GET comments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/#get-comments-endpoint)
 endpoint now correctly respects false as a value.

December 6, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-12-06 "Direct link to December 6, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Comment reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-types/#reaction-type)
     are no longer limited to a subset of 7 emoji. Comment Reactions now support emoji up to version 14.0.
*   Ability to get `boundVariables` on [LayoutGrid](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#layoutgrid-type)
     and [Effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#effect-type)
     properties.
*   New [VariableScope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#variable-scope-type)
     types for scoping variables to layer opacity, stroke weight, and effect fields.

November 29, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-11-29 "Direct link to November 29, 2023")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**API changes**

*   The `absoluteRenderBounds` node property in the [GET file](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-files-endpoint)
     and [GET file nodes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-file-nodes-endpoint)
     endpoints is now nullable, which fixes a bug where it previously gave a `Rectangle` with null values inside in some cases. A node has null render bounds if it is not visible.

**Documentation changes**

*   Clarify the behavior of the `ids` query parameter in the [GET file](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-files-endpoint)
     endpoint where we might include extra nodes if the desired node subtrees have dependencies
*   Add API call examples for file endpoints
*   Remove `containing_page` property for published components and component sets, since this property doesn't exist
*   Add missing `Path` data type for fill/stroke geometries
*   Add missing `strokeCap` enum values
*   Fix properties for the team entity in activity log events

November 8, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-11-08 "Direct link to November 8, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [GET image](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-image-endpoint)
 endpoint now supports a `contents_only` option, which can be set to false in order to render content that overlaps the requested nodes.

October 25, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-10-25 "Direct link to October 25, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add `variableIds` property to [variable collections](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#variable-collection-type)
 to expose the order of variables within a collection.

October 18, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-10-18 "Direct link to October 18, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Add `maskType` property to all nodes that support `isMask`, and deprecate `isMaskOutline` (which now corresponds to `maskType = 'VECTOR'`).
*   Support variables in `LIBRARY_PUBLISH` webhook events, which are triggered when a library is published.

October 4, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-10-04 "Direct link to October 4, 2023")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Add `svg_outline_text` query parameter for the [GET image](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-image-endpoint)
     endpoint to control whether text elements are rendered as outlines (vector paths) or as `<text>` elements in SVGs.

September 20, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-09-20 "Direct link to September 20, 2023")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Add `svg_include_node_id` as a query parameter for the [GET image](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-endpoints/#get-image-endpoint)
     endpoint. This adds node ids to svg elements as `data-node-id`.

August 31, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-08-31 "Direct link to August 31, 2023")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Variable and collection objects returned by the [GET published variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-endpoints/#get-published-variables-endpoint)
     endpoint include an `updatedAt` timestamp that indicates the last time a change to the variable or collection was published.

August 17, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-08-17 "Direct link to August 17, 2023")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Get and set `codeSyntax` for local variables in the [variables endpoints](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-endpoints/)
    .
*   Support for [deleting dev resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#delete-dev-resources-endpoint)
    .
*   Support filtering dev resources for specific `node_ids` when fetching [dev resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#get-dev-resources-endpoint)
    .

August 9, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-08-09 "Direct link to August 9, 2023")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Support for reading `layoutSizingHorizontal` and `layoutSizingVertical` on auto-layout frames and their children.

August 2, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-08-02 "Direct link to August 2, 2023")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Ability to get and set `scopes`, `description`, `hiddenFromPublishing` for local variables, and `hiddenFromPublishing` for local variable collections for the [variables endpoints](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-endpoints/)
    .

July 26, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-07-26 "Direct link to July 26, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Support space-separated scopes in addition to comma-separated scopes for [OAuth authentication](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#oauth2)
    .

July 10, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-07-10 "Direct link to July 10, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add `cornerSmoothing` property to all nodes that support `cornerRadius`.

June 21, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-06-21 "Direct link to June 21, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This update adds brand new endpoints for variables and dev resources, and introduces important changes to personal access and OAuth tokens.

*   New endpoints for [querying, creating, updating, and deleting variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-endpoints/)
    . Variables in Figma Design store reusable values that can be applied to all kinds of design properties and prototyping actions.
*   New endpoints for [querying, creating, and updating dev resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/)
    . Dev resources are developer-contributed urls that are attached to nodes in files and are shown in Figma Dev Mode.

**Authentication changes**

[Personal access tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#access-tokens)
 and [OAuth 2 tokens](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#oauth2)
 now support [scopes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/#scopes)
 that limit which endpoints the token has access to. For personal access tokens, there is a new flow for assigning scopes and expiration when generating a token. For OAuth 2, applications can ask for a new set of scopes when redirecting the user to allow access for the application.

Legacy tokens (personal access tokens with no scopes or OAuth tokens with the `file_read` scope) will continue to work as-is. However, the new endpoints above require additional scopes for your application to call them.

May 3, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-05-03 "Direct link to May 3, 2023")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Introduce the [Payments REST API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/payments-endpoints/)
     and [GET payments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/payments-endpoints/#get-payments-endpoint)
     endpoint.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Add `strokeDashes` property to [FRAME](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#frame-props)
     nodes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

     endpoint to return rich-text comments as markdown.

March 14, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-03-14 "Direct link to March 14, 2023")

*   Add [TABLE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#table-props)
     and [TABLE\_CELL](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#table-cell-props)
     node types.

February 6, 2023[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2023-02-06 "Direct link to February 6, 2023")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Update `componentPropertyReferences` to also show up on applicable instance sublayers.

December 16, 2022[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2022-12-16 "Direct link to December 16, 2022")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Font metrics improvements**

*   Node and style values for `fontWeight` will now be more accurate and reflect variable font settings and custom font style weights.
*   Node and style values for `lineHeight` will now use the main node or text style font metadata for determining percent-based line height values. Previously, it used the maximum `lineHeight` of all fonts used in the node.

December 7, 2022[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2022-12-07 "Direct link to December 7, 2022")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Add support for overrides**

*   Update [INSTANCE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#instance-props)
     nodes to include an `overrides` field. The `overrides` field is an array of all of the fields directly overridden on an instance.

November 9, 2022[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2022-11-09 "Direct link to November 9, 2022")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Add fill overrides**

*   Update [VECTOR](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-node-types/#vector-props)
     nodes to have `fillOverrideTable` which shows overriden fills for different regions in the vector.

October 28, 2022[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2022-10-28 "Direct link to October 28, 2022")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Update component set fields**

*   Update component set to include a `documentationLinks` field

September 28, 2022[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2022-09-28 "Direct link to September 28, 2022")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Add component properties fields**

*   Update nodes to have component properties-related fields

July 20, 2022[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2022-07-20 "Direct link to July 20, 2022")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Increase Comments Functionalities**

*   Update [Comment](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#comment-type)
     type to support [Region](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#region-type)
     and [FrameOffsetRegion](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#frameoffsetregion-type)
     types in `client_meta`
*   Update [Comment](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#comment-type)
     type to include [Reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#reaction-type)
    
*   Create [Reaction](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#reaction-type)
    , [Region](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#region-type)
    , and [FrameOffsetRegion](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#frameoffsetregion-type)
     types
*   Update [POST comments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/#post-comments-endpoint)
     endpoint to accept [Region](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#region-type)
     and [FrameOffsetRegion](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#frameoffsetregion-type)
     types as `client_meta`
*   Create [GET comment reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/#get-comment-reactions-endpoint)
    , [POST comment reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/#post-comment-reactions-endpoint)
    , and [DELETE comment reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-endpoints/#delete-comment-reactions-endpoint)
     endpoints

May 23, 2022[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/changelog/#2022-05-23 "Direct link to May 23, 2022")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**New properties**

The following properties have been added/updated:

*   `layoutPositioning`
*   `itemReverseZIndex`
*   `strokesIncludedInLayout`
*   `individualStrokeWeights`
*   `counterAxisAlignItems` can return `BASELINE`
*   `textAutoResize` can return `TRUNCATE`

# API Reference | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The Widget API allows you to create custom, interactive objects that extend the functionality of Figma design files and FigJam boards. You’ll likely use both the widget API and plugin API when building widgets.

*   Widget API: defines the custom object and how people can interact with it
*   Plugin API: access external resources or manipulate other nodes in the file

The Widget API is a component-based API similar to React. It gives you access to the tools you need to build a widget node on the canvas that responds to a variety of interactions.

There are three main aspects of the Widget API: components, functions, and hooks. Together they control rendering the widget in a file and managing its [state](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-state/)
 as you interact with it.

If you’ve used the Plugin API, you’ll already be familiar with the `figma` global object. You can access the Widget API from that same `figma` object, via `figma.widget`.

Components[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#components "Direct link to Components")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Components are the layers you’ll use to build your custom interactive widget. You can think of them as building blocks.

Each component supports a range of properties, which you can use to customize their appearance. Some properties are shared across components and some are unique to specific components.

There are components based on layers or node types you can already find and use in files:

*   [`AutoLayout`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/)
    
*   [`Frame`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Frame/)
    
*   [`Text`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Text/)
    
*   [`Rectangle`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Rectangle/)
    
*   [`Image`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/)
    
*   [`Ellipse`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Ellipse/)
    
*   [`SVG`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-SVG/)
    
*   [`Line`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Line/)
    

There are three other components available that aren’t based on layers:

*   [`Input`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Input/)
    : allows you to make a text component editable. This makes it possible for people to input or update text components within the widget.
*   [`Fragment`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Fragment/)
    : allows you to render children without having to group them in a parent node. You can't pass properties to a fragment.
*   [`Span`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Span/)
    : allows you to style ranges of text inside of a `Text` component.

Functions[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#functions "Direct link to Functions")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Functions are statements or blocks of code that perform a specific task. Functions accept parameters and other data as input and return a related output. There are only two functions defined in the Widget API:

*   [`register`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-register/)
    : the main entry point for rendering a widget. This function expects a widget function that describes your widget and returns the widget node made up of the components we mentioned above.
*   [`waitForTask`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-waitfortask/)
    : this function enables asynchronous work, such as data fetching. It accepts a promise and only terminates when that promise resolves.
*   [`colorMapToOptions`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-colormaptooptions/)
    : this function is called on a color palette defined in `figma.constants.colors.*`, and returns `WidgetPropertyMenuColorSelectorOption[]`.

Hooks[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#hooks "Direct link to Hooks")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hooks are a specific type of function. They allow you to reuse state-based logic or behavior across your components. You can identify hooks by their `use` prefix.

*   [`useEffect`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-useeffect/)
    : this hook can run any time the widget's state changes. It allows you to perform asynchronous tasks, bundle calls, or consolidate the side-effects of event handlers.
*   [`usePropertyMenu`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usepropertymenu/)
    : this hook allows you to define an interactive property menu that displays when the widget is selected.
*   [`useStickable`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usestickable/)
    : this FigJam only hook allows your widget to be stuck to other nodes in the file. This is similar to how stamps work in FigJam files.
*   [`useStickableHost`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usestickablehost/)
    : this FigJam only hook allows other nodes in the file to stick to your widget.
*   [`useSyncedState`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usesyncedstate/)
    : this hook declares that rendering your widget relies on a changeable state. You give this hook a key and default value, which you can use and update across different states.
*   [`useSyncedMap`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usesyncedmap/)
    : this hook also allows you to manage widget state. You can give this hook multiple keys and values and update widget state based on changes to individual values, not the entire map. This allows you to accurately render widget state when multiple people interact with a widget at once.
*   [`useWidgetId`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/properties/widget-usewidgetid/)
    : this hook allows you to reference a currently active widget. It returns a unique `id` which allows you to reference that WidgetNode in the Plugin API.

Destructure globals[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#destructure-globals "Direct link to Destructure globals")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We recommend [destructuring](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Operators/Destructuring_assignment#object_destructuring)
 components, hooks, and functions at the beginning of your widget code. This saves you from having to reference each of these components by their qualified names, such as `figma.widget.AutoLayout`.

    const { widget } = figmaconst {  // Components  AutoLayout,  Frame,  Text,  Input,  Rectangle,  Image,  SVG,  Ellipse,  Line,  Fragment,  // Hooks  useSyncedState,  useSyncedMap,  usePropertyMenu,  useEffect,  useStickable,  useStickableHost,  useWidgetId,  // Functions  register,  waitForTask} = widget

In our sample counter widget, we’ve done this to `figma.widget` as well as the individual hooks (`useSyncedState` and `usePropertyMenu`) and components (`AutoLayout`, `Text`, `SVG`) we’ll use in our widget code:

    const { widget } = figmaconst { useSyncedState, usePropertyMenu, AutoLayout, Text, SVG } = widget

*   [Components](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#components)
    
*   [Functions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#functions)
    
*   [Hooks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#hooks)
    
*   [Destructure globals](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/api-reference/#destructure-globals)

# <AutoLayout /> | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

`AutoLayout` is a frame with auto layout automatically applied. It therefore takes in some additional auto layout specific props, which are `direction` , `horizontalAlignItems` , `verticalAlignItems`, `spacing` and `padding`.

This means that children of an AutoLayout component will not be positioned by `x` and `y` constraints, but can have a width or height of `fill-parent`.

`BaseProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#baseprops "Direct link to baseprops")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### name?: string

The name of the component. This is useful to specify a data-layer attribute to make things more debuggable when you inspect the sublayers of your widget.

* * *

### hidden?: boolean

Toggles whether to show the component.

* * *

### onClick?: (event: [WidgetClickEvent](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-WidgetClickEvent/#widget-click-event)
) => Promise<any> | void

Attach a click handler on the given node. If the given function is async or returns a promise, the widget is only terminated when the async function has completed and the promise has been resolved. The click handler is also passed a [`WidgetClickEvent`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-WidgetClickEvent/)
 object that contains additional information about the click.

See also: [Handling User Events](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/handling-user-events/)
.

* * *

### key?: string | number

The key of the component.

* * *

### hoverStyle?: [HoverStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-HoverStyle/#hover-style)

The style to be applied when the mouse is hovering over the component.

* * *

### tooltip?: string

The tooltip that is shown to the user when hovering over the component.

* * *

### positioning?: 'auto' | 'absolute'

This value is ignored unless the node is a direct child of an AutoLayout frame.

| value | description |
| --- | --- |
| 'auto' | Layout this node according to auto-layout rules. |
| 'absolute' | Take this node out of the auto-layout flow, while still nesting it inside the auto-layout frame. This allows explicitly setting `x`, `y`, `width`, and `height`. |

* * *

### `BlendProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#blendprops "Direct link to blendprops")

### blendMode?: [BlendMode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-BlendMode/#blend-mode)

The blendMode of the component.

* * *

### opacity?: number

The opacity of the component.

* * *

### effect?: [Effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Effect/#effect)
 | [Effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Effect/#effect)
\[\]

The effect of the component.

* * *

### `ConstraintProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#constraintprops "Direct link to constraintprops")

### x?: number | [HorizontalConstraint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Constraint/#horizontal-constraint)

The x position of the node.

This value is ignored if the node is a child of an AutoLayout frame and has positioning set to 'auto'.

* * *

### y?: number | [VerticalConstraint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Constraint/#vertical-constraint)

The y position of the node.

This value is ignored if the node is a child of an AutoLayout frame and has positioning set to 'auto'.

* * *

### overflow?: Overflow

The overflow of the component - 'visible' | 'hidden' | 'scroll'

Specify `hidden` to clip the contents of the frame. `visible` and `scroll` both behave the same way and will allow the contents of the frame to overflow.

* * *

`SizeProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#sizeprops "Direct link to sizeprops")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### width?: [AutolayoutSize](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Size/#autolayout-size)

The width of the component.

* * *

### height?: [AutolayoutSize](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Size/#autolayout-size)

The height of the component.

* * *

### minWidth?: number

The minWidth of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

### maxWidth?: number

The maxWidth of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

### minHeight?: number

The minHeight of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

### maxHeight?: number

The maxHeight of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

`LayoutProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#layoutprops "Direct link to layoutprops")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### spacing?: number | 'auto' | [LayoutGap](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-LayoutGap/#layout-gap)

Determines distance between children of this AutoLayout frame. A single number value or 'auto' controls both horizontal and vertical gap.

"auto" spacing is the same as `justify-content: space-between` in css.

* * *

### padding?: [Padding](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Padding/#padding)

Determines the padding between the border of the AutoLayout frame and its children.

* * *

### direction?: 'horizontal' | 'vertical'

Determines the auto-layout direction of this frame

* * *

### horizontalAlignItems?: Omit<[AlignItems](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-AlignItems/#align-items)
, 'baseline'>

Determines how the children in this AutoLayout frame should be aligned in the horizontal direction.

* * *

### verticalAlignItems?: [AlignItems](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-AlignItems/#align-items)

Determines how the children in this AutoLayout frame should be aligned in the vertical direction.

* * *

### wrap?: boolean

Determines whether children that overflow the bounds of this frame will wrap to a new line. Only applicable when `direction` is set to `"horizontal"`.

* * *

### rotation?: number

The rotation of the node in degrees. Expects values from -180 to 180.

The rotation is with respect to the top-left of the object. Therefore, it is independent from the position of the object.

* * *

`CornerProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#cornerprops "Direct link to cornerprops")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### cornerRadius?: [CornerRadius](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-CornerRadius/#corner-radius)

The number of pixels to round the corners of the object by.

This value must be non-negative and can be fractional. If an edge length is less than twice the corner radius, the corner radius for each vertex of the edge will be clamped to half the edge length.

info

Rectangle nodes can also have different corner radii on each of the four corners.

* * *

`GeometryProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#geometryprops "Direct link to geometryprops")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### fill?: HexCode | [Color](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Color/#color)
 | [Paint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Paint/#paint)
 | ([SolidPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-SolidPaint/#solid-paint)
 | [GradientPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-GradientPaint/#gradient-paint)
)\[\]

The paints used to fill the area of the node

* * *

### stroke?: HexCode | [Color](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Color/#color)
 | [SolidPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-SolidPaint/#solid-paint)
 | [GradientPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-GradientPaint/#gradient-paint)
 | ([SolidPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-SolidPaint/#solid-paint)
 | [GradientPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-GradientPaint/#gradient-paint)
)\[\]

The paints used to fill the area of the node's stroke.

* * *

### strokeWidth?: number

The thickness of the stroke, in pixels. This value must be non-negative and can be fractional.

* * *

### strokeAlign?: [StrokeAlign](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-StrokeAlign/#stroke-align)

The alignment of the stroke with respect to the boundaries of the node.

Center-aligned stroke means the center of the stroke falls exactly on the geometry. Inside-aligned stroke shifts the stroke so it lies completely inside the shape, and outside-aligned stroke is vice versa.

info

Inside and outside stroke are actually implemented by doubling the stroke weight and masking the stroke by the fill. This means inside-aligned stroke will never draw strokes outside the fill and outside-aligned stroke will never draw strokes inside the fill.

* * *

### strokeDashPattern?: number\[\]

The alternating stroke dash and gap lengths, in pixels. An empty array gives a solid stroke and a single value will be applied to both the dash and gap length.

* * *

Default Props[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#default-props "Direct link to Default Props")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Prop | Defaults |
| --- | --- |
| `name` | `""` |
| `hidden` | `false` |
| `x` | `0` |
| `y` | `0` |
| `blendMode` | `"pass-through"` |
| `opacity` | `1` |
| `effect` | `[]` |
| `fill` | `[]` |
| `stroke` | `[]` |
| `strokeWidth` | `1` |
| `strokeAlign` | `"inside"` |
| `rotation` | `0` |
| `cornerRadius` | `0` |
| `overflow` | `"hidden"` |
| `width` | `"hug-contents"` |
| `height` | "hug-contents"\` |
| `direction` | `"horizontal"` |
| `wrap` | `false` |
| `spacing` | `0` |
| `padding` | `0` |
| `horizontalAlignItems` | `"start"` |
| `verticalAlignItems` | `"start"` |

*   [`BaseProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#baseprops)
    *   [`BlendProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#blendprops)
        
    *   [`ConstraintProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#constraintprops)
        
*   [`SizeProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#sizeprops)
    
*   [`LayoutProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#layoutprops)
    
*   [`CornerProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#cornerprops)
    
*   [`GeometryProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#geometryprops)
    
*   [Default Props](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-AutoLayout/#default-props)

# AlignItems | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-AlignItems/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Determines how the AutoLayout frame’s children should be aligned in primary/counter axis direction.

    type AlignItems = 'center' | 'start' | 'end' | 'baseline'

Remarks[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-AlignItems/#remarks "Direct link to Remarks")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `"start"` and `"end"` correspond to:
    *   left and right respectively for AutoLayout frames with "horizontal" direction.
    *   top and bottom respectively for AutoLayout frames with "vertical" direction.
*   `"baseline"` can only be set on horizontal AutoLayout frames, and aligns all children along the text baseline.

*   [Remarks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-AlignItems/#remarks)

# Re-publish OAuth Apps | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/republish-oauth-apps/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

We're making some changes to Figma's developer platform to provide a more reliable and consistent experience for builders and users of our APIs. This includes new requirements for publishing OAuth apps, and updated rate limits for the REST API which will go into effect on November 17, 2025. All existing apps will need to re-publish by November 17, 2025 to meet these requirements.

important

For more information about the changes, see [Updates to Figma's developer platform](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/)
.

For existing Figma OAuth apps, you'll need to re-publish the app via the [My Apps page](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
.

*   **Decide whether you want your app to be private or public.** Private apps are limited to your organization and don't require Figma approval. Public apps can be used by anyone with a Figma account and must be reviewed and approved by Figma.
*   **Identify the specific set of scopes you need for your OAuth app.** Figma has moved to using granular scopes to provide users clarity on what permissions they're granting your app. You need to ensure the scopes you're selecting for your OAuth app match the functionality. When you select a scope, you'll also need to provide reasoning why your app must use the scope.
*   **Re-publish your OAuth app.** With the changes we're making, we're adding an **Update required** label to existing OAuth apps. The label appears on the [My apps page](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
    . If your OAuth app supports an integration or application for Figma users in general, you'll need to publish your OAuth app as public and get Figma approval.

However, we've tried to ensure this won't impact the functionality of your existing OAuth apps for your users while you update the configuration:

*   **Your existing public OAuth app can still be used by Figma users.** This means that until November 17 your OAuth app will continue to function as expected while you update it.
*   **New allowed scopes will only be applied to you, your plan admins, and Figma's review team until you re-publish.** Existing users of your OAuth app shouldn't have their usage impacted by scope changes while you're updating the configuration.

### Configure and publish your OAuth app[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/republish-oauth-apps/#configure-and-publish-your-oauth-app "Direct link to Configure and publish your OAuth app")

To configure and publish your OAuth app, you go to [figma.com/developers/apps](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
 and click an OAuth app to go through the configuration flow. During the flow, you choose whether your OAuth app is private or public and select the scopes your OAuth app needs. You re-publish your app at the end of the configuration flow.

The following section describes the steps in detail.

**To configure a OAuth app:**

1.  In the list of your OAuth apps at [figma.com/developers/apps](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
    , click the OAuth app that you want to re-publish. If you've just created your OAuth app, the configuration modal will already be open.
    
    The configuration modal has several pages:
    
    *   General
        
    *   OAuth credentials
        
    *   OAuth scopes
        
    *   Embed API
        
        note
        
        **Note:** These steps cover working with the REST API. For an explanation of the Embed API page, see the [Embed API documentation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/embeds/embed-api/)
        .
        
    *   Publish
        
        *   Describe your app (for public and private apps)
        *   Review scopes (for public and private apps)
        *   Testing instructions (for public apps)
        *   Review and submit (for public apps)
2.  On the **General** page:
    
    1.  Optionally, change the name you entered when you created the app.
    2.  Upload a logo.
    3.  Select whether you want your app to be private or public.
3.  On the **OAuth credentials** page, click **Add a redirect URL** and add one or more redirect URLs for your OAuth app.
    
    Redirect URLs correspond to the callback URLs you use for exchanging and refreshing tokens. The Figma REST API will only allow token exchange with the redirect URLs you specify.
    
4.  On the **OAuth scopes** page, select one or more scopes that you want to use for your OAuth app. For example, if your application needs to read the layers and content of a Figma file, select the `file_content:read` scope under **Files**.
    
    important
    
    **Important:** This step is critical for ensuring your OAuth app correctly serves your users. You need to make sure the scopes you select for your app match the endpoints you're making requests to.
    
5.  If you're creating a private OAuth app for your team or organization, on the **Publish** page, select whether you want your app to be **Public** or **Private**.
    
6.  On the **Describe your app** page, add the required logo and description for your OAuth app.
    
    note
    
    **Note:** Name, logo, and description are all required. These values are visible on your app's Community page.
    
7.  On the **Review scopes** page, review the scopes that you selected for your OAuth app on the **OAuth scopes** page.
    
8.  For private OAuth apps, click the **Publish** button in the lower-right corner of the configuration modal. You're finished! Your private OAuth app is published and available use in your team or organization.
    
    For public OAuth apps, continue to the next step.
    
9.  On the **Testing instructions** page (public OAuth apps only):
    
    1.  Enter all the steps that are required for in order for the OAuth app reviewer to successfully test your application and understand its features.
    2.  Optionally, add a link to a testing video.
    3.  If necessary for working with your application, provide a free trial URL or login credentials that the OAuth app reviewer can use to access your application.
10.  On the **Review and submit** page (public OAuth apps only), read the summary of what to expect during the review process. You should also read Figma's [App review guidelines](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/34963247780247)
     to ensure your application meets the requirements.
    
    When you're ready, click the **By submitting my app for review…** checkbox, and then **Submit for review**.
    
    You're done! When your OAuth app has been reviewed and approved, you're ready to use your OAuth app to authenticate users and make REST API requests on their behalf.
    

*   [Configure and publish your OAuth app](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/republish-oauth-apps/#configure-and-publish-your-oauth-app)

# Updates to Figma's Developer Platform | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

We’re making some changes to Figma’s developer platform to provide a more reliable and consistent experience for builders and users of our APIs. These include new requirements for publishing OAuth apps, and updated rate limits for the REST API which will go into effect on November 17, 2025. All existing apps will need to be re-published by November 17, 2025 to meet these requirements.

Why we’re making these changes[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#why-were-making-these-changes "Direct link to Why we’re making these changes")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Users and teams that depend on Figma’s APIs have expressed a greater need for structure and predictability. App builders have asked for clearer guidelines and documentation, while users want more visibility into usage limits and assurance that integrations meet Figma’s standards.

The new resources and requirements detailed below give developers a clear blueprint for building apps that users can trust. Our REST API rate limits will be adjusted later this year to create a more predictable experience for developers and users of their apps.

Together, these changes reinforce the foundation of our developer platform, which allows us to confidently build out the REST API to support more products and use cases and roll out [improvements to the Figma MCP server](https://www.figma.com/blog/design-context-everywhere-you-build/)
.

What’s changing[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#whats-changing "Direct link to What’s changing")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OAuth apps that use Figma’s REST API will now need to provide the following information and undergo review by the Figma team before they’re published. If your app was created before September 23, 2025, you will need to re-publish your app on the [My apps page](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps)
 by 11:59 pm PST on November 17, 2025.

*   **App ownership** - Apps will need to designate a Figma team or organization as the app owner.
    
*   **Scope declaration** - Apps will need to choose and explain which scopes they plan to use. Apps using the `file_read` and `files:read` scopes today will need to switch to new, granular scopes like `file_content:read` and `file_metadata:read` for more precise permissions.
    
*   **App review and approval** - Apps that seek to be published publicly will need to be reviewed and approved by Figma’s team to ensure they meet [App review guidelines](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/34963247780247)
    . Apps that only seek to be published internally do not need to be reviewed by Figma. Later this year, all approved public apps that [opt in](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/35074258201495)
     will be published to the Figma Community.
    
*   **API rate limits** - Rate limits apply to all users. The rate limits vary depending on the API tier, as well as the user’s Figma plan (Starter, Professional, Organization, or Enterprise), and seat type (View, Collab, Dev, or Full). Personal access tokens will also be subject to the rate limits when they go into effect. You can reference our [rate limits documentation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/)
     for a deeper explanation.
    
    caution
    
    **Note:** Figma reserves the right to change rate limits. Changes may affect specific endpoints, tiers, or plans.
    

FAQ for app builders[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#faq-for-app-builders "Direct link to FAQ for app builders")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### How do I re-publish my app?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#how-do-i-re-publish-my-app "Direct link to How do I re-publish my app?")

Re-publish existing apps from the [My apps page](https://figma.com/developers/apps)
 by selecting the app and completing the steps that follow. You will be prompted to choose an app owner, determine if the app is for public or private consumption, and select scopes. If you’re re-publishing a public app, you will need to provide testing instructions for Figma’s team to use to review your public app. You can [read more about the re-publishing process here](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/republish-oauth-apps/)
.

### What will the app review process look like?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#what-will-the-app-review-process-look-like "Direct link to What will the app review process look like?")

Figma will use the testing info you provide to check that everything works as expected and follows our [App review guidelines](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/34963247780247)
, similar to what we do for public plugins and widgets. Our goal is to be thoughtful and reasonably prompt in our review. We’ll let you know over email if your app has been approved or needs changes.

### How will the new rate limits impact my app?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#how-will-the-new-rate-limits-impact-my-app "Direct link to How will the new rate limits impact my app?")

To help you understand how your app will handle these rate limits—and how your users will be affected—we’re holding a **rate limit preview period on October 21 from 9 am to 1 pm PST**. During that window, we’ll enable the rate limits so you can see how your app responds to 429 errors. The response will include information about the user’s seat type, so you can prompt them to manage their Figma plan or seat if needed.

If you’d prefer to skip the preview period, please reach out using our [review period opt-out form](https://form.asana.com/?k=Clyorc0yMSt3gfIQhkUIdA&d=10497086658021)
.

### When should I use the REST API versus the Figma MCP server to build integrations?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#when-should-i-use-the-rest-api-versus-the-figma-mcp-server-to-build-integrations "Direct link to When should I use the REST API versus the Figma MCP server to build integrations?")

It depends. If you are building agentic software coordinated by models, the Figma MCP server will save you time and give your users a better experience. If you need direct access to specific APIs, use the REST API.

### When is the deadline for re-publishing my app?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#when-is-the-deadline-for-re-publishing-my-app "Direct link to When is the deadline for re-publishing my app?")

Apps must be re-published by 11:59 pm PT November 17, 2025.

### What happens if I don’t do anything?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#what-happens-if-i-dont-do-anything "Direct link to What happens if I don’t do anything?")

Public and private OAuth apps that haven’t been re-published by the deadline will move into a draft state and be unable to call Figma's REST APIs. We do not want this to happen, so please contact us using our [support form](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
 if for any reason you need help to hit the deadline.

### I have more questions—what’s the best way to reach you for help?[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#i-have-more-questionswhats-the-best-way-to-reach-you-for-help "Direct link to I have more questions—what’s the best way to reach you for help?")

You can reach our developer platform team using our [support form](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
.

*   [Why we’re making these changes](https://developers.figma.com/docs/updates-to-figmas-developer-platform/#why-were-making-these-changes)

# Property types | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

In the previous section, we gave you a brief description of the comment types. The `client_meta` property of comments takes a custom Figma type, which is described below.

`Region` and `FrameOffsetRegion` represent comments that span a region. By default, the `comment_pin_corner` is in the bottom right. As an illustration, the following diagram shows how `node_offset`, `region_height`, and `region_width` interact with each other.

    +----------------------------------------+^node_offset origin                      ||                                        || +--------------------+                 || |    region_width    |                 || |                    |                 || |region_height       |                 || |                    |                 || |                    |                 || +--------------------+ <- node_offset  |+----------------------------------------+

| Type | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### FrameOffset[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#frameoffset-type "Direct link to FrameOffset")<br><br>Position of a comment relative to the frame to which it is attached. | `node_id`String<br><br>Unique id specifying the frame.<br><br>`node_offset`[Vector](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#vector-type)<br><br>2d vector offset within the frame from the top-left corner. |
| ### Region[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#region-type "Direct link to Region")<br><br>Position of a region comment on the canvas. | `x`Number<br><br>X coordinate of the position.<br><br>`y`Number<br><br>Y coordinate of the position.<br><br>`region_height`Number<br><br>The height of the comment region. Must be greater than 0.<br><br>`region_width`Number<br><br>The width of the comment region. Must be greater than 0.<br><br>`comment_pin_corner`Stringdefault: bottom-right<br><br>The corner of the comment region to pin to the node's corner as a string enum.<br><br>*   `bottom-right`<br>*   `bottom-left`<br>*   `top-right`<br>*   `top-left` |
| ### FrameOffsetRegion[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#frameoffsetregion-type "Direct link to FrameOffsetRegion")<br><br>Position of a region comment relative to the frame to which it is attached. | `node_id`String<br><br>Unique id specifying the frame.<br><br>`node_offset`[Vector](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/file-property-types/#vector-type)<br><br>2D vector offset within the frame from the top-left corner.<br><br>`region_height`Number<br><br>The height of the comment region.<br><br>`region_width`Number<br><br>The width of the comment region.<br><br>`comment_pin_corner`Stringdefault: bottom-right<br><br>The corner of the comment region to pin to the node's corner, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `bottom-right`<br>*   `bottom-left`<br>*   `top-right`<br>*   `top-left` |

*   [FrameOffset](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#frameoffset-type)
    
*   [Region](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#region-type)
    
*   [FrameOffsetRegion](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/comments-property-types/#frameoffsetregion-type)
