# Endpoints | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

See information about your projects and files within a project.

info

**Limited Access**

The Projects endpoints are only available for selected use cases and are not available to all apps. If you'd like to use these endpoints, you can [request access](https://forms.gle/xoWgCx5K25iYci5L6)
.

GET team projects[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#get-team-projects-endpoint "Direct link to GET team projects")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use this endpoint to get a list of all the projects within the specified team. It returns only projects visible to the authenticated user or owner of the developer token.

It is not possible to programmatically obtain team IDs. To obtain a team ID, navigate to the team page in the Figma file browser. The team ID is present in the URL after the word team and before your team name.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`projects:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#http-endpoint "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/teams/:team_id/projects`

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| team\_id | String<br><br>ID of the team to list projects from |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The "message" param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | The developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired |

GET project files[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#get-project-files-endpoint "Direct link to GET project files")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

List the files in a given project.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`projects:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/projects/:project_id/files`

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| project\_id | String<br><br>ID of the project to list files from |
| branch\_data | Booleandefault: false<br><br>Returns branch metadata in the response for each main file with a branch inside the project. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | The developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired |

*   [GET team projects](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#get-team-projects-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#http-endpoint)
        
*   [GET project files](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#get-project-files-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/projects-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1)

# Security | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-security/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

warning

**Important:** When creating a webhook you are required to pass in a `passcode`. The purpose of the `passcode` field is so that your application can verify that Figma is actually what is calling your endpoint (as opposed to some attacker trying to make you believe something about your users' Figma files).

For security purposes, when requesting webhooks using [GET webhook by id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-by-id-endpoint)
 or [GET webhooks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-endpoint)
, the `passcode` is redacted and an empty string is returned. This is to ensure that `passcode` is only exposed by events that we send you.

We recommend comparing the `passcode` we pass back to you in events with the `passcode` originally provided when creating the endpoint to make sure they match before acting on the webhook trigger. If you receive a request with the wrong `passcode`, you should respond with a `400 Bad Request` HTTP response which will immediately stop the webhook.

# Types | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

There are a number of different properties associated with a webhook events. The following contains all of the properties webhook events will have:

| Name | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### WebhookV2[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2-type "Direct link to WebhookV2")<br><br>A description of an HTTP webhook (from Figma back to your application). | `id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook.<br><br>`event_type`[WebhookV2Event](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2event-type)<br><br>The event this webhook triggers on.<br><br>`context`String<br><br>The type of context this webhook is attached to, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `PROJECT`<br>*   `TEAM`<br>*   `FILE`<br><br>`context_id`String<br><br>The ID of the context this webhook is attached to.<br><br>`team_id`String<br><br>The team id you are subscribed to for updates. If the webhook is subscribed to a file or project, this value is an empty string.<br><br>`plan_api_id`String<br><br>The plan API ID of the team or organization where this webhook was created.<br><br>`status`[WebhookV2Status](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2status-type)<br><br>The current status of the webhook.<br><br>`client_id`String<br><br>The client ID of the OAuth application that registered this webhook, if any.<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode that will be passed back to the webhook endpoint. For security, when using the GET endpoints, the value is an empty string.<br><br>`endpoint`String<br><br>The endpoint that will be hit when the webhook is triggered.<br><br>`description`String<br><br>Optional user-provided description or name for the webhook. This is provided to help make maintaining a number of webhooks more convenient. Max length 140 characters. |
| ### WebhookV2Request[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2request-type "Direct link to WebhookV2Request")<br><br>Information regarding the most recent interactions sent to a webhook endpoint. | `webhook_id`Number<br><br>The ID of the webhook the requests were sent to.<br><br>`request_info`[WebhookV2RequestInfo](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2requestinfo-type)<br><br>Information regarding the request sent to the webhook endpoint.<br><br>`response_info`0<br><br>Information regarding the response sent back from the webhook endpoint. `null` if no response was received.<br><br>`error_msg`String \| null<br><br>Error message for this request. `null` if no error occurred. |
| ### WebhookV2RequestInfo[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2requestinfo-type "Direct link to WebhookV2RequestInfo")<br><br>Information regarding the request sent to the webhook endpoint. | `endpoint`String<br><br>The actual endpoint the request was sent to.<br><br>`payload`[Payload](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#payload-type)<br><br>The contents of the request that was sent to the endpoint.<br><br>`sent_at`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the request was sent. |
| ### WebhookV2ResponseInfo[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2responseinfo-type "Direct link to WebhookV2ResponseInfo")<br><br>Information regarding the reply sent back from a webhook endpoint. | `status`String<br><br>HTTP status code of the response.<br><br>`received_at`String<br><br>The UTC ISO 8601 time when the response was received. |
| ### WebhookV2Event[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2event-type "Direct link to WebhookV2Event")<br><br>An enum representing the possible events that a webhook can subscribe to. | A webhook can subscribe to all events listed in the [Events section](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/)<br> except the `PING` event. |
| ### WebhookV2Status[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2status-type "Direct link to WebhookV2Status")<br><br>An enum representing the possible statuses you can set a webhook to. | *   `ACTIVE`: The webhook is healthy and receives all events.<br>*   `PAUSED`: The webhook is paused and will not receive any events. |
| ### LibraryItemData[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type "Direct link to LibraryItemData")<br><br>An object representing the library item information in the payload of the `LIBRARY_PUBLISH` event. | `key`String<br><br>Unique identifier of library item.<br><br>`name`String<br><br>Name of library item. |
| ### CommentFragment[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#commentfragment-type "Direct link to CommentFragment")<br><br>An object representing a fragment of a comment left by a user, used in the payload of the `FILE_COMMENT` event. Note only **ONE** of the fields below will be set. | `text`String<br><br>Comment text that is set if a fragment is text based.<br><br>`mention`String<br><br>User id that is set if a fragment refers to a user mention. |

*   [WebhookV2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2-type)
    
*   [WebhookV2Request](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2request-type)
    
*   [WebhookV2RequestInfo](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2requestinfo-type)
    
*   [WebhookV2ResponseInfo](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2responseinfo-type)
    
*   [WebhookV2Event](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2event-type)
    
*   [WebhookV2Status](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2status-type)
    
*   [LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)
    
*   [CommentFragment](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#commentfragment-type)

# Events | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The following table lists all the events that can be sent via a webhook.

| Event name | Trigger condition |
| --- | --- |
| ### PING[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#ping-event "Direct link to PING")<br><br>Debug event triggered whenever a webhook is created. Use this to verify that your endpoint was setup correctly. | `event_type`String<br><br>The type of the event, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `PING`<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided.<br><br>`timestamp`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.<br><br>`webhook_id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook that caused the callback.<br><br>**Example:**<br><br>      {    "event_type": "PING",    "passcode": "secretpasscode",    "timestamp": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "webhook_id": "22"  } |
| ### FILE\_UPDATE[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-update-event "Direct link to FILE_UPDATE")<br><br>Triggers within 30 minutes of editing inactivity in a file. | `event_type`String<br><br>The type of the event, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `FILE_UPDATE`<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The key of the file that was updated.<br><br>`file_name`String<br><br>The name of the file that was updated.<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided.<br><br>`timestamp`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.<br><br>`webhook_id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook that caused the callback.<br><br>**Example:**<br><br>      {    "event_type": "FILE_UPDATE",    "file_key": "CL06nJNn5eZLQKDoARMND5",    "file_name": "Developer page mockup demo",    "passcode": "secretpasscode",    "timestamp": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "webhook_id": "22"  } |
| ### FILE\_DELETE[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-delete-event "Direct link to FILE_DELETE")<br><br>Triggers whenever a file has been deleted. If you subscribe to `FILE_UPDATE`, you automatically get these notifications. Note that this does not trigger on all files within a folder, if the folder is deleted. | `event_type`String<br><br>The type of the event, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `FILE_DELETE`<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The key of the file that was deleted.<br><br>`file_name`String<br><br>The name of the file that was deleted.<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided.<br><br>`timestamp`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.<br><br>`triggered_by`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user that deleted the file and triggered this event.<br><br>`webhook_id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook that caused the callback.<br><br>**Example:**<br><br>      {    "event_type": "FILE_DELETE",    "file_key": "CL06nJNn5eZLQKDoARMND5",    "file_name": "Developer page mockup demo",    "passcode": "secretpasscode",    "timestamp": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "webhook_id": "22"  } |
| ### FILE\_VERSION\_UPDATE[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-version-update-event "Direct link to FILE_VERSION_UPDATE")<br><br>Triggers whenever a user creates a named version in the version history of a file.<br><br>This is useful for workflow integrations. For example, suppose you have a Figma document with icon assets. When the design for an asset is updated and ready to publish, you can have a member of the team tag that version in the version history and then use this webhook event to generate and deploy your new asset version. | `event_type`String<br><br>The type of the event, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `FILE_VERSION_UPDATE`<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The key of the file that was updated.<br><br>`file_name`String<br><br>The name of the file that was updated.<br><br>`created_at`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the version was created.<br><br>`version_id`String<br><br>ID of the published version.<br><br>`label`String<br><br>Label of the version in the version history.<br><br>`description`String<br><br>Description of the version in the version history.<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided.<br><br>`timestamp`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.<br><br>`triggered_by`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user that deleted the file and triggered this event.<br><br>`webhook_id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook that caused the callback.<br><br>**Example:**<br><br>      {    "created_at": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "event_type": "FILE_VERSION_UPDATE",    "file_key": "CL06nJNn5eZLQKDoARMND5",    "file_name": "Developer page mockup demo",    "version_id": "443",    "label": "Added new documentation!",    "description": "Wrote new documentation for webhooks v2",    "passcode": "secretpasscode",    "timestamp": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "triggered_by": {      "id": "813845097374535682",      "handle": "Dylan Field"    },    "webhook_id": "22"  } |
| ### LIBRARY\_PUBLISH[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#library-publish-event "Direct link to LIBRARY_PUBLISH")<br><br>Triggers whenever a library file is published.<br><br>This is useful for workflow integrations. This webhook could integrate with Slack, Asana or Jira, notifying designers to modify their assets when new components are published.<br><br>Note: depending on the type and number of assets that were published, a single publication may be delivered via multiple, separate `LIBRARY_PUBLISH` webhook events. There will be a separate `LIBRARY_PUBLISH` event for each type of published library asset (components, styles, and/or variables). Additionally, publications with a large number of assets (typically hundreds) will also be split into multiple separate events. | `event_type`String<br><br>The type of the event, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `LIBRARY_PUBLISH`<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The key of the file that was published.<br><br>`file_name`String<br><br>The name of the file that was published.<br><br>`description`String<br><br>Description of the library publish.<br><br>`created_components`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Components that were created by the library publish.<br><br>`created_styles`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Styles that were created by the library publish.<br><br>`created_variables`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Variables that were created by the library publish.<br><br>`modified_components`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Components that were modified by the library publish.<br><br>`modified_styles`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Styles that were modified by the library publish.<br><br>`modified_variables`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Variables that were modified by the library publish.<br><br>`deleted_components`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Components that were deleted by the library publish.<br><br>`deleted_styles`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Styles that were deleted by the library publish.<br><br>`deleted_variables`[LibraryItemData](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#libraryitemdata-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Variables that were deleted by the library publish.<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided.<br><br>`timestamp`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.<br><br>`triggered_by`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user that published the file and triggered this event.<br><br>`webhook_id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook that caused the callback.<br><br>**Example:**<br><br>      {    "event_type": "LIBRARY_PUBLISH",    "file_key": "zH44k2FUM629Fa4EMShiHL",    "file_name": "Mockup library",    "description": "Adjusted colors to match guidelines",    "created_components": [      {        "key": "ed26a50a541c7e6828420a68707eb49812bda2d3",        "name": "Simple button"      }    ],    "created_styles": [      {        "key": "a68707eb49ed26828420812bda2d36a50a541c7e",        "name": "Coral green"      }    ],    "modified_components": [      {        "key": "eacab7bc887f50d99d73ef0eaf6716d5dae0855f",        "name": "Date picker"      }    ],    "modified_styles": [      {        "key": "50d99d73ef0ab7bc887f716d5dae0855feaf6eac",        "name": "Background fill"      }    ],    "deleted_components": [      {        "key": "0a575f0b60f7238d9f7da3e8d57b99ab4451f28f",        "name": "Slider"      }    ],    "deleted_styles": [      {        "key": "0b60f0a577b99ab4451f25f7238d9f7da3e8d58d",        "name": "Header text"      }    ],    "passcode": "secretpassword",    "timestamp": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "triggered_by": {      "id": "813845097374535682",      "handle": "Dylan Field"    },    "webhook_id": "22"  } |
| ### FILE\_COMMENT[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-comment-event "Direct link to FILE_COMMENT")<br><br>Triggers whenever a user comments on a file.<br><br>This webhook could integrate with Asana or Jira and automatically create tasks whenever a user comments. It could also integrate with Slack and notify mentioned users.<br><br>The contents of a comment is returned in an array of [CommentFragment](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#commentfragment-type)<br>. A `CommentFragment` either contains a text or mention field. A text field contains raw comment text with formatting derivatives, newlines will be encoded as `\n`, etc.<br><br>Text fields can also contain emojis, which are always encoded as a separate fragment and are surrounded by two `:` characters. Mention fields occur if a user types `@OtherUser` in their comment. A mention contains the user id of the mentioned user. All mentioned users are added to the mentioned array field, where additional user information is included. | `event_type`String<br><br>The type of the event, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `FILE_COMMENT`<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The key of the file that was updated.<br><br>`file_name`String<br><br>The name of the file that was updated.<br><br>`comment`[CommentFragment](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#commentfragment-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Contents of the comment itself.<br><br>`comment_id`Number<br><br>Unique identifier for comment.<br><br>`mentions`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>Users that were mentioned in the comment.<br><br>`order_id`Number<br><br>Only set for top level comments. The number displayed with the comment in the UI.<br><br>`parent_id`Number<br><br>If present, the id of the comment to which this is the reply.<br><br>`created_at`String<br><br>The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the comment was left.<br><br>`resolved_at`String<br><br>If set, the UTC ISO 8601 time the comment was resolved.<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided.<br><br>`timestamp`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.<br><br>`triggered_by`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user that made the comment and triggered this event.<br><br>`webhook_id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook that caused the callback.<br><br>**Example:**<br><br>      {    "event_type": "FILE_COMMENT",    "file_key": "zH44k2FUM629Fa4EMShiHL",    "file_name": "Mockup library",    "comment": [      {        "text": "TODO: \n"      },      {        "mention": "811724164054158337"      },      {        "text": "Change selection colors"      },    ],    "comment_id": "32",    "mentions": [      {        "id": "811724164054158337",        "handle": "Evan Wallace"      }    ],    "order_id": "23 ",    "parent_id": "",    "created_at": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "resolved_at": "",    "passcode": "secretpasscode",    "timestamp": "2020-02-23T20:27:16Z",    "triggered_by": {      "id": "813845097374535682",      "handle": "Dylan Field"    },    "webhook_id": "22"  } |
| ### DEV\_MODE\_STATUS\_UPDATE[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#dev-mode-status-update-event "Direct link to DEV_MODE_STATUS_UPDATE")<br><br>Triggers whenever the Dev Mode status changes for a layer in a file.<br><br>This can be useful if, for example, you want to create an integration that tracks the status of designs that are being worked on in your organization.<br><br>The event includes the Dev Mode `status`, the `file_key` and `node_id` of the layer where the status changed, and the user that made the change. It also includes any related links (Dev resources) that are applied to the layer in the file. | `event_type`String<br><br>The type of the event, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `DEV_MODE_STATUS_UPDATE`<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The key of the file where the change occurred.<br><br>`node_id`String<br><br>The id of the node where the Dev Mode status changed. For example, `43:2`.<br><br>`status`String<br><br>The new Dev Mode status, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `NONE`<br>*   `READY_FOR_DEV`<br>*   `COMPLETED`<br><br>`change_message`String<br><br>If one was provided when the status was updated, a message describing the change.<br><br>`related_links`[DevResource](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#devresource-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>An array of related links that have been applied to the layer in the file.<br><br>`passcode`String<br><br>The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided.<br><br>`timestamp`String<br><br>UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.<br><br>`triggered_by`[User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/users-types/#user-type)<br><br>The user that made the change and triggered this event.<br><br>`webhook_id`Number<br><br>The id of the webhook that caused the callback.<br><br>**Example:**<br><br>      {    "event_type": "DEV_MODE_STATUS_UPDATE",    "file_key": "ABzTs1A2aFSy960zBI3nMM",    "node_id": "43:2",    "status": "READY_FOR_DEV",    "change_message": "New rectangle",    "related_links": [      {        "id": 1118075899259441212,        "name": "Issue BB-8",        "url": "https://example.atlassian.net/jira/core/projects/BB/board?groupBy=status&selectedIssue=BB-8",        "node_id": "43:2",        "file_key": "ABzTs1A2aFSy960zBI3nMM"      }    ],    "passcode": "secretpasscode",    "timestamp": "2025-05-14T23:28:40Z",    "triggered_by": {      "id": "813845097374535682",      "handle": "Dylan Field"    },    "webhook_id": "434"  } |

*   [PING](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#ping-event)
    
*   [FILE\_UPDATE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-update-event)
    
*   [FILE\_DELETE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-delete-event)
    
*   [FILE\_VERSION\_UPDATE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-version-update-event)
    
*   [LIBRARY\_PUBLISH](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#library-publish-event)
    
*   [FILE\_COMMENT](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#file-comment-event)
    
*   [DEV\_MODE\_STATUS\_UPDATE](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-events/#dev-mode-status-update-event)

# Endpoints | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

You can use the following Webhooks via the Figma API:

POST webhook[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-post-endpoint "Direct link to POST webhook")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create a new webhook which will call the specified endpoint when the event triggers. By default, this webhook will automatically send a `PING` event to the endpoint when it is created. If this behavior is not desired, you can create the webhook and set the status to `PAUSED` and reactivate it later.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`webhooks:write` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`POST /v2/webhooks`

**Return value**

The [WebhookV2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2-type)
 that was successfully posted

| Body parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| event\_type | [WebhookV2Event](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhookv2event-type)<br><br>The type of event that will trigger this webhook to fire. |
| context | String<br><br>Context to create the webhook for, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `team`<br>*   `project`<br>*   `file` |
| context\_id | String<br><br>The id of the context you want to receive updates about. |
| endpoint | String<br><br>The HTTP endpoint that will receive a `POST` request when the event triggers. Max length 2048 characters. |
| passcode | String<br><br>String that will be passed back to your webhook endpoint to verify that it is being called by Figma. Max length 100 characters. |
| status | [WebhookV2Status](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2status-type)<br>optional<br><br>State of the webhook, including any error state it may be in. |
| description | Stringoptional<br><br>User provided description or name for the webhook. Max length 150 characters. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Required parameter missing or invalid |
| 403 | The developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired |

GET webhook by id[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-by-id-endpoint "Direct link to GET webhook by id")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Returns the [WebhookV2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2-type)
 corresponding to the ID provided, if it exists.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`webhooks:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v2/webhooks/:webhook_id`

**Return value**

The [WebhookV2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2-type)
 requested

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| webhook\_id | String<br><br>The id of the webhook you want to query for |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 403 | The developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired |
| 404 | Webhook does not exist or you do not have permissions to access this webhook |

Get webhooks[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-endpoint "Direct link to Get webhooks")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Returns a list of webhooks corresponding to the context or plan provided, if they exist. For plan, the webhooks for all contexts that you have access to will be returned, and the response is paginated.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`webhooks:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-2 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v2/webhooks`

**Return value**

    {  "webhooks": WebhookV2[]  "pagination": {    "next_page": String (optional)    "prev_page": String (optional)  }}

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| context | String<br><br>Context to get webhooks for, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `team`<br>*   `project`<br>*   `file` |
| context\_id | String<br><br>The id of the context that you want to get attached webhooks for. If you're using `context_id`, you cannot use `plan_api_id`. |
| plan\_api\_id | String<br><br>The id of your plan. Use this to get all webhooks for all contexts you have access to. If you're using `plan_api_id`, you cannot use `context` or `context_id`. When you use `plan_api_id`, the response is paginated. |
| cursor | String<br><br>If you're using `plan_api_id`, this is the cursor to use for pagination. If you're using `context` or `context_id`, this parameter is ignored. Provide the `next_page` or `prev_page` value from the previous response to get the next or previous page of results. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 403 | The developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired |
| 404 | Webhook does not exist or you do not have permissions to access this webhook |

PUT webhook[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-put-endpoint "Direct link to PUT webhook")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Updates the webhook with the specified properties.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`webhooks:write` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-3 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`PUT /v2/webhooks/:webhook_id`

**Return value**

The [WebhookV2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2-type)
 that was successfully updated

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| webhook\_id | String<br><br>The id of the webhook you want to update. |

| Body parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| event\_type | [WebhookV2Event](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhookv2event-type)<br>optional<br><br>The type of event that will trigger this webhook to fire. |
| endpoint | Stringoptional<br><br>The HTTP endpoint that will receive a POST request when the event triggers. |
| passcode | Stringoptional<br><br>String that will be passed back to your webhook endpoint to verify that it is being called by Figma. |
| status | [WebhookV2Status](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2status-type)<br>optional<br><br>State to put the webhook in, as a string enum. The webhook cannot be put into an error state this way. |
| description | Stringoptional<br><br>User provided description or name for the webhook. Max length 140 characters. Providing a description '' (empty string) will delete the description. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Required parameter missing or invalid |
| 403 | The developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired |
| 404 | Webhook does not exist or you do not have permissions to access this webhook |

DELETE webhook[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-delete-endpoint "Direct link to DELETE webhook")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deletes the specified webhook. This operation cannot be reversed.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`webhooks:write` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-4 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`DELETE /v2/webhooks/:webhook_id`

**Return value**

The [WebhookV2](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-types/#webhookv2-type)
 that was successfully deleted

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| webhook\_id | String<br><br>The id of the webhook you want to delete. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Required parameter missing or invalid |
| 403 | The developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired |
| 404 | Webhook does not exist or you do not have permissions to access this webhook |

GET team webhooks[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-teams-endpoint "Direct link to GET team webhooks")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

\[DEPRECATED\] Returns all webhooks registered under the specified team. This endpoing is being deprecated. Instead, use the [GET /v2/webhooks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-endpoint)
 endpoint with context set to `team` and `context_id` set to the team id you want to query for. For example, requests to `GET v2/webhooks/:team_id/webhook` should be replaced with `GET v2/webhooks?context=team&context_id=:team_id`.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`webhooks:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-5 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v2/teams/:team_id/webhooks`

**Return value**

    {  "webhooks": WebhookV2[]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| team\_id | String<br><br>The team id you wish to query for. |

GET webhook requests[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-requests-endpoint "Direct link to GET webhook requests")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Returns all webhook requests sent within the last week. Useful for debugging.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`webhooks:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-6 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v2/webhooks/:webhook_id/requests`

**Return value**

    {  "requests": WebhookV2Request[]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| webhook\_id | String<br><br>The id of the webhook subscription you want to see events from. |

*   [POST webhook](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-post-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint)
        
*   [GET webhook by id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-by-id-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1)
        
*   [Get webhooks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-get-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-2)
        
*   [PUT webhook](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-put-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-3)
        
*   [DELETE webhook](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-delete-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-4)
        
*   [GET team webhooks](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#webhooks-v2-teams-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/webhooks-endpoints/#http-endpoint-5)

# Endpoints | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Components and styles endpoints allow several ways to get information about published components and styles in a team library. You can get a list of components or styles using a team\_id, or a specific component or style using a key.

GET team components[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-team-components-endpoint "Direct link to GET team components")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get a paginated list of published components within a team library.:::info This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`team_library_content:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
. :::

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/teams/:team_id/components`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {    "components": [      {       "key": String,       "file_key": String,       "node_id": String,       "thumbnail_url": String,       "name": String,       "description": String,       "updated_at": String,       "created_at": String,       "user": User,       "containing_frame": FrameInfo,      },      ...    ],    "cursor": {      "before": Number,      "after": Number,    },   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| team\_id | String<br><br>ID of the team to list components from. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| page\_size | Numberdefault: 30<br><br>Number of items in a paged list of results. Maximum of 1000. |
| after | Number<br><br>Cursor indicating which id after which to start retrieving components for. Exclusive with before. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids. |
| before | Number<br><br>Cursor indicating which id before which to start retrieving components for. Exclusive with after. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET file components[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-file-components-endpoint "Direct link to GET file components")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get a list of published components within a file library.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_content:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/files/:file_key/components`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {    "components": [      {       "key": String,       "file_key": String,       "node_id": String,       "thumbnail_url": String,       "name": String,       "description": String,       "updated_at": String,       "created_at": String,       "user": User,       "containing_frame": FrameInfo,      },      ...    ],   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| file\_key | String<br><br>File to list components from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key, as it is not possible to publish from branches. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET component[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-component-endpoint "Direct link to GET component")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get metadata on a component by key.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_assets:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-2 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/components/:key`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {     "key": String,     "file_key": String,     "node_id": String,     "thumbnail_url": String,     "name": String,     "description": String,     "updated_at": String,     "created_at": String,     "user": User,     "containing_frame": FrameInfo,   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| key | String<br><br>The unique identifier of the component. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET team component sets[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-team-component-sets-endpoint "Direct link to GET team component sets")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get a paginated list of published component sets within a team library.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`team_library_content:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-3 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/teams/:team_id/component_sets`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {    "component_sets": [      {       "key": String,       "file_key": String,       "node_id": String,       "thumbnail_url": String,       "name": String,       "description": String,       "updated_at": String,       "created_at": String,       "user": User,       "containing_frame": FrameInfo,      },      ...    ],    "cursor": {      "before": Number,      "after": Number,    },   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| team\_id | String<br><br>ID of the team to list component sets from. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| page\_size | Numberdefault: 30<br><br>Number of items in a paged list of results. |
| after | Number<br><br>Cursor indicating which id after which to start retrieving components for. Exclusive with before. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids. |
| before | Number<br><br>Cursor indicating which id before which to start retrieving components for. Exclusive with after. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET file component sets[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-file-component-sets-endpoint "Direct link to GET file component sets")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get a list of published component sets within a file library.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_content:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-4 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/files/:file_key/component_sets`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {    "component_sets": [      {       "key": String,       "file_key": String,       "node_id": String,       "thumbnail_url": String,       "name": String,       "description": String,       "updated_at": String,       "created_at": String,       "user": User,       "containing_frame": FrameInfo,      },      ...    ],   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| file\_key | String<br><br>File to list component sets from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key, as it is not possible to publish from branches. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET component set[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-component-set-endpoint "Direct link to GET component set")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get metadata on a component set by key.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_assets:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-5 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/component_sets/:key`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {     "key": String,     "file_key": String,     "node_id": String,     "thumbnail_url": String,     "name": String,     "description": String,     "updated_at": String,     "created_at": String,     "user": User,     "containing_frame": FrameInfo,   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| key | String<br><br>The unique identifier of the component set. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET team styles[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-team-styles-endpoint "Direct link to GET team styles")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get a paginated list of published styles within a team library.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`team_library_content:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-6 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/teams/:team_id/styles`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {    "styles": [      {       "key": String,       "file_key": String,       "node_id": String,       "style_type": StyleType,       "thumbnail_url": String,       "name": String,       "description": String,       "updated_at": String,       "created_at": String,       "sort_position": String,       "user": User,       },      ...    ],    "cursor": {      "before": Number,      "after": Number,    },   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| team\_id | String<br><br>ID of the team to list styles from. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| page\_size | Numberdefault: 30<br><br>Number of items in a paged list of results. |
| after | Number<br><br>Cursor indicating which id after which to start retrieving styles for. Exclusive with before. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids. |
| before | Number<br><br>Cursor indicating which id before which to start retrieving styles for. Exclusive with after. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET file styles[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-file-styles-endpoint "Direct link to GET file styles")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get a list of published styles within a file library.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_content:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

`GET /v1/files/:file_key/styles`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {    "styles": [      {       "key": String,       "file_key": String,       "node_id": String,       "style_type": StyleType,       "thumbnail_url": String,       "name": String,       "description": String,       "updated_at": String,       "created_at": String,       "sort_position": String,       "user": User,       },      ...    ],   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| file\_key | String<br><br>File to list styles from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key, as it is not possible to publish from branches. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

GET style[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-style-endpoint "Direct link to GET style")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get metadata on a style by key.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_assets:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP Endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-8 "Direct link to HTTP Endpoint")

`GET /v1/styles/:key`

**Return value**

    {  "status": Number,  "error": Boolean,  "meta": {     "key": String,     "file_key": String,     "node_id": String,     "style_type": StyleType,     "thumbnail_url": String,     "name": String,     "description": String,     "updated_at": String,     "created_at": String,     "sort_position": String,     "user": User,   },}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| key | String<br><br>The unique identifier of the style. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Error with the request. The `message` param on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | Insufficient permission on the team. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | Requested resource was not found. |

*   [GET team components](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-team-components-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint)
        
*   [GET file components](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-file-components-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1)
        
*   [GET component](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-component-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-2)
        
*   [GET team component sets](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-team-component-sets-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-3)
        
*   [GET file component sets](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-file-component-sets-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-4)
        
*   [GET component set](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-component-set-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-5)
        
*   [GET team styles](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-team-styles-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-6)
        
*   [GET file styles](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-file-styles-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-7)
        
*   [GET style](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#get-style-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP Endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/component-endpoints/#http-endpoint-8)

# The Typings File | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/typings/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Here’s a sample tsconfig.json to get you started. Widgets created from the default templates will already have this included:

    {   "compilerOptions": {      "jsx": "react",      "jsxFactory": "figma.widget.h",      "jsxFragmentFactory": "figma.widget.Fragment",      "target": "es6",      "strict": true,      "typeRoots": [         "./node_modules/@types",         "./node_modules/@figma"      ]   }}

You’ll also need to install widget and plugins typings from npm

    npm install --save-dev @figma/widget-typings @figma/plugin-typings

# Widget Manifest | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-manifest/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Each widget must define a `manifest.json` file. If you use the "Create new widget" option, Figma will automatically create a simple manifest for you.

Here’s an example of a widget manifest file:

info

This file is similar to the [plugin manifest](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/manifest/)
 with the additional `containsWidget` and `widgetApi` properties and without plugin-specific options like menus or relaunchButtons.

manifest.json

    {  "name": "MyWidget",  "id": "737805260747778093",  "api": "1.0.0",  "widgetApi": "1.0.0",  "editorType": ["figma", "figjam"],  "containsWidget": true,  "main": "code.js",  "ui": "ui.html",  "documentAccess": "dynamic-page",  "networkAccess": {    "allowedDomains": ["none"]  }}

### name: string

The name of the widget, as it will appear in the menu.

* * *

### id?: string

The widget ID to publish updates to. This ID will be assigned to you by Figma and is typically obtained using the "Create new Widget" feature, which will generate a manifest file with a new `id`. You can also get a new widget ID at the time of publishing your widget.

* * *

### widgetApi: string

The version of the widget API used by the widget. The current widgetApi version is `"1.0.0"`. In the future, when there are more versions of the widget API, this will be used to ensure stability across widget versions. See [Stability and Updates](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/stability-and-updates/)
 for more information about updates to the api.

**We recommend updating to the latest version whenever possible** to get the latest features, bug fixes, and documentation.

* * *

### containsWidget: true

All widget manifests should have this set to true.

* * *

### editorType: ('figma' | 'figjam')\[\]

This allows you to specify the editor that your widget is intended for and will impact which editor your widget appears in.

The types we support currently are:

    "editorType": ["figma"]"editorType": ["figjam"]"editorType": ["figma", "figjam"]

* * *

### main: string

The relative file path to the JavaScript code of your widget.

* * *

### ui?: string | { \[key: string\]: string }

Used to specify HTML file/files that can be used in the iframe modal via `figma.showUI`, if you choose to have one.

*   If a single string is specified, this is the relative file path to the HTML file whose contents will be available as a string in the Javascript code via the constant [`__html__`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/api-reference/#html)
    .
*   If a map is specified, each entry of the map will be available at [`__uiFiles__`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/api/api-reference/#uifiles)
    

* * *

### documentAccess?: 'dynamic-page'

This field ensures the widget supports dynamic page loading. The field is required for all new widgets and the value must be `dynamic-page`.

*   If the manifest field is included, then the widget will run immediatley.
*   If the manifest field is not included, then when the widget is run or interacted with for the first time after a user opens a file, the entire file will load and Figma will show a "Loading n pages for widget…" notification, where `n` is the number of pages being loaded.

If your existing widget's manifest doesn't include this field, you should [migrate your widget to use dynamic page loading](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/migrating-to-dynamic-loading/)
.

note

This field is required for all new widgets and new versions of widgets.

* * *

### networkAccess?: NetworkAccess

caution

This property is currently in public beta and is subject to change.

Used to specify the list of domains that your widget is permitted to access. When `networkAccess` is used:

*   Your widget can only access the domains that you specify. If your widget attempts to access other domains, the widget is prevented from doing so.
    
    note
    
    The enforcement of network access is limited only to requests made by the widget, such as requests to a public API. In a situation such as a widget rendering a website in a frame, network access limits only apply to the website's domain. Network access limits don’t affect the website’s resources.
    
*   The list of domains that your widget can access is displayed on your widget's Community page.
    

`networkAccess` has the following properties:

*   `allowedDomains` is a required list of strings. The strings are match patterns for domains that your widget is permitted to access. If `networkAccess` is used, `allowedDomains` must include at least one pattern. Optionally, patterns can start with one of the permitted schemes: `http`, `https`, `ws`, or `wss`. The `*` character may be used as a wildcard for subdomains, or used to represent all domains.
*   `reasoning` is a usually-optional string that describes why your widget needs to access the allowed domains. `reasoning` is required if:
    *   Your `allowedDomains` list includes `"*"`
    *   Your `allowedDomains` list includes local or development servers. If you only need local or development servers for development, please use `devAllowedDomains` instead.
*   `devAllowedDomains` is an optional list of strings. The strings are match patterns for domains that your widget is permitted to access during development. You can use most of the same patterns in `devAllowedDomains` as you can in `allowedDomains`.

Valid patterns for `allowedDomains`:

*   `["none"]`: The widget cannot access any external network resources. Note that we preload the Inter font for use in widgets, so you don't need to include an Inter font source in your allowedDomains.

Valid patterns for `allowedDomains` and `devAllowedDomains`:

*   `["*"]`: The widget may access any external network resources. If this pattern is included in `allowedDomains`, the `reasoning` property is required.
*   `["*.example.com"]`: The `*` character can be used to permit all subdomains of a given domain.
*   `["http://example.com", "https://example.com", "ws://example.com", "wss://example.com"]`: `http`, `https`, `ws`, and `wss` are permitted schemes that can be used to prefix domains. Other schemes, such as `file`, cannot be used.
*   `["api.example.com/rest/get", "www.example.com/images/", "http://s3.amazonaws.com/example_bucket/"]`: URLs to specific resources can be used.
*   `["example.com", "figma.com"]`: Domains can be used without schemes, subdomains, or wildcards.
*   `["http://localhost", "https://localhost", "http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:8081"]`: During development, the widget can access a local/development web server. If the server is running on a port other than port 80, the port number can be provided after the URL. If this pattern is included in `allowedDomains`, the `reasoning` property is required.

note

In the previous example, the trailing slash in `www.example.com/images/` identifies a path to multiple resources. For example, `www.example.com/images/` lets a widget access images at `www.example.com/images/img1.png` and `www.example.com/images/img2.png`.

A pattern with _no_ trailing slash blocks any deeper access to files on that path. For example, `api.example.com/rest/get` stops a widget from accessing `api.example.com/rest/get/exampleresource.json`. This can be useful, for example, if your widget uses a REST API endpoint and you want to restrict the widget to that endpoint only.

For example:

    "networkAccess": {  "allowedDomains": [    "figma.com",    "*.google.com",    "https://my-app.cdn.com",    "wss://socket.io",    "example.com/api/",    "exact-path.com/content"  ],  "devAllowedDomains": [    "http://localhost:3000"  ]}

More formally,

    interface NetworkAccess {  allowedDomains: string[]  reasoning?: string  devAllowedDomains: string[]}

* * *

### build?: string

**Experimental!** A shell command to run before we load the file specified in `main` and `ui`. This can be used to call build commands such as compiling with Typescript, running Webpack, etc. The command is run in the directory of the manifest.

* * *

Plugin API Specific Options[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-manifest/#plugin-api-specific-options "Direct link to Plugin API Specific Options")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here are some plugin specific manifest options that are also applicable to widgets:

### api: string

The version of the Figma Plugin API used by the widget. **We recommend updating to the latest version whenever possible** to get the latest features, bug fixes, and documentation.

* * *

### permissions?: PermissionType\[\]

This allows you to specify what permissions your widget wants access to.

    type PermissionType = "currentuser" | "activeusers"

info

`activeuser` must be specified if your widget uses `figma.activeUser` and `currentuser` must be specified if your widget uses `figma.currentUser`.

* * *

### enableProposedApi?: boolean

See [Plugin Proposed API](https://developers.figma.com/docs/plugins/proposed-api/)
.

caution

This flag is only meant for development, and will not work in published widgets!

* * *

### enablePrivatePluginApi?: boolean

This enables Plugin API that's specific to private widgets. Setting this will also enable local widgets to work with these APIs during development.

* * *

*   [Plugin API Specific Options](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-manifest/#plugin-api-specific-options)

# <Image /> | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

An `Image` is essentially syntactic sugar for a [`Rectangle`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Rectangle/)
 with an image fill. Instead of the fill prop, it has a `src` prop, that can be either a URL string or an ImagePaint. If a URL string is passed that will be used to create an ImagePaint.

Usage Example[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#usage-example "Direct link to Usage Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Usage example

    <Image  // Pass a data uri directly as the image  src="data:image/png;base64,"  width={100}  height={100}/>

### src: string | [ImagePaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-ImagePaint/#image-paint)

A string representing Image URL/DataURI or an ImagePaint.

* * *

`SizeProps` (Required)[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#sizeprops-required "Direct link to sizeprops-required")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### width: [Size](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Size/#size)

The width of the component. This is required.

* * *

### height: [Size](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Size/#size)

The height of the component. This is required.

* * *

### minWidth?: number

The minWidth of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

### maxWidth?: number

The maxWidth of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

### minHeight?: number

The minHeight of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

### maxHeight?: number

The maxHeight of this component. Only affects AutoLayout and its children.

* * *

### rotation?: number

The rotation of the node in degrees. Expects values from -180 to 180.

The rotation is with respect to the top-left of the object. Therefore, it is independent from the position of the object.

* * *

`CornerProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#cornerprops "Direct link to cornerprops")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### cornerRadius?: [CornerRadius](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-CornerRadius/#corner-radius)

The number of pixels to round the corners of the object by.

This value must be non-negative and can be fractional. If an edge length is less than twice the corner radius, the corner radius for each vertex of the edge will be clamped to half the edge length.

info

Rectangle nodes can also have different corner radii on each of the four corners.

* * *

`GeometryProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#geometryprops "Direct link to geometryprops")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### fill?: HexCode | [Color](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Color/#color)
 | [Paint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Paint/#paint)
 | ([SolidPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-SolidPaint/#solid-paint)
 | [GradientPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-GradientPaint/#gradient-paint)
)\[\]

The paints used to fill the area of the node

* * *

### stroke?: HexCode | [Color](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Color/#color)
 | [SolidPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-SolidPaint/#solid-paint)
 | [GradientPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-GradientPaint/#gradient-paint)
 | ([SolidPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-SolidPaint/#solid-paint)
 | [GradientPaint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-GradientPaint/#gradient-paint)
)\[\]

The paints used to fill the area of the node's stroke.

* * *

### strokeWidth?: number

The thickness of the stroke, in pixels. This value must be non-negative and can be fractional.

* * *

### strokeAlign?: [StrokeAlign](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-StrokeAlign/#stroke-align)

The alignment of the stroke with respect to the boundaries of the node.

Center-aligned stroke means the center of the stroke falls exactly on the geometry. Inside-aligned stroke shifts the stroke so it lies completely inside the shape, and outside-aligned stroke is vice versa.

info

Inside and outside stroke are actually implemented by doubling the stroke weight and masking the stroke by the fill. This means inside-aligned stroke will never draw strokes outside the fill and outside-aligned stroke will never draw strokes inside the fill.

* * *

### strokeDashPattern?: number\[\]

The alternating stroke dash and gap lengths, in pixels. An empty array gives a solid stroke and a single value will be applied to both the dash and gap length.

* * *

`BaseProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#baseprops "Direct link to baseprops")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### name?: string

The name of the component. This is useful to specify a data-layer attribute to make things more debuggable when you inspect the sublayers of your widget.

* * *

### hidden?: boolean

Toggles whether to show the component.

* * *

### onClick?: (event: [WidgetClickEvent](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-WidgetClickEvent/#widget-click-event)
) => Promise<any> | void

Attach a click handler on the given node. If the given function is async or returns a promise, the widget is only terminated when the async function has completed and the promise has been resolved. The click handler is also passed a [`WidgetClickEvent`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-WidgetClickEvent/)
 object that contains additional information about the click.

See also: [Handling User Events](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/handling-user-events/)
.

* * *

### key?: string | number

The key of the component.

* * *

### hoverStyle?: [HoverStyle](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-HoverStyle/#hover-style)

The style to be applied when the mouse is hovering over the component.

* * *

### tooltip?: string

The tooltip that is shown to the user when hovering over the component.

* * *

### positioning?: 'auto' | 'absolute'

This value is ignored unless the node is a direct child of an AutoLayout frame.

| value | description |
| --- | --- |
| 'auto' | Layout this node according to auto-layout rules. |
| 'absolute' | Take this node out of the auto-layout flow, while still nesting it inside the auto-layout frame. This allows explicitly setting `x`, `y`, `width`, and `height`. |

* * *

### `BlendProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#blendprops "Direct link to blendprops")

### blendMode?: [BlendMode](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-BlendMode/#blend-mode)

The blendMode of the component.

* * *

### opacity?: number

The opacity of the component.

* * *

### effect?: [Effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Effect/#effect)
 | [Effect](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Effect/#effect)
\[\]

The effect of the component.

* * *

### `ConstraintProps`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#constraintprops "Direct link to constraintprops")

### x?: number | [HorizontalConstraint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Constraint/#horizontal-constraint)

The x position of the node.

This value is ignored if the node is a child of an AutoLayout frame and has positioning set to 'auto'.

* * *

### y?: number | [VerticalConstraint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/type-Constraint/#vertical-constraint)

The y position of the node.

This value is ignored if the node is a child of an AutoLayout frame and has positioning set to 'auto'.

* * *

Default Props[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#default-props "Direct link to Default Props")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Prop | Default |
| --- | --- |
| `name` | `""` |
| `hidden` | `false` |
| `x` | `0` |
| `y` | `0` |
| `blendMode` | `"pass-through"` |
| `opacity` | `1` |
| `effect` | `[]` |
| `stroke` | `[]` |
| `strokeWidth` | `1` |
| `strokeAlign` | `"inside"` |
| `rotation` | `0` |
| `cornerRadius` | `0` |

*   [Usage Example](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#usage-example)
    
*   [`SizeProps` (Required)](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#sizeprops-required)
    
*   [`CornerProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#cornerprops)
    
*   [`GeometryProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#geometryprops)
    
*   [`BaseProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#baseprops)
    *   [`BlendProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#blendprops)
        
    *   [`ConstraintProps`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#constraintprops)
        
*   [Default Props](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Image/#default-props)
