# Endpoints | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

There are six methods provided by the Library Analytics REST API. Components, styles, and variables each have an actions endpoint and a usages endpoint.

[GET component actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-component-actions-endpoint)
: Get time series data about what actions your users have performed with your library's components on a week-by-week basis.

[GET component usages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-component-usages-endpoint)
: Get summary information about how many instances of a library's components exist across all files.

[GET styles actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-styles-actions-endpoint)
: Get time series data about what actions your users have performed with your library's styles on a week-by-week basis.

[GET styles usages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-styles-usages-endpoint)
: Get summary information about how many instances of a library's styles exist across all files.

[GET variables actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-variables-actions-endpoint)
: Get time series data about what actions your users have performed with your library's variables on a week-by-week basis.

[GET variables usages](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-variables-usages-endpoint)
: Get summary information about how many instances of a library's variables exist across all files.

GET component actions[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-component-actions-endpoint "Direct link to GET component actions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `/v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/component/actions` endpoint provides historical time series data regarding how a design system library is used. The data is returned in descending order, from most recent to least recent. Action data can be returned broken down in different ways using the required `group_by` query parameter. Data is available for up to one year.

### Types of events[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#types-of-events "Direct link to Types of events")

We track two types of events:

*   `insertions`: Tracks how many times a component in the library was inserted into a document. This can happen in many ways, including: duplicating an existing instance, copy pasting the instance from another document, or inserting the component from the asset panel.
*   `detachments`: Tracks how many times a component in the library was detached.

### Data sparseness[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#data-sparseness "Direct link to Data sparseness")

Only weeks containing data are returned. Teams and components only appear when there are associated actions.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_analytics:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#http-endpoint "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`GET /v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/component/actions`

**Return value**

Actions grouped by component

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "component_key": String,      "week": String,      "detachments": Number,      "insertions": Number,      "component_name": String,      "component_set_key": String,      "component_set_name": String    },    ...  ]}

Actions grouped by team

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "week": String,      "detachments": Number,      "insertions": Number,      "team_name": String,      "workspace_name": String    },    ...  ]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library\_file\_key | Stringrequired<br><br>File key of the library to fetch analytics data for. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| group\_by | Stringrequired<br><br>Controls how action data is organized in the response.<br><br>*   `component` returns actions broken down by each component within the library.<br>*   `team` returns actions broken down by the team using the library. |
| start\_date | String<br><br>Filters the response to include time series data that occurs after the start date (inclusive). If unset, returns the prior year.<br><br>Accepts an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). For example: `2023-12-03`. Dates are rounded back to the previous start of a week. For example, if you supply a Wednesday, you will get data starting on the preceding Sunday 00:00 UTC time. |
| end\_date | String<br><br>Filters the response to include time series data that that occurs before the end date (inclusive). If unset, returns up to the last full week.<br><br>Accepts an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). For example: `2023-12-03`. Dates are rounded forward in time to the nearest end of a week. |
| cursor | String<br><br>Cursor for pagination. Used to retrieve the next page of data. Obtained from the response of the previous call. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | API is not available. Possible error messages are `Limited by Figma plan`, `Incorrect account type`, or `Invalid scope`. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |

GET component usages[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-component-usages-endpoint "Direct link to GET component usages")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `/v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/component/usages` endpoint provides data on how components are used across different files and teams. This data can be broken down by component or file.

### Components and files[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#components-and-files "Direct link to Components and files")

The endpoint can group usage by:

*   `component`: Returns a list of all the components with usage in the library (unused components are excluded). The response includes the name of the component, the number of files and teams using the component, and the total number of existing instances of the component.
*   `file`: Returns a list of all the files that use the library. The response includes the team name, the file name, and the total number of times that resources from the library are used. Files that you don't have access to are returned as `File not visible`. A total count of instances used in those files is also included in the response.

### Data sparseness[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#data-sparseness-1 "Direct link to Data sparseness")

Rows only include teams or components with data.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_analytics:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`GET /v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/component/usages`

**Return value**

Usage grouped by component

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "component_key": String,      "usages": Number      "teams_using": Number,      "files_using": Number,      "component_name": String,      "component_set_key": String,      "component_set_name": String    },    ...  ]}

Usage grouped by file

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "usages": Number,      "team_name": String,      "workspace_name": String,      "file_name": String    },    ...  ]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library\_file\_key | Stringrequired<br><br>File key of the library to fetch analytics data for. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| group\_by | Stringrequired<br><br>Controls how usage data is organized in the response.<br><br>*   `component` returns usages broken down by each component within the library.<br>*   `file` returns usages broken down by each file and team that used the library. |
| cursor | String<br><br>Cursor for pagination. Used to retrieve the next page of data. Obtained from the response of the previous call. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | API is not available. Possible error messages are `Limited by Figma plan`, `Incorrect account type`, or `Invalid scope`. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |

GET styles actions[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-styles-actions-endpoint "Direct link to GET styles actions")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `/v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/style/actions` endpoint provides historical time series data regarding how design system styles were used. The data is returned in descending order, from most recent to least recent. Actions data can be returned organized in different ways using the required `group_by` query parameter.

### Data sparseness[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#data-sparseness-2 "Direct link to Data sparseness")

Only weeks containing data are returned. Teams and styles only appear when there are associated actions.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_analytics:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#http-endpoint-2 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`GET /v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/style/actions`

**Return value**

Actions grouped by style

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "style_key": String,      "week": String,      "detachments": Number,      "insertions": Number,      "style_name": String,      "style_type": String    },    ...  ]}

Actions grouped by team

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "week": String,      "detachments": Number,      "insertions": Number,      "team_name": String,      "workspace_name": String    },    ...  ]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library\_file\_key | Stringrequired<br><br>File key of the library to fetch style usage analytics for. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| group\_by | Stringrequired<br><br>Controls how action data is organized in the response.<br><br>*   `style` returns actions broken down by each style.<br>*   `team` returns actions broken down by the team using the library. |
| start\_date | String<br><br>Filters the response to include data occurring after the start date (inclusive). Accepts an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). |
| end\_date | String<br><br>Filters the response to include data occurring before the end date (inclusive). Accepts an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). |
| cursor | String<br><br>Cursor for pagination. Used to retrieve the next page of data. Obtained from the response of the previous call. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | API is not available. Possible error messages are `Limited by Figma plan`, `Incorrect account type`, or `Invalid scope`. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |

GET styles usages[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-styles-usages-endpoint "Direct link to GET styles usages")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `/v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/style/usages` endpoint provides data on how styles are used across different files and teams. This data can be broken down by style or file.

### Data sparseness[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#data-sparseness-3 "Direct link to Data sparseness")

Rows only include teams or styles with data.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_analytics:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#http-endpoint-3 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`GET /v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/style/usages`

**Return value**

Usages grouped by style

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "files_using": Number,      "teams_using": Number,      "usages": Number,      "style_key": String,      "style_name": String,      "style_type": String    },    ...  ]}

Usages grouped by file

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "usages": Number,      "team_name": String,      "file_name": String,      "workspace_name": String    },    ...  ]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library\_file\_key | Stringrequired<br><br>File key of the library to fetch style usage data for. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| group\_by | Stringrequired<br><br>Controls how usage data is organized in the response.<br><br>*   `style` returns usages broken down by each style.<br>*   `file` returns usages broken down by each file. |
| cursor | String<br><br>Cursor for pagination. Used to retrieve the next page of data. Obtained from the response of the previous call. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | API is not available. Possible error messages are `Limited by Figma plan`, `Incorrect account type`, or `Invalid scope`. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |

GET variables actions[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-variables-actions-endpoint "Direct link to GET variables actions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `/v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/variable/actions` endpoint provides historical time series data regarding how variables within a design system were used. The data is returned in descending order, from most recent to least recent. The data can be grouped by variable or by team.

### Data sparseness[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#data-sparseness-4 "Direct link to Data sparseness")

Only weeks containing data are returned. Teams and variables only appear when there are associated actions.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_analytics:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#http-endpoint-4 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`GET /v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/variable/actions`

**Return value**

Actions grouped by variable

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "variable_key": String,      "week": String,      "detachments": Number,      "insertions": Number,      "variable_name": String,      "variable_type": String,      "collection_key": String,      "collection_name": String    },    ...  ]}

Actions grouped by team

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "week": String,      "detachments": Number,      "insertions": Number,      "team_name": String,      "workspace_name": String    },    ...  ]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library\_file\_key | Stringrequired<br><br>File key of the library to fetch variable usage analytics for. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| group\_by | Stringrequired<br><br>Controls how action data is organized in the response.<br><br>*   `variable` returns actions broken down by each variable.<br>*   `team` returns actions broken down by the team using the library. |
| start\_date | String<br><br>Filters the response to include data occurring after the start date (inclusive). Accepts an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). |
| end\_date | String<br><br>Filters the response to include data occurring before the end date (inclusive). Accepts an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). |
| cursor | String<br><br>Cursor for pagination. Used to retrieve the next page of data. Obtained from the response of the previous call. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | API is not available. Possible error messages are `Limited by Figma plan`, `Incorrect account type`, or `Invalid scope`. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |

GET variables usages[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-variables-usages-endpoint "Direct link to GET variables usages")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `/v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/variable/usages` endpoint provides data on how variables are used across different files and teams. This data can be grouped by variable or file.

### Data sparseness[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#data-sparseness-5 "Direct link to Data sparseness")

Rows only include teams or variables with data.

info

This is a [Tier 3 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`library_analytics:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#http-endpoint-5 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`GET /v1/analytics/libraries/:library_file_key/variable/usages`

**Return value**

Usages grouped by variable

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "files_using": Number,      "teams_using": Number,      "usages": Number,      "variable_key": String,      "variable_name": String,      "variable_type": String,      "collection_key": String,      "collection_name": String    },    ...  ]}

Usages grouped by file

    {  "cursor": String,  "next_page": Boolean,  "rows": [    {      "usages": Number,      "team_name": String,      "file_name": String,      "workspace_name": String    },    ...  ]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library\_file\_key | Stringrequired<br><br>File key of the library to fetch variable usage data for. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| group\_by | Stringrequired<br><br>Controls how usage data is organized in the response.<br><br>*   `variable` returns usages broken down by each variable.<br>*   `file` returns usages broken down by each file. |
| cursor | String<br><br>Cursor for pagination. Used to retrieve the next page of data. Obtained from the response of the previous call. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 403 | API is not available. Possible error messages are `Limited by Figma plan`, `Incorrect account type`, or `Invalid scope`. This could also indicate the developer / OAuth token is invalid or expired. |

*   [GET component actions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/library-analytics-endpoints/#get-component-actions-endpoint)

# <Fragment /> | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/api/component-Fragment/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The Fragment component does not take any props outside of `children` and an optional `key` prop. This component simply renders its `children` and behaves similarly to [Fragments in React](https://reactjs.org/docs/fragments.html)
.

> **This component cannot be used as the root component of a widget.**

To set this up, make sure you have the following lines added to your tsconfig.json

tsconfig.json

    {  "compilerOptions": {    "jsxFactory": "figma.widget.h",    // Add this line    "jsxFragmentFactory": "figma.widget.Fragment",    ...  }}

When writing JSX, you can now use the following syntax:

Fragment with empty tags

    const { widget } = figmaconst { Text } = widgetfunction NameList({ names }: { names: string[] }) {  return (    <>      {names.map(name => <Text key={name}>{name}</Text>)}    </>  )}

Alternatively, if you can also reference the **Fragment** component directly if you need to:

Fragment component

    const { widget } = figmaconst { Text, Fragment } = widgetfunction NameList({ names }: { names: string[] }) {  return (    <Fragment>      {names.map(name => <Text key={name}>{name}</Text>)}    </Fragment>  )}

This is useful if you need to specify the key prop when [working with lists](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/working-with-lists/)
.

# Endpoints | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Dev Resources endpoints allow you to create, read, update, and delete dev resources in Figma files.

GET dev resources[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#get-dev-resources-endpoint "Direct link to GET dev resources")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get dev resources in a file.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`file_dev_resources:read` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`GET /v1/files/:file_key/dev_resources`

**Return value**

    {  "dev_resources": DevResource[]}

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| file\_key | String<br><br>File to get dev resources from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key. |

| Query parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| node\_ids | Stringoptional<br><br>Comma separated list of nodes that you care about in the document. If specified, only dev resources attached to these nodes will be returned. If not specified, all dev resources in the file will be returned. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |

POST dev resources[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#post-dev-resources-endpoint "Direct link to POST dev resources")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Bulk create dev resources across multiple files.

Dev resources that are successfully created will show up in the `links_created` array in the response.

If there are any dev resources that cannot be created, you may still get a 200 response. These resources will show up in the `errors` array. Some reasons a dev resource cannot be created include:

*   Resource points to a `file_key` that cannot be found.
*   The node already has the maximum of 10 dev resources.
*   Another dev resource for the node has the same url.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`file_dev_resources:write` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`POST /v1/dev_resources`

**Return value**

    {  "links_created": DevResource[]  "errors": [    {      "file_key": String | null,      "node_id": String | null,      "error": String,    },    ...  ]}

| Body parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| dev\_resources | [DevResourceCreate](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-types/#devresourcecreate-type)<br>\[\]required<br><br>A list of dev resources that you want to create. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |

PUT dev resources[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#put-dev-resources-endpoint "Direct link to PUT dev resources")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Bulk update dev resources across multiple files.

Ids for dev resources that are successfully updated will show up in the `links_updated` array in the response.

If there are any dev resources that cannot be updated, you may still get a `200` response. These resources will show up in the `errors` array.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`file_dev_resources:write` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint-2 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`PUT /v1/dev_resources`

**Return value**

    {  "links_updated": String[]  "errors": [    {      "id": String,      "error": String,    },    ...  ]}

| Body parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| dev\_resources | [DevResourceUpdate](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-types/#devresourceupdate-type)<br>\[\]required<br><br>A list of dev resources that you want to update. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 400 | Invalid parameter. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |

DELETE dev resources[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#delete-dev-resources-endpoint "Direct link to DELETE dev resources")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Delete a dev resources from a file.

info

This is a [Tier 2 endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/rate-limits/#rate-limits-tier-table)
 and requires the [`file_dev_resources:write` scope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/)
.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint-3 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`DELETE /v1/files/:file_key/dev_resources/:dev_resource_id`

**Return value**

Nothing is returned from this endpoint

| Path parameters | Description |
| --- | --- |
| file\_key | String<br><br>File to delete dev resource from. |
| dev\_resource\_id | String<br><br>Dev resource id to delete. |

| Error codes | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 401 | Issue with authentication. The `message` parameter on the response will describe the error. |
| 404 | The specified dev resource / file was not found |

*   [GET dev resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#get-dev-resources-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint)
        
*   [POST dev resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#post-dev-resources-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint-1)
        
*   [PUT dev resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#put-dev-resources-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint-2)
        
*   [DELETE dev resources](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#delete-dev-resources-endpoint)
    *   [HTTP endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-endpoints/#http-endpoint-3)

# Types | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

| Name | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### DevResource[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-types/#devresource-type "Direct link to DevResource")<br><br>A dev resource in a file. | `id`String<br><br>Unique identifier of the dev resource<br><br>`name`String<br><br>The name of the dev resource.<br><br>`url`String<br><br>The URL of the dev resource.<br><br>`file_key`String<br><br>The file key where the dev resource belongs.<br><br>`node_id`String<br><br>The target node to attach the dev resource to. |

This must be a main file key, not a branch key.<br><br>`node_id`String<br><br>The target node to attach the dev resource to. |
| ### DevResourceUpdate[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/dev-resources-types/#devresourceupdate-type "Direct link to DevResourceUpdate")<br><br>Payload to update a dev resource in a file. | `id`String<br><br>Unique identifier of the dev resource<br><br>`name`String<br><br>The name of the dev resource.<br><br>`url`String<br><br>The URL of the dev resource. |

# Widget State | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/widgets/widget-state/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Every instance of a widget has its own state. You can think of a widget’s state as its data representation. A widget with the same state should always render the same output. **If you want to change how a widget looks in response to a user interaction, you should update its state!**

Here’s an example of a widget that increments a count for every click:

Increment count with synced state

    const { widget } = figmaconst { Text, useSyncedState } = widgetfunction SimpleCounter() {  const [count, setCount] = useSyncedState("count", 0)  return (    <Text      onClick={() => {        setCount(count + 1)      }}    >      {count}    </Text>  )}widget.register(SimpleCounter)

info

We interchangeably refer to a widget's state as synced state because all of a widget's state is currently synced across all clients. In the future, we intend to support things like local state but for now all state is synced state.

We use the `useSyncedState` hook above to declare a data value on the widget that defaults to `0`. Under-the-hood, you can think of this widget's state as a JSON object that looks like this:

caution

If you have multiple `useSyncedState` calls, make sure you give them unique keys!

    // initially{  "count": 0}// click once{  "count": 1}// click again{  "count": 2}

Each time this object gets updated, the associated widget is re-rendered to reflect the changes. Any JSON serializable value is a valid state value.

# Examples | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This section provides examples of how the SCIM API endpoints can be used.

PATCH groups: Add User to Group[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#patch-groups-add-user-to-group "Direct link to PATCH groups: Add User to Group")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Adds a user to an existing group in Figma using the SCIM API. This example demonstrates how to use the [PATCH groups](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-endpoints/#patch-groups-endpoint)
 endpoint to update group membership. You must know the Figma group ID and the user ID of the member you want to add.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#http-endpoint "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`PATCH :baseURL/Groups/:figmaGroupId`

`:figmaGroupId` is a required path parameter, the Figma group ID of the target group.

For example, a group ID returned from `GET :baseURL/Groups`: `"id": "1b5f9234-df40-4664-9767-334fdd7d51f6"`

**Headers**

    Authorization = "Bearer [FIGMA API TOKEN]"Content-Type = application/json

**Example request body**

    {  "schemas": [    "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:PatchOp"  ],  "Operations": [    {      "op": "add",      "path": "members",      "value": [        {          "value": "1360815874257948001",          "display": "newscimuser@example.com"        }      ]    }  ]}

**Example response body**

    {  "schemas": [    "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"  ],  "id": "1b5f9234-df40-4664-9767-334fdd7d51f6",  "members": [    {        "value": "1427545874257949557",        "display": "existingscimuser@example.com"    },    {        "value": "1360815874257948001",        "display": "newscimuser@example.com"    },  ],  "external_id": "123456789",  "displayName": "Existing Group",  "meta": {    "resourceType": "Group",    "created": "2024-10-17T02:22:22Z",    "lastModified": "2024-10-17T04:03:06Z",    "version": "W/\"1729137786\"",    "location": "https://www.figma.com/scim/v2/1401286822445493852/Groups/1b5f9234-df40-4664-9767-334fdd7d51f6"  }}

In this example, after adding the user to the group, a subsequent request to GET the group will show the updated membership list.

PUT users: Soft Delete User[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#put-users-soft-delete-user "Direct link to PUT users: Soft Delete User")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deletes a user in Figma but maintains SCIM provisioning of that user. This is an example of a specific usage of the [PUT users](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-endpoints/#put-users-endpoint)
 endpoint. You can update the `active` attribute to `false`. This will delete the user from Figma, but maintain a record in the SCIM provisioning log of the user. This means the user will be listed if you were to leverage the [GET users](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-endpoints/#get-users-endpoint)
 endpoint in bulk or by email filter.

### HTTP endpoint[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#http-endpoint-1 "Direct link to HTTP endpoint")

`PUT :baseURL/Users/:figmaUserId`

`:figmaUserId` is a required path parameter, the Figma user ID of the operation's target user.

For example, a user ID returned from `GET :baseURL/USERS`: `"id": "977321366678728562"`

**Headers**

    Authorization = "Bearer [FIGMA API TOKEN]"Content-Type = application/json

**Example request body**

    {  "schemas": ["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User", "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"],  "userName": "scimuser@example.com",  "name": {    "givenName": "Scim",    "familyName": "User"  },  "emails": {    "primary": "true",    "value": "scimuser@example.com",    "type": "work"  },  "title": "Developer",  "displayName": "Scim User",  "active": false,  "externalId": "123456789"}

**Example response body**

    {  "schemas": [    "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User",    "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"  ],  "userName": "scimuser@example.com",  "name": {    "givenName": "Scim",    "familyName": "User"  },  "emails": {    "primary": "true",    "value": "scimuser@example.com",    "type": "work"  },  "title": "Developer",  "displayName": "Scim User",  "active": false,  "external_id": "123456789",  "id": "974330387624283297",  "meta": {    "resourceType": "User",    "created": "2021-05-12T15:21:06Z",    "lastModified": "2021-05-13T00:11:04Z",    "version": "W/\"1620864664\"",    "location": "https://www.figma.com/scim/v2/964615200748176016/Users/974330387624283297"  }}

In this example, when running a subsequent request to GET Users, the `totalResults` are 5, and the user is still listed.

*   [PATCH groups: Add User to Group](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#patch-groups-add-user-to-group)
    *   [HTTP endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#http-endpoint)
        
*   [PUT users: Soft Delete User](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#put-users-soft-delete-user)
    *   [HTTP endpoint](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-examples/#http-endpoint-1)

# JSON file schema | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The files provided by the Discovery API are in JSON format and represented as an array of JSON objects. The following table describes the objects that can appear in the array.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ### file\_key[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#file_key "Direct link to file_key") | String<br><br>A unique identifier for the file where the text communication took place. |
| ### text[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#text "Direct link to text") | String<br><br>The text itself that was communicated. |
| ### timestamp[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#timestamp "Direct link to timestamp") | Number<br><br>A unix timestamp with second level precision representing when the text communication occurred. |
| ### action\_type[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#action_type "Direct link to action_type") | String<br><br>The action performed that resulted in text communication, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `insert`<br>*   `update`<br>*   `delete`<br>*   `transient` |
| ### file\_name[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#file_name "Direct link to file_name") | String<br><br>The file name at the time text communication took place. |
| ### file\_workspace\_id[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#file_workspace_id "Direct link to file_workspace_id") | String \| null<br><br>The ID of the workspace the file belonged to at time of text communication. Can be `null` if the file is not in a workspace. |
| ### node\_id[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#node_id "Direct link to node_id") | String \| null<br><br>The identifier for the entity within Figma that the text belongs to. This value is unique within a file, but could be the same value in a different file. |
| ### text\_type[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#text_type "Direct link to text_type") | String<br><br>What object type within Figma that text belongs to, as a string enum. See [text types](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#text-types)<br> for more details.<br><br>**Note:** It's possible that new values will be added for this enum. When building with this API, keep in mind that the list of valid values might expand.<br><br>*   `in_file`<br>*   `file_comments`<br>*   `file_comment_reactions`<br>*   `developer_related_links`<br>*   `dev_mode_annotation`<br>*   `component_documentation_link`<br>*   `component_documentation_description`<br>*   `cursor_chat` |
| ### resource\_id[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#resource_id "Direct link to resource_id") | String \| null<br><br>A unique identifier, valid for the following `text_type` values:<br><br>*   `file_comments`<br>*   `file_comment_reactions`<br>*   `developer_related_links` |
| ### parent\_comment\_id[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#parent_comment_id "Direct link to parent_comment_id") | String \| null<br><br>A unique identifier for a resource’s parent comment, valid for the following `text_type` values:<br><br>*   `file_comments`<br>*   `file_comment_reactions` |
| ### user\_id[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#user_id "Direct link to user_id") | String<br><br>A unique identifier for the user who performed the text communication. |
| ### user\_email[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#user_email "Direct link to user_email") | String<br><br>The user’s email at the time text communication took place. When a user is in a FigJam open session but not logged in, will be marked as `anonymous`. |
| ### user\_billing\_group\_id[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#user_billing_group_id "Direct link to user_billing_group_id") | String \| null<br><br>The ID of the billing group the user belonged to at time of text communication. Can be `null` if the user is not in a billing group. |

*   [file\_key](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#file_key)
    
*   [text](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#text)
    
*   [timestamp](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#timestamp)
    
*   [action\_type](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#action_type)
    
*   [file\_name](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#file_name)
    
*   [file\_workspace\_id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#file_workspace_id)
    
*   [node\_id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#node_id)
    
*   [text\_type](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#text_type)
    
*   [resource\_id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#resource_id)
    
*   [parent\_comment\_id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#parent_comment_id)
    
*   [user\_id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#user_id)
    
*   [user\_email](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#user_email)
    
*   [user\_billing\_group\_id](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-schema/#user_billing_group_id)

# Types | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

**Variables** in Figma have preset types (string, boolean, float, etc…), belong to a **variable collection**, and hold a different value for each **mode** in the collection. Certain node properties can **bind** to variables, enabling these properties to change dynamically when variables are updated or when the node is associated with a different mode.

The below types are returned by the [GET local variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-endpoints/#get-local-variables-endpoint)
 and [GET published variables](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-endpoints/#get-published-variables-endpoint)
 endpoints, with the published variables endpoint returning a subset of these properties. Please refer to the return values for each endpoint for more details.

| Name | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| ### VariableCollection[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablecollection-type "Direct link to VariableCollection")<br><br>A grouping of related Variable objects each with the same modes. | `id`String<br><br>The unique identifier of this variable collection.<br><br>`name`String<br><br>The name of this variable collection.<br><br>`key`String<br><br>The key of the variable collection.<br><br>`modes`Map<String, String><br><br>A map of `modeId` to mode `name` for this variable collection.<br><br>`defaultModeId`String<br><br>The id of the default `mode`.<br><br>`remote`Boolean<br><br>Whether the variable collection is remote.<br><br>`hiddenFromPublishing`Boolean<br><br>Whether this variable collection is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.<br><br>`variableIds`String\[\]<br><br>The ids of the variables in the collection. Note that the order of these variables is roughly the same as what is shown in Figma Design, however it does not account for groups. As a result, the order of these variables may not exactly reflect the exact ordering and grouping shown in the authoring UI. |
| ### Variable[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variable-type "Direct link to Variable")<br><br>A Variable is a single design token that defines values for each of the modes in its VariableCollection. These values can be applied to various kinds of design properties. | `id`String<br><br>The unique identifier of this variable.<br><br>`name`String<br><br>The name of this variable.<br><br>`key`String<br><br>The key of the variable.<br><br>`variableCollectionId`String<br><br>The id of the variable collection that contains this variable.<br><br>`resolvedType`String<br><br>The resolved type of the variable, as a string enum.<br><br>*   `BOOLEAN`<br>*   `FLOAT`<br>*   `STRING`<br>*   `COLOR`<br><br>`valuesByMode`Map<String, Boolean \| Number \| String \| Color \| [VariableAlias](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablealias-type)<br> ><br><br>The values for each mode of this variable.<br><br>`remote`Boolean<br><br>Whether the variable is remote.<br><br>`description`String<br><br>Description of this variable.<br><br>`hiddenFromPublishing`Boolean<br><br>Whether this variable is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.<br><br>If the parent `VariableCollection` is marked as `hiddenFromPublishing`, then this variable will also be hidden from publishing via the UI. `hiddenFromPublishing` is independently toggled for a variable and collection. However, both must be true for a given variable to be publishable.<br><br>`scopes`[VariableScope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablescope-type)<br>\[\]<br><br>An array of scopes in the UI where this variable is shown. Setting this property will show/hide this variable in the variable picker UI for different fields.<br><br>Setting scopes for a variable does not prevent that variable from being bound in other scopes (for example, via the Plugin API). This only limits the variables that are shown in pickers within the Figma UI.<br><br>`codeSyntax`[VariableCodeSyntax](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablecodesyntax-type)<br><br>Code syntax definitions for this variable. Code syntax allows you to represent variables in code using platform-specific names, and will appear in [Dev Mode](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/15023124644247)<br>'s code snippets when inspecting elements using the variable.<br><br>`deletedButReferenced`Boolean<br><br>Indicates that the variable was deleted in the editor, but the document may still contain references to the variable. References to the variable may exist through bound values or variable aliases. |
| ### VariableAlias[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablealias-type "Direct link to VariableAlias")<br><br>An object that serves as an alias for a variable. All properties are required. | `type`String<br><br>`VARIABLE_ALIAS`<br><br>`id`String<br><br>The id of the variable that this alias points to. In the POST variables endpoint, you can use the temporary id of a variable. |
| ### VariableScope[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablescope-type "Direct link to VariableScope")<br><br>Scopes allow a variable to be shown/hidden in the variable picker UI for various fields. This declutters the Figma UI if you have a large number of variables. Currently only supported on `FLOAT` and `COLOR` variables.<br><br>`ALL_SCOPES` is a special scope that means that the variable will be shown in the picker UI for all current and any future fields. If `ALL_SCOPES` is set, no additional scopes can be set.<br><br>Likewise, `ALL_FILLS` is a special scope that means that the variable will be shown in the picker UI for all current and any future color fill fields. If `ALL_FILLS` is set, no additional fill scopes can be set. | Valid scopes for `FLOAT` variables:<br><br>*   `ALL_SCOPES`<br>*   `CORNER_RADIUS`<br>*   `TEXT_CONTENT`<br>*   `WIDTH_HEIGHT`<br>*   `GAP`<br>*   `STROKE_FLOAT`<br>*   `OPACITY`<br>*   `EFFECT_FLOAT`<br>*   `FONT_WEIGHT`<br>*   `FONT_SIZE`<br>*   `LINE_HEIGHT`<br>*   `LETTER_SPACING`<br>*   `PARAGRAPH_SPACING`<br>*   `PARAGRAPH_INDENT`<br><br>Valid scopes for `STRING` variables:<br><br>*   `ALL_SCOPES`<br>*   `TEXT_CONTENT`<br>*   `FONT_FAMILY`<br>*   `FONT_STYLE`<br>*   `FONT_VARIATIONS`<br><br>Valid scopes for `COLOR` variables:<br><br>*   `ALL_SCOPES`<br>*   `ALL_FILLS`<br>*   `FRAME_FILL`<br>*   `SHAPE_FILL`<br>*   `TEXT_FILL`<br>*   `STROKE_COLOR`<br>*   `EFFECT_COLOR` |
| ### VariableCodeSyntax[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablecodesyntax-type "Direct link to VariableCodeSyntax")<br><br>An object containing platform-specific code syntax definitions for a variable. All platforms are optional. | Map<String, String><br><br>A map of platform to code syntax definitions for this variable. Platform may be one of:<br><br>*   `WEB`<br>*   `ANDROID`<br>*   `iOS` |

*   [VariableCollection](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablecollection-type)
    
*   [Variable](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variable-type)
    
*   [VariableAlias](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablealias-type)
    
*   [VariableScope](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablescope-type)
    
*   [VariableCodeSyntax](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/variables-types/#variablecodesyntax-type)

# Supported Schemas | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The SCIM API supports the following schemas.

`["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"]`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#core-user-schema "Direct link to core-user-schema")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter name | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| roles | "roles": [  {    "type": "seatType",    "value": "Full"  }] | Array. Enterprise plan only. Each item in the array is an object with two properties: `"type": "seatType"` and `"value":`, which must be one of `Full`, `Dev`, `Collab`, or `View`. |
| givenName | `John` |     |
| familyName | `Doe` |     |
| displayName | `John Doe` | This value is limited to 100 characters or less. |
| title | `Design Manager` |     |
| userName | `johndoe@example.com` | String. The user's email. Changing this attribute will change the user account's email in Figma. This value is limited to 100 characters or less. |
| active | `true` | Boolean. Use this parameter to deactivate or reactivate users in Figma. Deactivated users are removed from the organization. |
| externalId | `aaaabbbbbbccccc` | String. The IdP's external ID for the user. Figma stores but does not use this parameter. This value is limited to 255 characters or less. |

`["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"]`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#enterprise-user-schema "Direct link to enterprise-user-schema")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter name | Example |
| --- | --- |
| employeeNumber | `70198422` |
| costCenter | `093923` |
| organization | `Product` |
| division | `Mobile` |
| department | `Designers` |
| managerValue | `examplemanager@example.com` |
| managerDisplayName | `John Doe` |

`["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"]`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#core-group-schema "Direct link to core-group-schema")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter name | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| displayName | `example_group_name` | String. Sets the name of the SCIM group. This value is limited to 255 characters or less. |
| members | "members": [    {        "value": "1427545874257949557",        "display": "scimuser@example.com"    },    {        "value": "1360815874257948001",        "display": "anotherscimuser@example.com"    },] | Array. A list of members for the SCIM group. |
| externalId | `123456789` | String. An ID that uniquely identifies the group in the IdP. This value is limited to 255 characters or less. |

Custom Parameters[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#custom-parameters "Direct link to Custom Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The custom parameter provided by this schema is used to designate organization administrators.

info

**Note:** Previously, product seats were managed using custom parameters. Seats are now managed using the `roles` parameter, a part of the `Core` user schema.

### `["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:figma:enterprise:2.0:User"]`[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#figma-enterprise-user-schema "Direct link to figma-enterprise-user-schema")

| Parameter name | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| figmaAdmin | `true` | Boolean. Enterprise plan only. If `true` sets the user as a Figma organization administrator. |

*   [`["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"]`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#core-user-schema)
    
*   [`["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"]`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#enterprise-user-schema)
    
*   [`["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"]`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#core-group-schema)
    
*   [Custom Parameters](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#custom-parameters)
    *   [`["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:figma:enterprise:2.0:User"]`](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scim-supported-schemas/#figma-enterprise-user-schema)

# Text types | Developer Docs

[Skip to main content](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The following table lists the different `text_type` values that can be returned by the Discovery API and provides links to the corresponding documentation in the help center.

| text\_type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ### file\_comments[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#file_comments "Direct link to file_comments") | [Add comments to files](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/360041068574-Add-comments-to-files#add%22) |
| ### file\_comment\_reactions[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#file_comment_reactions "Direct link to file_comment_reactions") | [Add emoji to comments](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/360041068574-Add-comments-to-files#emoji) |
| ### developer\_related\_links[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#developer_related_links "Direct link to developer_related_links") | [Link Dev resources to layers in Dev Mode](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/15023231995927-Link-Dev-resources-to-layers-in-Dev-Mode#h_01HNAZC814NF6YH8AP7WKRKPNX) |
| ### in\_file[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#in_file "Direct link to in_file") | *   [Text layers](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/360039956434-Guide-to-text-in-Figma-Design#h_01HXCJTXVJ6ES4C32PZMYCVK3Z)<br>    <br>*   [Shapes with text](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/1500004414382-Visualize-information-using-shapes-with-text)<br>    <br>*   [Sticky notes](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/1500004414322-Sticky-notes-in-FigJam)<br>    <br>*   [Tables](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/12583849250199-Tables-in-FigJam) |
| ### dev\_mode\_annotation[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#dev_mode_annotation "Direct link to dev_mode_annotation") | [Add measurements and annotate designs in Dev Mode](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/20774752502935-Add-measurements-and-annotate-designs-in-Dev-Mode#h_01HNDX36EFDJC56QE79XBQ3J7T) |
| ### component\_documentation\_link[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#component_documentation_link "Direct link to component_documentation_link") | [Add descriptions to styles, components, and variables](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/7938814091287-Add-descriptions-to-styles-components-and-variables#add-component-descriptions) |
| ### component\_documentation\_description[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#component_documentation_description "Direct link to component_documentation_description") | [Add descriptions to styles, components, and variables](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/7938814091287-Add-descriptions-to-styles-components-and-variables#add-component-descriptions) |
| ### cursor\_chat[​](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#cursor_chat "Direct link to cursor_chat") | [Send messages with cursor chat](https://help.figma.com/hc/articles/1500004414842-Send-messages-with-cursor-chat) |

*   [file\_comments](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#file_comments)
    
*   [file\_comment\_reactions](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#file_comment_reactions)
    
*   [developer\_related\_links](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#developer_related_links)
    
*   [in\_file](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#in_file)
    
*   [dev\_mode\_annotation](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#dev_mode_annotation)
    
*   [component\_documentation\_link](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/discovery-text-types/#component_documentation_link)
